### ealachan — 2012-09-03T15:55:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-49170>

**Leptin Reset, Day 179**

b: two eggs, 4ish oz roast beef, coffee w/ co, hwc, and cocoa

l: not planning on it

d: cook-out @ my mom's - burgers, steak, grilled veg

\- dinner last night was shrimp tikka masala and it was very tasty. lots of leftovers for hubs to have for lunch. Had a small glass of wine and a little chocolate for dessert.

\- was in bed by 8 last night. Read for a while - the orange light in the bedroom isn't so bad, I guess. It makes everything look exactly the same as wearing orange glasses (literally cannot tell a difference between glasses on or off under the orange bulb) so that's a good sign, I suppose. We got in a little oxytocin therapy and I was asleep by 9-ish. Slept well at first but then kept waking up. It was chilly and we'd taken the duvet off the bed due to it being warmer earlier in the week...I think that might be what did it. Got up at 4 to take hubs to work, felt really tired and crappy for the first time in a long time. Went back to bed when I got home, got up around 7:15. Still socked in with clouds / fog here this morning, so that's a bummer.

\- have had a sore throat since mid-afternoon yesterday. It's only on the left side, and my throat isn't actually swollen up at all (I have huge tonsils and usually if something irritates my throat, they swell up), it just feels scratchy and annoying on the one side, and my nose on that side feels a bit runny / post-nasal-drippy too. The only reason for this that I can come up with is that I deep-cleaned the bathroom yesterday, which included sucking the accumulated dust out of the vents on the exhaust fan. My face was right up next to it the whole time, and I'm sure I probably breathed some of that \*\*\*\* in - it was likely moldy / mildewy too, just by virtue of being the shower exhaust fan. So maybe I'm having a bit of a reaction to that? Or maybe aliens have taken over my throat. Could go either way, really.

### ealachan — 2012-09-04T18:30:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-49365>

**Leptin Reset, Day 180**

_6 months, roughly! Still trying to decide what I have to show for it...lol_

b: steak (didn't weigh, had to be at least 6oz), two eggs, coffee w/ CO and HWC

L: -

d: not sure yet...likely the rest of the steak, since I have to use it up.

\- dinner yesterday was cook-out stuff - I had a pork rib (no sauce), a chicken thigh (no skin, no sauce), a couple small pieces of kielbasa, some roasted potatoes, roasted pepper salad with garlic, grilled zucchini and summer squash, a few deviled eggs (mom made them, so they likely had sketchy stuff in them) and a couple pieces of celery stuffed with olives and cream cheese. I also drank two beers. Serving of dark chocolate for dessert.

\- slept like CRAP last night. Some kind of allergy thing kicked in on Sunday afternoon - sore throat like I mentioned before - but then yesterday I started feeling really tired and out of it, and had wicked body aches along with sinus pain. Also weird hot-and-then-cold sensations. Felt for all the world like a fever (body aches tend to be the tell-tale sign of a fever for me), but my temp never got above 99.8, so I don't know. Is there such a thing as a mini-fever? LOL I finally had to get up and take some Tylenol at around 10:30pm because I was so achey that I couldn't sleep and was just laying there in misery. Today I don't have body aches and temp was at around 98.4 this morning, but my sinuses feel like lead and are really achey. So that's fun. Hopefully whatever bloomed that set this off will die off quickly!

### ealachan — 2012-09-05T15:53:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-49577>

**Leptin Reset, Day 181**

b: two chicken sausages, two eggs, four cherry tomatoes, coffee w/ hwc, co and butter and a tablespoon of unsweetened cocoa powder

l: -

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was ribeyes, sweet potato, and organic mushrooms and leeks sauteed in butter and the steak run-off. Tasty, but the meal sent DH's blood sugar up like a rocket. Whoopsies. Guess we won't have a huge slab of meat AND sweet potato AND a bunch of onions all at the same time again. Heh. I also had a couple glasses of wine.

\- went to bed around 9:30, passed out within about 10 minutes, slept well for a while but then got tossy-turny in the middle of the night. Sinuses still really full, so it was making it hard to breathe, which of course makes it hard to sleep. Had to get up at 6am, which felt like a bit of a struggle, but once I was up I was ok. Very dark and gloomy morning again today...makes it hard to get up / wake up.

\- allergies (or whatever this is) still kicking my ass, though my face doesn't hurt as badly as it did yesterday (knock on wood). Mostly just faucet-nose and post-nasal drip now. Ugh.

\- started CoQ10 this morning. interested to see if it makes any difference with the PQQ I've been taking. I don't think I'm really taking a big enough dose of the PQQ to do a whole lot (it's 20mg, and that \*\*\*\* is EXPENSIVE!), but we'll see.

\- noticed loose skin in weird places this morning - wrinkles around my elbows that never used to be there, sides of my stomach are getting wrinkled too. Also, I really wish I'd have taken "before" pictures of my thighs, because the difference is pretty astounding. My thighs aren't a whole lot SMALLER than 6 months ago, but the difference in cellulite is amazing. I used to have wicked cottage-cheese thighs...like REALLY deep dimples, all over. Now, I still have some dimples on the tops and sides, but way less, and nowhere near as deep...and the BACKS of my thighs seriously look like someone else's legs. It's crazy. I have hardly any cellulite there anymore! And it's not like I'm sitting in a cold bath every night, either. I haven't sat in a cold bath since like May. I do take cold showers, and I sleep with ice packs on my neck and the a/c cranked up, but that's really it in terms of CT for me lately. Crazy.

### ealachan — 2012-09-06T14:56:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-49719>

**Leptin Reset, Day 182**

b: three eggs, some sausage (didn't weigh, at least two large links' worth), coffee w/ co, hwc and butter

L: we laugh in the face of lunch, haaaaaa hahaha!

d: not sure yet - company coming over tonight.

\- dinner last night was my new favorite standby: roasted cod with tomato olive tappenade. My tappenade was made with cherry tomatoes from the farmer's market that were seriously amazing...so yummy. I had quick-braised kale with it, which I have decided I like even more than the spinach I usually have with it. Kale is more work, but it tastes better and is probably healthier anyway.

\- got some butter and some mozzarella cheese both made from milk from a herd of local grass-fed Devon cows last night. The butter is salted, which I didn't realize until it was too late. Still decent, though. The mozzarella is very nice...would happily nom way too much of that.

\- allergies still kicking my ass. \*sigh\* Went to bed around 9:30 last night, oxytocin'ed up, zonked out. Slept well for a few hours, then woke up tossy-turny again. Annoying. I'm hoping that this is just something to do with being congested, and not my new sleep pattern.

\- had to be up at 5:15 this morning. Tomorrow is a 4am day, and I think I'll try to go back to bed for a bit after I drop hubs off at work, as 4:30 is just way too early for me to go in.

\- hubs stayed up to watch football last night...with his blue-blockers on and the obnoxious orange lightbulb in the lamp. This morning he gets up and goes "wow, I must have been really tired last night...I couldn't even stay up for the whole football game. By 10pm my eyes just wouldn't stay open!" Hee hee hee. In the past, he'd have had the lamp, TV and laptop all going and would have been up until gone midnight, at least. Score one for blocking blue light.

### ealachan — 2012-09-07T02:20:26+0300

People. I am HOT. Not even hot to trot (although...\*eyes husband from across the room\*)...I mean I'm like, overheating. The thing is, it's not that hot out. I don't have a fever, I'm not flushed...I'm just FECKING HOT. Is this some kind of crazy detox my body is doing or something? I'm too young to be having peri-menopausal hot flashes, aren't I?

This has been going on for the last few days. I just can't seem to get comfortable unless I'm sitting in front of an a/c unit. It's super annoying.

### ealachan — 2012-09-07T17:55:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-50019>

**Leptin Reset, Day 183**

b (7am): two chicken sausages, two eggs, coffee with hwc / butter / co, 1tbsp cocoa

L: nyet

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was zucchini stuffed with a mixture of ground beef, leeks, cherry tomatoes, mushrooms, some herbs, and topped with a bit of local raw-milk mozzarella. Side salad of greens dressed with balsamic and olive oil. YUM. Had one beer for dessert.

\- went to bed around 9pm, zonked right out, woke up some time in the night for a pee, then zonked out again. Had to get up at 4am, which kinda sucked, but I put my blue-blockers on straight away and went right back to bed around 4:30. Slept like a log until 7am, then got up and took the doggie out for what little light exposure we could get through the thick fog (ahh, fall in Vermont...so foggy!).

\- allergies / cold / whatever this is feels better this morning - sinuses don't hurt so much and ears aren't as crackly, and I don't feel dead on my feet today like I have the last two days, so that's good. Wondering if the overheating thing I've had for the past few days has been my body fighting off whatever I've had going on, since said overheating started like 24 hours after the cold-like symptoms set in. Hmm.

### ealachan — 2012-09-10T17:12:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-50719>

**Leptin Reset, Day 184**

_In which we skip over the weekend because not much positive happened...heh..._

B: can o' salmon, three slices of bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ hwc, co, butter and 1 tbsp cocoa

L: nyerhe

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was t-bones (they were on sale), sauteed kale, and portobello mushrooms that I stuffed with a crab meat / cream cheese mixture. The mushrooms were good, but recipe needs to be re-worked to not use cream cheese, because...hello, cream cheese. Also, I over-garlic'ed the filling so you couldn't really taste much crab. AND...I had no idea crab was fat-free. That means no DHA, right? Which means I don't need to bother buying any more of it...lol

\- took a cold shower just as it was getting dark last night, then put my blue-blockers on and stayed up to watch a little football. I went to bed around 9:30, oxytocin therapy, zonked out with ice packs on neck by 10pm. Slept like a frickin' ROCK. I woke up once in the night because I was coughing ( tail end of cold / allergies clearing out), but other than that it was zonk-out city. Woke up this morning when hubs uncovered the window at 7am. A couple minutes of sun in the eyes and I perked right up. It's nice to not feel like a complete zombie for an hour after getting up in the morning nowadays...

\- I did wake up with an annoying split in my bottom lip, which I think is from mouth-breathing due to congestion. It's right on the edge so it really hurts when I smile. Guess I'll just have to pretend to be grumpy today...haha!

\- weekend was fun - we went away for a nerdy card tournament (Which my husband won...aww yeah!). We got to see lots of folks we don't see too often, and it's nice to get out and be social with them. The downside was that diet and circadian rhythms went right in the toilet for the day. Ah, well. You only live once. Might as well have a good time when you can. It's not like we eat pizza and drink beer and stay up til midnight regularly any more.

\- cold shower in the evening last night was an attempt to start working my way up to cold baths again. Tap water was around 65 last night. I figure if I can start working my way into it again now, then I'll be well on my way by the time the tap water gets down to like 45-50 degrees this winter.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-11T02:56:40+0300

### ealachan — 2012-09-11T17:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-50950>

**Leptin Reset, Day 185**

b: two strips of bacon, tin of water-packed sardines, two eggs, coffee w/ hwc, butter, co and 1tbsp cocoa

l: nyet

d: leftover chili

\- dinner last night was Quick and Dirty Chili, so named because it doesn't cook all day like my normal chili does. I have a couple tricks I use to cram as much flavor as I can into the mix in a short amount of time - first being that I use a little chopped bacon to saute the chopped veggies in...that gives a nice smokey base to the chili right off the bat. The other trick is to add all my spices to the cooked meat / veg and then let it cook without adding any liquid for a few minutes. This helps take the rawness off the spices, makes them bloom, kind of. It's the same concept as when you toast spices before adding them to a curry. Anyway - chili consisted of ground beef and ground pork, a little bacon, onion, garlic, green pepper, mushroom, carrot, canned tomatoes, spices...and then I topped the servings with chopped avocado and a little cheddar cheese. It was good stuff, and we have enough leftovers for dinner tonight as well. Chili is one thing that hubs never minds eating two nights in a row!

\- drank too much wine last night and was tossy-turny in the middle of the night. Also had a weird headache when I woke up, which I never get from wine normally, but this wine was not my normal brand nor my preferred variety...so I'm going to blame that. Good thing it's gone now.

\- gorgeous sunrise this morning, but I forgot to take a picture. Nice to finally see the sun first thing, after so many foggy mornings in a row! Looking at the sun this morning was like flipping a switch in my head again - I went from bleary-eyed and groggy to wide awake and ready to go in literally the space of about 60 seconds. SO COOL.

\- totally off blood pressure med for a week now, and things are holding steady in the 115/75 average range...WOO HOO!

\- stomach fat feels different lately - looser, more fluid. I've definitely been shrinking, but in odd places where people don't usually measure, if that makes sense...lol I can see my knees again - like, the actual kneecap and stuff. My knees have just been vauge round things for a really long time. Calf definition is getting a lot better, which, considering I was on a diuretic before and I used to get swollen lower legs at the drop of a hat, I take this as a really good sign! Upper arms aren't getting smaller yet, but fat distribution is definitely changing. Sides of stomach have wrinkles of loose skin now when I "suck it in". Also, I now have a slightly indented line from sternum to belly button, just like what people with washboard stomachs have, except of course mine is on fat, not muscle. It's fun and new none the less, though...

\- I just feel GOOD. Life's not perfect, work is not ideal, my husband still leaves wet towels on the bathroom floor, but none of that bothers me nearly as much as it did even a month ago. Maybe it's just a product of where I am in my cycle? Maybe my husband is secretly spiking my coffee with happy drugs. Don't know...but as long as it doesn't go away, I'm totally ok with it.

### ealachan — 2012-09-12T16:57:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51193>

**Leptin Reset, Day 186**

b: can o' salmon, three strips o' bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ hwc, butter and co, last of the cocoa...\*pout\*

l: -

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was left-over chili with some cheddar cheese, and a glass of wine. Was in bed by 8:15 last night, dropped off to sleep after about 15 min or so. Woke up right before hubs' alarm went off at 3:45, then dozed unti he woke me up at 4. Took him to work, came back home and went back to bed. Oxytocin'ed up, then slept like a rock until 7.

\- wearing a skirt to work today that just barely fit but wasn't comfortable when I bought it a month or so ago. It now fits perfectly and is totally comfortable. I went for literally YEARS without wearing skirts in public because I hated my legs so much. A combination of maturing (ie: getting to that "who \*\*\*\*ing cares if anyone doesn't like my legs?!" stage of a 30-something) and my legs starting to look better has broken me out of that habit. Yay! Now I just have to remember to conduct myself like a lady when I have a skirt on. THAT is the really hard part for me...LOL Always been kind of a tomboy.

\- A friend of mine who had either gastric bypass or a lap band (can't remember which) about a year ago is now on Facebook complaining that even though she's eating 1400 calories a day and doing 30 min of cardio a day, she's gaining weight. I feel bad for her, but my few suggestions that she try something totally different (ie: epi-paleo) have been met with responses that it's a little too far out for her. It makes me sad. I feel so much better personally, and I really wish people were open-minded enough to give this stuff a try themselves. Oh well...can't save 'em all, I guess.

### ealachan — 2012-09-13T16:03:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51417>

**Leptin Reset, Day 187**

B: can of sardines, two eggs, strip of bacon, coffee with all the fat (which I just realized is probably cancelling out the fish I'm eating...whoops)

L: probably something

d: not sure

\- dinner last night was steak, sauteed kale with mushrooms and onions, and some steamed asparagus. Also some chocolate. No booze, woo!

\- was in bed by 10ish, oxytocin therapy, slept like a rock until 7:30 this morning.

\- Day off today - waiting for the plumber to come fix the kitchen sink, then we're going to the fair! woo, fair! Unreasonably excited for the fair.  We...may eat bad things there. I can't lie. It will be fun, though. YAY FAIR!!

### ealachan — 2012-09-14T17:07:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51640>

**Leptin Reset, Day 188**

b: two chicken sausages, two eggs, coffee w/ all the fats, cup of green tea on the way to work

L: -

d: dunno yet

\- ate some horrible things at the fair yesterday, but managed to not get any cotton candy, and talked myself right out of fried dough, which I was very proud of. It was still a pretty bad food day, though. And fun. So much fun.  Walked a couple miles, got lots of sun and fresh air, got to pet lots of cows and goats and sheep, and just generally had a really good day out with my husband. We took the long way back home and it was a lovely scenic drive, and a very nice ending to the day.

\- got new shoes yesterday! My sneakers died a stinky and painful death when we were on vacation a month or so ago, so Hubs took me to get some new ones yesterday. They're Keens and they're super comfy, even for my big paddle-feet.

\- lots to do at work and not really motivated to do it. I just want to go out and lay in the sun.

### ealachan — 2012-09-15T17:22:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51812>

**Leptin Reset, Day 189**

b: two chicken sausages, two eggs, coffee w/ all the fats

l: -

d: not sure yet. thinking something fishy.

\- dinner last night was not optimal, but we were pressed for time. I got a rotisserie chicken from the deli and we each had a leg plus some salad. It supposedly didn't have any MSG in it, but I got a pretty banging headache later on in the evening, so I'm suspicious. I'll let hubs eat the rest of the chicken for lunch today and be done with that. We used to eat that crap all the time...hard to believe nowadays.

\- got to bed around 9:30ish, fell asleep quickly, woke up around 3:30 when hubs' alarm went off, laid there half-awake for 20 minutes until he finally got up. Managed to get my blue-blockers on before he started turning lights on...haha! Took him to work, got back home at 4:30 and went back to bed, oxytocin'ed up, zonked out and didn't wake up until 8:30!! Went right out and got some sun first thing, which is good because its gone behind a cloud now...boo.

\- weather is supposed to be super nice tomorrow. I want to get up in the morning and do something fun outside, but I'm at a loss as to what. We live in an apartment, so we don't really have any outside space to ourselves that I could work in, and I don't really want to go for a hike by myself (hubs has to work in the morning again tomorrow). I don't know. Maybe I'll just take the dog over to the lake and walk around over there for a while. He's not very good walking on the road, so that idea doesn't appeal to me hugely, but we'll see.

\- not sure if I'm imagining it, but it seems a weird thing to imagine: my eyebrows are growing in thicker lately? They've always been pretty sparse, but they do seem more substantial lately. interesting.

### ealachan — 2012-09-15T21:37:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51855>

I'm on a mission to get oysters for tonight. They're all I've been able to think about for a week now. Must have.

### ealachan — 2012-09-16T17:00:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51923>

**Leptin Reset, Day 190**

b: three eggs, sauteed kale, squirt of Sriracha, fat coffee

l: -

d: fish goddamnit!

\- last night's dinner ended up not being fish at all. I made meatballs and tomato sauce and had it over roasted spaghetti squash. Chocolate and macadamia nuts for dessert. NO BOOZE!

\- wasn't feeling the BAB this morning for some reason, so I listened to my body and had sauteed kale and eggs instead. It wasn't that I wasn't hungry, I just couldn't muster my normal enthusiasm for meaty breakfast things. Kale and eggs with fat coffee hit the spot. Will be interested to see how long it lasts me before I get hungry again.

\- went to bed around 8 last night, oxytocin'ed up, was zonked out by 9ish. Slept fine for a while, then got tossy-turny later on in the night. Got up at 4 to take hubs to work, went back to bed when I got home, oxytocin'ed up again (I am a machine...lol), zonked out again, woke up at 8. Sunlight right away, perked right up. Went out to walk the dog in shorts and a tank top, came back in and saw the weather-bug on the computer claiming that it was 45 degrees out. WTF?! I wasn't the least bit chilly while I was outside. The sun was nice and warm on my skin, there was a cool breeze, but I was by no means cold. If this is cold adaptation, I'll take it! Been sitting here with all the windows open, still in my shorts and tank top, with a breeze blowing in and goosebumps on my legs, but I don't actually feel cold at all. craziness.

\- currently having a conundrum re: breakfast - right now, I have a big cup of coffee w/ fat added, along with some eggs and then either meat or fish. My problem is that the sat fat in the coffee and the eggs is likely cancelling out any good the DHA in the fish would be doing me. But...the fat is what keeps me full all day long. Do I ditch the fat coffee and eggs and just eat fish for breakfast, and start eating lunch again? Or do I keep on with what's worked really well fullness-wise for me for months now, and just try harder to get my fish in at other times of the day? This would certainly be easier if I DID eat lunch, but I normally don't WANT lunch...so I don't really know what to do.

### chocolate — 2012-09-16T17:21:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51925>

Can't you do olive oil with fish some way? I'm so limited with what I can eat. I use lettuce and olive oil to get the oil down. And I went back to the odd fish oil with cooked fish. Life Extension just posted a dissection of the fish oil study.

### ealachan — 2012-09-16T17:53:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51930>

> **chocolate;52540 wrote:** Can't you do olive oil with fish some way? I'm so limited with what I can eat. I use lettuce and olive oil to get the oil down. And I went back to the odd fish oil with cooked fish. Life Extension just posted a dissection of the fish oil study.

I love olive oil...I can drink it straight. I don't want to put it in my coffee, though...

My conundrum is more an issue of whether I should stick with the sat-fat-heavy breakfast / protein-heavy dinner template that's been working pretty well for me hunger-wise, or if I should try switching to less fat in the mornings so that I want lunch, at which point I could eat fish without having to worry about combining it with too much sat-fat.

I know if I ask Jack, he'll tell me I need tests to decide...which is fine, I know I do, but I can't afford them quite yet.

### ealachan — 2012-09-17T01:37:16+0300

### ealachan — 2012-09-17T16:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-52102>

**Leptin Reset, Day 191**

b: two pork sausages, two eggs, coffee w/ co and butter (trying to lay off the HWC for a while)

l: can of olive-oil packed tonno (tuna)

d: piiiiiiiiiig. There's been a pork butt in the crock pot since bedtime last night. OMG, THE SMELL. It was hard to not get into it for breakfast this morning.

\- dinner last night is documented above. I noticed something last night - I eat WAY slower now than I used to, and way, WAY slower than my husband does. It's almost a little disturbing. His plate is clean when I'm like halfway done. I wonder if he even tastes half of what he eats sometimes. He says it's because he's always really hungry, and because my cooking is really good. I'm going to try not to notice it so much, because it's one of those things that truly doesn't matter one bit, and yet I fixate...lol

\- went to bed around 8:45 last night, read for a while. No oxytocin therapy, as Hubs was zonked out from having been up at 4am. Had a hard time falling asleep, but finally dropped off maybe half an hour after I shut the light off. Must have slept in a weird position all night because I woke up with a really stiff neck. Boo. Also, I love my dog to bits, but he needs to learn to sleep at the end of the bed rather than in between Hubs and I. He only weighs like 13lbs, but you'd think he was a full-sized cinder block when he's weighing the blankets down on the bed!

\- going to try eating lunch this week even though I'm likely not going to be hungry. I figure if I can force some fish down every day at lunch, it's better for me in the long run than skipping lunch entirely and then running the risk of not getting enough fish in.

\- my nails are growing super fast and my hair seems to be as well, but I still have all these annoying short broken hairs around my temples and widows' peak that look really bad. I wish they'd hurry up and grow in so that I don't look like quite so much of a nutter. I'm not sure how I managed to break the hair off like that because I don't style it - the most I ever do is pull it back in a ponytail. I HAVE been washing it every day (with regular shampoo) all summer long...maybe it's dried out from that? It doesn't look / feel dry at all, but maybe the shampoo has made it fragile or something. I need to start doing every-other-day washes to prepare for this winter anyway. If I wash it every day during the winter, it turns into static city, and that's no good.

### ealachan — 2012-09-18T17:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-52350>

**Leptin Reset, Day 192**

b: one each pork sausage and chicken sausage (using stuff up), two eggs, coffee w/ co and butter, handful of wakame

\- green tea w/ nothing added, mid-morning -

l: tin o' smoked herring

d: something with leftover pork

\- dinner last night was pulled pork. I used the recipe from Nomnompaleo.com, which is just a pork butt (shoulder), three strips of bacon, and some red Hawaiian salt. Stick it all in a crockpot for like 16 hours, then remove, shred and enjoy. It was really good - we made Asian-style lettuce wraps with it: lettuce, pork, a little hoisin sauce, squeeze of lime juice, scallions, red pepper, shredded carrot, cilantro. Pick up and eat like a taco. Yum! The pork was pretty fatty and rich, though...I ate too much, and felt a bit ill a few hours later, like it was having trouble leaving my stomach or something. I remembered my enzymes before we ate, so thinking I just ate too much flat out. Been a while since that's happened!

\- got to bed around 9:45 last night after a round of oxytocin therapy. Dropped off to sleep ok but then woke up and was kind of tossy-turny on and off all night. Again, think it may be a product of having eaten too much, because I was pretty indigestion-y while tossing and turning as well. Got up around 5:45 to pee, went back to bed and slept until 7. Dark dreary morning...bleh. Woke up with kind of a halfway-stuffed nose, as well. Annoying.

\- cold shower this morning - skin got down to 67 in about 5 minutes. Need to suck it up and start filling the tub and getting in it tonight. I know it won't be that bad once I actually DO it, I just...keep finding other things to do instead...lol

### ealachan — 2012-09-19T17:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-52694>

**Leptin Reset, Day 193**

b: two eggs, two pork sausages, guacamole (1/4 cup, maybe?), salsa (couple tablespoons), coffee w/ CO and butter

l: can o' olive-oil packed tonno

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was the left-over pork on a big bed of lettuce with some chopped green and red pepper, salsa and home-made guacamole. I'm pretty sure I could happily eat guacamole every day for a week and never get sick of it. Had some dark chocolate chips and macadamia nuts for dessert.

\- got to bed around 9:30-ish. Had no problems dropping off, slept hard until 2:15 when I woke up thinking I could smell something weird. I got up and investigated but it must have just been a dream. I laid there maybe 15-20 minutes before I fell back to sleep. Woke up once more around what was probably 5ish, then slept until 6:45.

### ealachan — 2012-09-20T17:37:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-52902>

**Leptin Reset, Day 194**

_In which our heroine is sitting at her desk shivering...and loving it._

b: two eggs, two pork sausages, coffee w/ co and butter and half an ounce of unsweetened baker's chocolate

l: can o' tonno and some decaff green tea

d: not sure yet - early night tonight, so needs to be something fast. probably steak.

\- dinner last night was SO GOOD: mushrooms, garlic, shrimp, linquica sausage, olive oil, asparagus, parsley, sherry, a little butter, juice of half a lemon. I did it all in one big pan, then spooned it into bowls and we went to town on it. It was quick, easy, and SO GOOD. Gotta make it again and write it up for the blog.

\- got to bed kind of late last night - 10:15ish. Couldn't seem to get comfy or settled down. Finally fell asleep, woke up in the middle of the night all tossy-turny again. I'm hoping that this is a passing phase in my personal evolution and isn't going to become a chronic thing.

\- cold shower this morning (as every morning!) - skin was 90 degrees before I got in, 68 when I got out. Took my oral temp as 98.2 before shower, 98.8 when I got out...yee hah! Sitting here at work now with all the windows open, shivering and goose-bumpy, with my co-workers wearing sweaters... Bring it! Bring on the cold! I love it. It makes me feel alive.

\- batteries in the scales at home are dead. Gotta remember to get some more. I've lost a couple inches off my belly in the last couple weeks, so really curious to see if I've dropped any pounds.

\- I SWEAR I can see better. I'm due to get my eyes checked anyway, and I'm REALLY curious to see how it goes. These glasses are two years old and scratched to \*\*\*\*, but I can see far-away stuff much clearer than I could even a few months ago, I swear it. If it's some kind of optical illusion or something, then it's a damn impressive one.

### ealachan — 2012-09-21T15:36:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-53152>

> **Lyndra;53661 wrote:** Kilts and headbanging. There must be a story in there...

My husband is Welsh and very proud of his Celtic heritage. When we were planning our wedding, he said he wanted to wear a kilt for the wedding, as that's quite traditional in Celtic families. His best man wanted to match, so he wore one too...and they looked quite spiffy, if I do say so myself.  The headbanging was just a product of good music and a fair amount of booze at the reception...LOL We're into rock and heavy metal, so it was kind of inevitable that the DJ would play something we'd head-bang and play air guitar to...

This was me and the hubs, before the headbanging...haha! It's hard to see the pattern on his kilt because it's dark, but it's the Pride of Wales tartan, which is blue, black and green with a thin red stripe running through. Also, I knit that lace shrug that I have on in the picture :

### ealachan — 2012-09-21T15:42:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-53154>

> **Cavemam;53717 wrote:** Did I hear "Men in Kilts". . . I love that.

I had no idea just how many working parts there are to a dress kilt set-up until the rentals showed up in huge boxes a couple days before the wedding. Shirt, vest, kilt, hose, flags, sporran, those crazy shoes, jacket, kilt pin, jacket pin, tartan thingy to pin to the shoulder...I think my husband probably had a longer / harder time getting dressed for the wedding than I did. All I had to do was get my hair did and put a big poofy dress on!

### ealachan — 2012-09-21T17:03:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-53173>

**Leptin Reset, Day 195**

b: linguica sausage (pork - probably 5 oz), two eggs, coffee w/ co, butter and a small handful of dark chocolate chips

l: can o' tonno

d: not sure yet

\- last night for dinner I ate almost a pound of steak. Holy hell. I wasn't scarily hungry or anything, it was just a big steak and I plowed through it. I wasn't uncomfortably full afterward, either. Weird. I can't imagine that that big of a chunk of protein in the evening is a great thing for me, but I don't generally eat that much at night so I'm not going to worry about it too much, I guess. I also had spinach and mushrooms sauteed with a little bacon, onion, garlic, and some wakame, and some dark chocolate and macadamia nuts for dessert. I've decided that I'd much rather eat the wakame dry than reconstitute it. The texture of it when it's reconstituted is like eating rubbery slime and I can't handle it. Dried, it's like super-salty potato chips though...win!  I bought a couple other kinds of sea veg to try - kelp, dulse, and some different wakame. It should be here in a few days. It's coming from Maine, so relatively local!

\- Went to bed around 9 last night. Fell asleep ok, woke up in the night and tossed and turned. This is getting old. It's like I can't get back into a deep sleep once I've woken up. I'll doze off, and then hubs will move, or I'll move, or the dog will move, or I'll hear something, or my pillow won't feel right, or you name it...and BING, I'm awake again. SO annoying. I went back to bed at 4:30 after taking hubs to work and zonked right out until 7am, though. Go figure.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-21T17:12:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-53175>

Grind/crush those seaweeds and sneak em into other stuff!!!

### ealachan — 2012-09-22T18:47:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-53414>

**Leptin Reset, Day 196**

b: three eggs, half an avocado, 2 tbsp salsa (don't know why, just fancied it), coffee w/ co and butter

l: can of tonno around 11am

d: not sure yet, going to a b'day party.

\- Ok, last night I didn't just fall off the wagon...the wagon left me in the DUST. Ouch. We went out for dinner. Two beers, a small piece of bread, some fried stuff, a big bowl of jambalaya (it has tons of shrimp and mussels in it, but sooo much rice), and half a pint of Ben and Jerry's finest later...yeah. Not so awesome. But something really interesting happened: I slept LIKE A LOG. I had a little trouble dropping off (went to bed around 8:30), but then I seriously didn't hear a thing until 4am. It was the kind of sleep where you wake up and need to take a minute to get your bearings, because you've been sleeping so hard. It felt SO GOOD. I'm not saying I'm going to start eating \*\*\*\* again just for the sake of sleeping like that, but I thought it was a very interesting connection, if nothing else. And no, I wasn't drunk from two beers, so it wasn't me passing out...lol

\- Got up at 4, took Hubs to work, back in bed by 4:30ish. Went right back to sleep, slept hard again until about 8. Got up, walked the dog, had breakfast, life is good.

\- Took my cold shower this morning and tried holding my breath a couple times when I had my face under the spray. I think I held my breath a little too long at one point or hyperventilated myself or something, because I felt funny afterwards. Not like passing out or light-headed, but just strange and kind of out of it. I took my BP and it was fine, pulse was 78, which is a little faster than I've been running for a while now...but nothing alarming. I decided to eat a can of tuna to see if that helped any, and it seems to have a little bit, so I don't know. Maybe last night's sugar excesses just caught up with me all at once this morning or something?

\- birthday party tonight. There will definitely be margaritas. Food-wise, I'm not sure what there will be, so I'll likely eat dinner with Hubs before I go...that way I have an excuse not to eat if there's stuff there that I'd rather not have.

### chocolate — 2012-09-23T02:36:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-53525>

Ealachan, my word o' de day is osmolyte. I'm learning sugars can help equalize cell volume. Us bp peeps have issues with that. But things besides sugars can, and that's where we are trying to focus in my world of dilipidated adrenals and undernourished organs.

### ealachan — 2012-09-23T18:24:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-53605>

**Leptin Reset, Day 197**

b: two chicken sausages, two hard-boiled eggs w/ celtic sea salt, coffee w/ co (out of butter, waaaagh!!)

L: -

d: fish fish fish

\- dinner last night actually turned out to be pretty paleo-friendly...yay! There was a big salad with some raspberries and peaches in it, some grilled steak and chicken, a pasta dish which I avoided like the plague, some spinach-stuffed mushrooms, and a ton of shrimp which I happily ate a whole pile of. I did drink a couple margaritas, but I was filling my glass with mostly ice because I didn't want to get too looped to be able to drive home, so all in all I really didn't drink much. Also successfully avoided dessert, which was chocolate fondue with pound cake and strawberries.

\- got to bed around 10:30, oxytocin session, zonked out. Slept like the dead until 4am. Got up, took hubs to work, came back home, oxytocin'ed again (I'm a glutton...lol), slept like the dead AGAIN until 8:30. Sun exposure first thing with a doggie walk, life is good.

\- got batteries for the scale yesterday, put them in...and the bloody thing won't turn on! Is it a sign?!  I know I'm losing. I'm down inches all over. Previously fat areas are looking deflated. Clothes are fitting better / getting too big. Did I mention that life is good?

\- edited to add: lately, I seem to want to / be able to eat celtic sea salt straight from the bag - like, stick my finger in, lick it clean, rinse and repeat a few times. Is that...normal? It tastes amazing to me, for some reason. And it's not like sea salt is anything new to me...I've used it for years.

### ealachan — 2012-09-24T16:57:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-53783>

**Leptin Reset, Day 198**

b: three eggs, pork sausage from local farm (no msg! didn't weigh, but was less than half a pound), coffee w/ raw hwc, co and butter, and some leftover pumpkin custard

L: can o' tonno

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was cod w/ tomato olive tappenade, and kale sauteed with bacon. It was good, but we totally over-ate. I'm pretty sure I put away at least a pound of fish (not including sauce or side), as did Hubs. Then we had pumpkin custard for dessert. I over-gingered and under-cloved the custard because I wasn't paying attention to what I was doing when I mixed it up...oops. Also, it had sugar in it, although not very much. I need to pick up some Splenda if I'm going to attempt to do the occassional baked dessert, I guess. Theoretically, I could have just left the sugar out completely and just had the custard as a side-dish instead...hmm. Anyway, it came out nicely texture-wise...the spices were just wonky.

\- went to bed around 8 last night, slept very deeply and had some pretty bad dreams. Woke up a couple times really briefly but then went right back to sleep. Got up at 4 to take hubs to work, then went back to bed and slept deeply again (complete with the worst dream of the night...ugh) until 7:15. Pup and I had a walkies in the cold, but we were socked in with fog so didn't get much sunshine.

\- not physically tired today, but mentally tired. I don't give a rat's ass about work, which is really bad because this is the busiest week of the year and it's just not a good time to be in this mental state. Looking at the calendar, I see I'm about two days out from Shark Week starting, so that actually explains a lot. Doesn't help...but at least I've put 2 and 2 together... It's strange, because I'm not in a super-pissy over-all PMS mood like I've had in the past. It's literally just work that I can't get motivated to give a \*\*\*\* about. The irony being, of course, that if I lose my job I'm so far up \*\*\*\* creek that no one will ever find me...LOL

\- I tried on the Old Navy jeans (aka: my goal jeans!) on Saturday. I can get them up over my hips with a bit of effort, but they're skin-tight on the thighs and they're still a good 3-4 inches from zipping up in any sort of wearable way.

### ealachan — 2012-09-25T17:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-54122>

**Leptin Reset, Day 199**

b: 5ish oz pork sausage (local, no weird stuff in it), two eggs, coffee w/ raw hwc, co, butter and a tablespoon of cocoa, some pumpkin custard, 1 sheet of toasted kombu kelp

l: can o' tonno

d: beef heart stew

\- dinner last night was not optimal. We've done worse, but we've done a lot better, too: stirfried kielbasa and asparagus, mashed sweet potato with butter. Kielbasa is one of those things that every once in a while I forget just how gross it makes me feel, so I go and eat it again...and then spend the next 3 hours really wishing I hadn't. That was last night. Ugh. Very few things give me indigestion these days, but that crap does it every time. No more!

\- Got my seaweeds in the mail last night...w00t! I got kombu (kelp), wakame, and dulse. It's low-temperature dried, so it comes out of the bag kind of leathery, not brittle at all. The directions on the bag of kelp suggested, among other things, to try unrolling a piece of it (it comes out to roughly a 5X5 square) and toasting it until crisp in a pan, then eating it like chips. It came out tasting almost exactly like kale chips that I've made in the past, but with far better texture. I really liked it! I'm going to toast some more up tomorrow morning and bring it with me to have with lunch. It has a pretty huge iodine content, and I'm quite curious to see if that makes any difference for me.

\- Went to bed around 10:30 last night, dropped off fine, felt like I kept having bouts of sleeping really deeply, then waking up briefly, then sleeping hard again. I seem to get this when I sleep with ice packs on my neck, now. Weird. Woke up at about 6:30 with no alarm. It was 36 degrees out, and I walked the dog in a tank top and shorts and wasn't even chilly.

\- made a big beef stew in the crockpot for tonight. It does have potatoes in it, but I can pick around those. I used mostly veal heart. First time cooking with it, and I think I like it. It was kind of a pain to trim up, but not a huge issue really. I tried a couple pieces when I was searing it off before I stuck it in the stew, and it's quite nice. Very strongly beefy, but not unpleasant.

### ealachan — 2012-09-26T16:59:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-54419>

**Leptin Reset, Day 200!!!!**

b: half a can of salmon cooked in bacon fat, two slices of bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ raw hwc, butter, co, and 1 tbsp cocoa powder

l: can o' tonno, kelp chips, green tea

d: leftover beef heart stew

\- dinner last night was my beef heart stew, and some Brussels sprouts roasted with bacon. The stew came out REALLY well...will definitely make that again. In fact, I'm going to go over to the farm and buy up any hearts they have in the freezer, because grass-fed veal heart for $1.99 a pound seems like a good deal to me! I also finished off the pumpkin custard, which I have deemed evil because every time I eat it in the evenings, I have bad dreams and/or can't sleep. Last night, could. not. sleep. Ugh.

\- As mentioned above, couldn't sleep last night. Fairly convinced it was the sugar in the pumpkin custard of doom, so I won't be making that from that particular recipe again. Should have known better! Anyway - tossed and turned, couldn't get comfy, couldn't drop off to sleep or stay asleep for very long all night. Finally got up around 7:15, and my ass is pretty well dragging already. Gonna be a long day.

\- Webinar and Q&A last night were very interesting. I doubled my curcumin dose this morning (from 500mg to 1000mg of the Jarrow 95% extract stuff), and when I get my resveretrol in the mail, i'll start in on that. I'm hoping that I can also up my unsweetened chocolate consumption as a cheaper way to supplement resveratrol, otherwise i'm going to have to take a serious look at dropping some other supps so that I can afford the high dose of resveratrol that Dr. K is recommending.

\- gotta get my ass into the bathtub. I keep putting it off, and I just need to suck it up and DO IT already. It's not even bad once I get in there...it's just the mental block I have about actually getting into the cold water that's tripping me up. Cold showers don't bother me a bit, so it's not the actual COLD that's keeping me from doing it.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-09-27T15:40:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-54734>

### ealachan — 2012-09-27T17:31:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-54746>

> **paulalynn;55556 wrote:** OMG...I hope this happens to me. I haven't even been able to bring myself to buy a can of it yet so I can't imagine eating it! So seriously, it's decent when it's cooked in bacon grease?

It's not bad! I usually cook a couple strips of bacon and crumble them up in the salmon, too...that gives it something other than just the mushy texture, which helps. Don't freak out when you open the can and see skin and bones...those are super good for you, and they mash right into the rest of the fish pretty easily, so you don't even know they're there. Try it, go on try it!

### ealachan — 2012-09-27T18:51:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-54763>

**Leptin Reset, Day 201**

_In which our heroine wonders if she should stop calling it the Leptin Reset because she's kind of deviated from that at this point...hmmm..._

b: half a can of salmon cooked in bacon fat, two strips of bacon, fat coffee w/ 1 tbsp cocoa

l: can o' tonno, kelp "chips" (one sheet of kelp's worth), green tea (decaf, no fat added)

d: not sure yet, going to be out of the house

\- dinner last night was leftover stew, a glass of wine, and some chocolate chips and macadamia nuts. I managed to get my husband hooked on the chocolate chips and mac nuts just in time to tell him we can't have them anymore...whoops. The chocolate is only 60% dark and it's just too sugary, ESPECIALLY for him. I think rather than try and find desserts that he can have, I need to just use a little tough love and say no desserts at all anymore.

\- got to sleep around 10:30 last night, slept deeply most of the night. Woke up a few times after around 2:30ish but kept falling right back asleep. Woke up as it started getting light in the bedroom around 6:40. Got up, had a walk out in the almost-sunshine with the pup and the hubs. Sky was pretty today...wish I'd remembered my cell phone when we were out.

\- Found an app for tracking one's poo via the Bristol Stool chart. AWWW YEAH. Unreasonably excited by the prospect of being able to keep track of my level 4/5 clunkers...LOL Yes I am secretly a 12 year old boy with a 32-year old obese woman's hormone panel...hehehehe

### ealachan — 2012-09-28T18:01:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-55017>

**EP Journal, Day 202**

B: two eggs, two chicken sausages, coffee w/ co, butter, and 1 tbsp cocoa

l: can o' tonno, kelp chips

d: not sure yet

\- was out last night, so dinner was late-ish (7pm). I had a caeser salad with a little bit of chicken on it, which was all in all pretty not tasty and just generally a waste of money. Hubs bought me a bar of chocolate later on as well, which I of course ate. It didn't keep me up when I went to bed, so I guess I was really tired.

\- got to bed around 10:30, zonked right out. Slept deeply until whining dog woke me up around 2:30am. Got up for a pee, passed out again as soon as I got back into bed. Slept deeply again until about 6:45 when the dog insisted someone get up and pay attention to him.  Took him out in tank top and yoga pants...it was about 45 degrees out and I was perfectly comfortable.

\- changed the name of my ramblings to "EP Journal" (EP for epi-paleo), because I'm not really following the Leptin Reset very carefully anymore. I've decided to focus harder on circadian issues and getting enough fish and healthy fats in, while adding in CT and trying to go into solid ketosis for the winter, and see how I do.

\- going to be up early and out all day tomorrow at an event. Should be fun, though - lots of people I don't get to see very often.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-28T18:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-55021>

ok, so you seem to love your Tonno - is this just tuna, or is it a special kind?!

### ealachan — 2012-09-28T18:33:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-55024>

It's tuna packed in olive oil. Some brands like to use the Italian word for it, tonno, to make it look fancy-pants, I think... I actually hate plain water-packed tuna, and "traditional" tuna salad made with mayo, but I friggin' LOVE olive-oil packed tuna with a little black pepper on it. That's seriously all it needs. So nummy. Not hugely high in DHA or iodine as far as I can tell, but tons of selenium and protein, with no work involved. SOLD!

eta: I like the Genova brand, but Bumble Bee is pretty good, too. And 2 cans for $4 isn't too shabby price-wise.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-28T19:47:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-55043>

### ealachan — 2012-10-01T17:27:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-55574>

**EP Journal, day 203**

b: half a can of salmon, two slices of bacon, coffee w/ co, hwc and butter and 1 tbsp cocoa

l: can o' tonno, kelp chips, green tea

d: leftover chowdah

\- dinner last night was the clam and cod chowder I linked to in the last post. It was good, but it gave me heartburn. I think it might have been the cream that did it. Interestingly, I had some sugar-free Jello for dessert and it killed the heartburn dead in its tracks.

\- Saturday I fell off the food wagon hard and had a 20-hour day full of artificial light...felt hung over all day yesterday, like worse than after a night of heavy drinking. Holy crap. Really drove home the point that I eat and live SO much better now than I used to.

\- I felt FULL after breakfast this morning. Not just sated, but actually FULL. It was strange, as it's really not that much food when you think about it, especially for someone my size. Not sure if it was a by-product of my guts being messed up from Saturday still, or if things are finally starting to click in my gut and my brain.

\- Shark Week (aka: TOM) started in the wee hours of Sunday morning, which means I had a 31 day cycle. Had exactly one bout of cramps lasting about 5 minutes, which was how I knew I'd started. A little spotting on Saturday as well, but nothing major.

\- asthma has been playing up lately when I've been out walking the dog. It tends to get a little worse when it gets cold out, but it hasn't been THAT cold. Thinking I might need to try axing all the dairy for a couple weeks and see what happens. I don't have tons of dairy anyway...mostly just hwc and butter in my morning coffee. Should be easy enough to make a batch of ghee and use that in my coffee instead, I would think.

\- slept hard last night - zonked out around 9:30ish and woke up at 4am feeling like I'd just closed my eyes. Put on the blue blockers, took hubs to work, came home and crawled back into bed, woke up at 6:45 when the alarm went off and actually hit the snooze button for another 15 minutes! I think my body is still trying to repair the damage from Saturday. It didn't help that it's grey and rainy and cold out and I was just in total hibernation mode.

### ealachan — 2012-10-02T19:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-55868>

**EP Journal, Day 204**

b: half a can of salmon, three slices of bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ butter and CO

l: can o' tonno, kelp chips, green tea

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was leftover clam cod chowder, along with a bratwurst sausage. Had some jello with the last of the HWC for dessert.

\- had a really \*\*\*\*ty afternoon yesterday, and did something I haven't done in a LONG time - I went to the store and got a brownie and wolfed it down. It instantly made me feel even worse, of course, because I knew exactly what I was doing to myself as I was doing it, but it was a train I couldn't stop at that point. I don't know if yesterday was a case of what Quelsen has explained as re-living everything you lived through as you put the fat on, but boy howdy was I re-living some unpleasant \*\*\*\* yesterday afternoon. It started out as just general malaise and apathy, but over the course of a few hours it snowballed into straight-up self-loathing. I honestly haven't felt that way about myself in a really long time. The upside to the experience is that if it happens again, I'll be ready for it. Sans brownies.

\- was asleep by around 9:15 last night. Passed right out, woke up once in the night but dropped right back off, got up at 4:15 to take hubs to work. Got back to sleep around 5am, slept til 6:45. No sun again this morning, socked in with heavy fog.

\- trying to take my resveratrol buccally this morning was kind of a joke. I got the NOW Foods kind, and it's a powder in a capsule. I figured I could just open the capsule, dump the powder in between my cheek and gum, and let it sit there for a while. Well. Remember Pixie Stix, from when you were a kid? Those straws full of sweet-tart sugary powder stuff? And how, if you didn't hold your breath just right when you dumped one in your mouth, you'd kind of inhale it, choke and sputter and spit it everywhere? YEAH. That's how it went with the resveratrol this morning. Really glad my husband wasn't home to see that little performance...LOL! It didn't actually taste horrible to me - it tastes like dirt and roots, kind of bitter...but not as OMG HORRIBLE as I was expecting.

\- Got a note back from my doctor's office that my insurance won't cover even basic blood work until after 11/22 (as that's the date of my last annual exam), which is annoying, but sort of what I was expecting. They won't do any of the advanced stuff I want, anyway...i'll have to get my doc to write an order to Quest for that stuff and then drive 90 minutes each way to have it drawn. You know, because the insurance company has decided that it will save the customer money by not having labs done through the local hospital that my clinic is affiliated with, that's 3 miles down the road. It might be saving THEM money, but it sure isn't saving ME any money. Jerks.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-02T19:41:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-55870>

Sorry about yesterday! We all go there. Sounds like a really quick recovery!!!

Your insurance is worse than suck?! I have some advice, get a NEW doc and go in complaining about all kinds of things wrong with you. Be sure and tell them about all the people in your family that have died from Cancer, Heart Disease, Stroke, Diabetes etc.

Just sayin,

### ealachan — 2012-10-02T20:07:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-55884>

> **Cavemam;57061 wrote:** Sorry about yesterday! We all go there. Sounds like a really quick recovery!!!
>
>
>
> Your insurance is worse than suck?! I have some advice, get a NEW doc and go in complaining about all kinds of things wrong with you. Be sure and tell them about all the people in your family that have died from Cancer, Heart Disease, Stroke, Diabetes etc.
>
>
>
> Just sayin,

It's not actually my doctor that's the problem so much as the insurance. I'm pretty sure that once I'm able to actually sit down with my doctor and say "look, I want to know what these numbers are", she won't have a problem writing the orders for the labs...it's just that I need to then take like half a day off to drive all over creation to go to an independent lab for the actual blood pulls if I want the insurance to even think about covering them. If I get them all done locally, it's all subject to my $3k deductible. I have an FSA and about a grand left in it, but I use that to pay for stuff like dental work, glasses, and hubs' test strips. I don't really want to empty it out just on one set of labs. Also, I'm trying to figure out if I can use my FSA to cover the saliva cortisol tests. There's no way I'll get my doctor to order that test for me, so that's a better use of FSA funds if I can make it work.

Insurance is one of my pet peeves. I utterly hate it. It should not be this expensive or this complicated...but it always will be, because there's so much money to be made off the sick and dying. It's disgusting.

### ealachan — 2012-10-03T18:10:25+0300

How do I swish with powder, though? I'm thinking I might need to put the powder in a very small amount of water to make kind of a paste of a swishable consistency...if you see what I mean. I didn't have time to mess around with it this morning so I just took the capsule like normal.

Yeah, I seem to have bounced back from the crappy day pretty quickly, which is definitely a plus. A year ago, I'd have spent three days getting over the one bad day! And given that I used to spend every single day medicated for depression and anxiety, one bad day now and then doesn't seem so bad in the grand scheme of things.

### ealachan — 2012-10-03T18:24:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-56072>

**EP Journal, Day 205**

b: 1.5 pork sausages, two eggs, coffee w/ co, butter and 1 tbsp cocoa

l: can o' tonno, kelp chips, green tea

d: leftover chili

\- dinner last night was quick and dirty chili. It's loosely based off the chili recipe on Mark's Daily Apple - bacon, ground beef, spices, onion, garlic, carrot, green pepper, tomatoes, salt. I over-cumin'ed because I was running my mouth to our dinner guest and not paying attention, but no one seemed to mind much. I served the chili with diced avocado and a little diced sharp cheddar on top. It did have half a beer added...and I did drink the other half of the beer...but to my credit, I didn't go and get ANOTHER beer to go with dinner, so yay me.  Had a bit of dark chocolate for dessert.

\- went to sleep around 9:30, don't remember waking up at all in the night (!!!), woke up at around 6:30 this morning with a bladder so full it hurt. Weird. I did drink some water before bed, but certainly not more than like half a cup's worth. Must be my body was just shedding water as I slept or something. I won't complain...I'll take all the water-shedding I can get!

\- started on Mg Malate last night. The dose is 3 pills, but I only took two last night, to see what it would do. The bottle actually says to take 1 pill three times daily with meals, but I don't want to take mag in the mornings when I'm taking d3, so I'm going to try splitting it between lunch and evening. Anyway - I took 2 last night (and no threonate). It made me a little sleepy. No bowel problems. Not sure if the Mg Malate was why I slept so deeply...we'll see if the pattern continues. I've got 1x Malate and 2x Threonate, plus 1x Resveratrol to take with lunch. I need to re-jig both my and hubs' supplements in the near future to make sure we're not taking stuff that's competing for absorbtion, etc. Also, I got some LEF high-dose vitamin K2 to start us on once we're done with the crappy stuff I got at GNC.

### ealachan — 2012-10-04T17:09:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-56224>

**Ep Journal, Day 206**

_In which our heroine really wishes this headache would \*\*\*\* off..._

b: half a can of salmon, two slices bacon, couple tablespoons dulse, two eggs, coffee w/ co and butter, 1 tbsp cocoa

l: can o' tonno, green tea

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was leftover chili with a little cheese on it, and some chocolate and macadamia nuts. NO BEER, even though we had some left over from dinner guest the other night. WOO!

\- got to bed around 10, dropped right off to sleep, slept deeply until about 5am when the dog started in barking at something. Dozed on and off until 6:45, then got up. Another crappy dark morning...bleh. Hard to get going when there's no sunshine to wake me up.

\- headachey. I've noticed it kind of on and off for a couple days now, but this morning it's pretty nasty. Hurts in the front of my head and my sinuses. Not sure if it's a side-effect of the resveratrol I started taking, or if my body is fighting something off, or maybe it's from all the seaweed I've been eating? Ugh. I'm laying off the seaweed today and maybe tomorrow to see if that helps.

\- had a thought in the shower this morning that maybe I should drop the coconut oil out of my coffee, just do butter and cocoa in it for a while. Since I'm trying to listen to my body more, I figure I'll give it a try and see what happens. Maybe my body is trying to tell me that since coconut oil is not appropriate for the season, I shouldn't be eating it?

\- ran out of ox bile a few days ago. I was fine for a couple days, but things were pretty loose and urgent this morning (not full-blown D, but...not good...lol), so I guess I still need the ox bile. Shall be ordering some up ASAP.

### ealachan — 2012-10-04T23:35:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-56330>

I WILL HAVE YOU KNOW...I just turned down beer. COMPANY-BOUGHT BEER. Beer I did not have to pay for. Turned it down. Said I didn't want it. DID NOT TAKE THE BEER.

I'm still thinking about the beer...but I didn't take the beer.

I don't think y'all understand how much I love beer, and how hard it was to just say "thank you but no" to FREE BEER. THE GOOD STUFF.

Somebody make me up a trophy.

### ealachan — 2012-10-05T16:54:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-56477>

**EP Journal, Day 207**

b: half a can of salmon, 3 strips bacon, coffee w/ butter and less CO than usual, 1 tbsp cocoa

l: can of tonno

d: not sure yet. Have some ground beef I need to use up, so likely that.

\- dinner last night was not optimal - hot dogs (albeit the "all natural, nitrate free" ones), broccoli salad, cole slaw. It was combination of me being unprepared and husband having company over right at dinner time, so we were rushing around to eat something quick and get it cleaned up before company arrived. Meh. At least I didn't go for rotisserie chicken again. Last time we were in this situation, I got that and it made me not feel well after.

\- had a nice evening of relaxing and no TV last night, for a change. Put the laundry away, played with the dog, played some games on my iPod, then curled up and read for a while, all with my blue-blockers on of course. Got to sleep around 10ish and zonked right out. Didn't even hear husband come to bed, but he woke me up at like 6am for nookie. I'm not a morning nookie person AT ALL. I like to sleep. LOL

\- feet ache lately - specifically the large joint on both feet where the big toe joins to the foot. No swelling, no redness, no heat...just ache. It's not bad enough to want to take painkillers or anything, it's just annoying. If it was gout, it would be super painful and swollen, right?

\- sun's out for the first time all week. Might sneak out for a walk in the woods at lunch time.

\- sketchy guts last night and this morning. Not sure if it was from the poor dinner choices or if the lack of ox bile plus the addition of mag malate is finally catching up to me...

### ealachan — 2012-10-05T17:46:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-56483>

Also, forgot to mention earlier - I busted out some random Pilates moves last night while I was reading. They seemed a lot easier than I remember them being a couple years ago when I learned them in a class. Yay, abs! Yay, butt and thighs!

### ealachan — 2012-10-06T17:17:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-56596>

**EP Journal, Day 208**

b: omlette with ham, cheese, veggies, 2 sausage patties, coffee w/ half and half (we went out for breakfast)

l: tuna, maybe?

d: cod

\- dinner last night was kind of a crazy hot mess - ground beef and pork, onion, garlic, green pepper, mushrooms, kalamata olives, herbs, and tomato sauce, all cooked together and then baked with some cheese on top. It was originally supposed to be stuffed peppers, but I didn't have the time or the inclination to mess around with actually stuffing and baking the peppers, so we just had the stuffing in bowls instead. It was tasty! I loaded it up with fennel, oregano and thyme, so it actually tasted like I had just scraped all the good stuff off a couple pieces of pizza and dumped it into a bowl. Pizza is one of the few SAD things I find myself missing, so this was a win!

\- stayed up wayyyy too late. Had my orange glasses on the whole time, but still...not good. DIdn't get to bed til almost 1am, and was up by 6:45. Hubs wasn't home, and I have a hard time going to bed and getting to sleep when he's not around. I did sleep well once I actually went to bed, so that's a plus.

\- have to clean the apartment thoroughly today. Not excited. Hate cleaning. Parents are coming up for dinner tonight which should be fun, though.

### Endless — 2012-10-06T18:57:24+0300

### ealachan — 2012-10-06T21:15:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-56617>

### ealachan — 2012-10-07T03:47:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-56662>

Made what is quickly becoming my signature dish, cod with tomato olive tapenade, for my mom and dad tonight. They both seemed to really like it. My mom has always liked fish, but my dad has NOT, so getting him to admit he enjoyed a fish dish was a victory... Also spent some time explaining my pile of supps to my mom. She was sitting at the breakfast bar where all the bottles are sitting, and started picking up each one and asking what it "does for you". I patiently explained why I took every single one of them, and she was interested in getting some resveratrol and curcumin for herself. Another victory! I also gave her my copy of Practical Paleo to flip through (gotta start somewhere) and she seemed to think most of it looked pretty do-able.

Slowly, slowwwwwwly...be vewy vewy quiet. We're hunting SAD-eaters...

### ealachan — 2012-10-09T17:32:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-57101>

**EP Journal, Day 209**

b: two chicken sausages, two eggs, coffee w/ butter & 1 tbsp cocoa

l: olive-oil packed tuna, kelp chips

d: something involving shrimp, I think.

\- dinner last night was wonton soup without the wontons: I made a batch of pork dumpling filling (ground pork, napa cabbage, scallions, ginger, tamari, sesame oil, sherry) and made little meatballs out of it. I cooked them in chicken stock that I had doctored up with some scallion and ginger. I added some more shredded Napa cabbage, some dulse, a little more tamari and some vinegar. It was REALLY friggin' good. Next time I'm going to add some chopped shrimp as well, I think.

\- Went to bed around 10pm, dropped right off to sleep quite quickly, and had no trouble staying asleep...until my husband started cuddling me at like 5:30am. I like the cuddling, don't get me wrong, but...when I'm asleep, I want to STAY asleep until daylight if possible. Pretty tired and grumpy this morning.

\- Ate too much sweet stuff over the weekend, and too much dairy yesterday. I made pumpkin custard again, which is good but it has cream in it, plus more whipped cream on top...yeah. I woke up this morning with my sinuses all stuffed up. I'm 99% sure it's dairy doing it to me. Need to axe it completely for a few weeks and see what happens.

\- My skin is SO DRY lately. If I don't smear on a ton of coconut oil right after my shower, my forehead gets all tight and burny-feeling. Do not like!

### ealachan — 2012-10-10T16:45:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-57314>

**EP Journal, Day 210**

b: half a can of salmon, 3 slices of slightly burnt bacon (oops), two eggs, coffee w/ butter and 1 tbsp cocoa

l: can of tuna

d: dunno yet. Going out to a swanky place as it's our anniversary!

\- dinner last night was shrimp scampi over spaghtti squash. It was one of my better batches, I must say. Usually I have to drain the cooked spaghetti squash for ages and it STILL ends up a wet mess on the plate, but this one I had last night cooked up perfectly. The only thing I did differently was that I stabbed a couple of "vent" holes in the back of the squash before I put it into the oven. I don't know if that's what made the difference, or if it was just a drier squash, but I was really happy with how it came out. We finished off the pumpkin custard for dessert, and I'm going to try not to make it again until Thanksgiving, because even with the reduced amount of maple syrup I put into it, it just made me want sweet, sweet and more sweet...and that's bad for me.

\- got to bed around 9ish, read for a while, probably fell asleep by 10? Woke up once in the night when the dog barked at something, then zonked right back out and didn't hear anything until husband's alarm went off at 6:30ish. The difference in sleep I've been getting since switching to Mg Malate in the evenings has been pretty noticeable. I'm not waking up at every little sound anymore. I feel like I'm sleeping much more deeply.

\- cloudy and dreary again today. Meh.

### SeaHorse — 2012-10-11T15:51:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-57572>

THIS morning was beautiful though wasn't it? Amazing what a little sun will do for your spirits.

### ealachan — 2012-10-11T16:20:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-57581>

> **SeaHorse;58889 wrote:** THIS morning was beautiful though wasn't it? Amazing what a little sun will do for your spirits.

Absolutely! I'm now sitting in my office looking at the sun filtering through the golden leaves, and scheming a way to get out of work at lunch time to go for a walk in the woods... Methinks we better enjoy it while we can. They're talking about snow up north the next few days. Nothing major, but still.

### ealachan — 2012-10-11T16:31:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-57584>

**EP Journal, Day 211**

b: half a can of salmon, two strips of bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ cocoa and butter

l: can of tonno - forgot my kelp chips, ack!

d: not sure yet

\- Well, all you raw-fish eaters should be proud of me. We went to a very nice restaurant for our anniversary dinner last night, and I ordered tuna and hamachi tartare for a starter! I'd never had hamachi before, raw or cooked. It was paired with mango, green onion and...not sure what else. It was ok. The tuna part of the duo was raw tuna, capers, pimiento, olive oil...and it was lovely. Could have happily eaten a whole bowl of just that! I also had shellfish stew as my main, which was clams, mussels, shrimp and scallops in an AMAZINGLY yummy broth full of fennel and onions...ugh. So good. Dessert was a flourless chocolate torte with a macadamia nut crust and raspberry sauce. Very good. I did drink two beers, but they weren't full pints, only 12oz each.

\- Got to bed around 10:15, zonked right out. I think I woke up once around like 4am, but other than that, I slept like a rock right through until 7ish. I think I need to start leaving our window uncovered at night now, because it doesn't get light until like 6:45 and I'm not waking up early on my own anymore. We don't get tons of light in the window at night unless the moon happens to be full, so I'm thinking it's a fair trade-off for being able to actually wake up on time without an alarm in the morning again.

\- had a wicked headache again yesterday afternoon, felt like sinuses. I hope it's just a passing thing and not the new norm because it makes me really grumpy.

### ealachan — 2012-10-12T17:34:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-57767>

**EP Journal, Day 212**

b: 6 pieces of bacon, coffee w/ butter and 1 tbsp cocoa

l: tuna?

d: beef stew

\- was. not. hungry. this morning. Not sick-feeling, not anti-food, just...not hungry. The only reason I had the bacon was because I wanted the grease and the hot pan to sear my beef off for beef stew. Otherwise, I'd have happily just had my coffee and been done with it. Weird. I guess when I think about it, I really haven't been "OMGSOHUNGRY" in the mornings for a while now. Back when I first started the reset, I was seriously ravenous every morning and every evening. Now I'm usually just like "oh yeah, I should eat". Interesting.

\- dinner last night was steak, with kale, mushrooms and onions on the side. Plus half a bottle of champagne. And some ice cream. Whoopsies.

\- got to bed around 8:30 last night, oxytocin-fest, was asleep by 9:30ish, but kept waking up and slept fitfully. Pretty sure I have myself to thank for that, what with the ice cream and champers consumption. Heh. Had to get up at 4 to take hubs to work - came home and went right back to sleep, woke up at 7am.

\- beef stew in the crock pot for dinner tonight - beef heart and chuck seared in bacon fat, carrot, celery, onions, mushrooms, beef stock, red wine, garlic, bay leaves, thyme, salt and pepper. Interested to see how the heart ends up after only 8 hours of crockpot cooking, as last time it went for like 16 hours and was pretty tender.

### tggywnkle@aol.com — 2012-10-12T21:55:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-57835>

Hi, Ealachan-- I made your cod w tomato olive tapenade today. It was so good! Thank you so much for posting the recipe. I am going to try your pork dumplings next, they sound yummy! I love wonton soup, but if I really think about it, it's the filling & broth I like bc that's actually where the flavor is. Realizing that pasta or bread doesn't really add that much flavor to things has really helped me avoid a lot of it. Thanks for helping w that.

### ealachan — 2012-10-12T22:23:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-57844>

> **Molliecat;59156 wrote:** Hi, Ealachan-- I made your cod w tomato olive tapenade today. It was so good! Thank you so much for posting the recipe. I am going to try your pork dumplings next, they sound yummy! I love wonton soup, but if I really think about it, it's the filling & broth I like bc that's actually where the flavor is. Realizing that pasta or bread doesn't really add that much flavor to things has really helped me avoid a lot of it. Thanks for helping w that.

That's the same epiphany I had about wonton soup, too! I was like "what are the wrappers really adding to this, except kind of a gluey, slimey texture?" Lo and behold, wonton soup without the actual wontons is good.

The actual recipe I used for the pork dumplings is [here](http://http://www.epicurious.com/recipes/food/views/Pork-Dumplings-106237). The recipe does include directions for making your own wonton skins, but just skip that part, of course. Also, the first time I used this recipe I was kind of horrified by the amount of salt and soy sauce (I use tamari, you could also use coconut aminos) that goes into it, but...it works. I tried it once with half the salt called for and it just wasn't quite right. One more tip, while I'm thinking of it - I found that the Napa cabbage is way easier to deal with if you cut the ribs out of it first. I pretty much discarded all of the ribs from the cabbage, roughly chopped the leaves, then blanced them in boiling water for a couple minutes. Really squeeeeeeeeze the moisture out of the cabbage once it has cooled off - if you leave it soggy, it will make your dumplings / meatballs fall apart. Learned that one the hard way.
