# Thread: Liver - do you or don't you
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 58
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndra — 2012-03-16T01:41:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1152>

I haven't been able to bring myself to make liver, after a nasty experience years ago when I stunk up the house for days. I know it has all sorts of health benefits so I'd like to get to the point where I can tolerate it.

Is there any "mild" form of liver? Better ways to prepare it so it won't smell so much? I bought some liverwurst at the deli one day, and it didn't taste bad, but I worry about what else is in it.

### Gemini — 2012-03-16T02:16:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1174>

This may sound a bit odd - - - but here it goes,

I grill my liver - I use a marinade (term is used very lightly) of left over bacon grease, some salt and pepper, just smear grease over liver slices then salt and pepper to taste.

Put it on the grill using indirect heat - it does not take too long to cook, and it is delicious!!!

If I happen to take it off the grill too soon, I just finish the cooking in a skillet, but the grill flavor remains.

Beef liver is the strongest in my opinion - maybe try lamb or veal liver.

I will use chicken livers (usually from whole foods market) and make a chinese dish - I make my own soy sauce using water, lime juice, and some blackstrap molasses.

### Kcaliaz — 2012-03-16T02:35:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1185>

A really good way to get it in with no taste (my kids even eat it and have no idea!) is to get it ground into your grassfed ground beef. I purchase the "primal ground beef" from Slankers (can find them online). Great product and sooooo easy to eat liver!! Try it, you'll love it! In fact, I just ordered 15 more pounds of it today...

### rbu5@sssnet.com — 2012-03-16T02:49:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1200>

Liver is my favorite meal after steak! I use beef liver (soaked overnight in milk and rinsed well). I just cook it in bacon grease, salt and pepper it well. Sometimes I add onions if I am able to add extra carbs. Another yummy way is to wrap bacon around chicken livers and bake for 30 or so minutes till bacon is crispy in a large dish. I will make a pound of the livers and 1/2 lb bacon (half slices) and eat off this for days..usually 4 or 5 pieces per meal. I soak the chicken livers in a marinade of garlic powder, garlic salt, a little soy sauce and Worcestershire sauce to flavor.

### kalibex — 2012-03-16T03:25:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1229>

You might like to try chicken livers first - they're a bit 'milder', I find.

### sandy — 2012-03-16T04:36:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1268>

I just can't even make myself try it. I have to make it for hub once in awhile and its so gross. I do love liver sausage, and will eat that on some coconut "bread"

### Glamazon — 2012-03-16T05:05:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1280>

This is good. I found it on the net. It reminds me of liver sausage which is not as intensely flavored as just liver. I always think of pate as baby food for adults.

LisaÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s Luscious Liver Pate

1 pound liver, as free range and grass fed as possible, cut into pieces 

1/2 onion, chopped sea salt and pepper 

1 bunch parsley 

butter, coconut oil, olive oil, or organic lard 

seasonings of your choice: try an Italian blendÃ¢â‚¬Â¦herbes de ProvenceÃ¢â‚¬Â¦rosemary, garlic, thyme, sageÃ¢â‚¬Â¦ 

1\. Sautee onion in oil until translucent (5 min). Remove and place in blender. 

2\. Add more oil to the pan and add liver. 

3\. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and seasonings. 

4\. Cook on both sides. Inside should still be pink (few minutes on both sides). 

5\. Add liver AND JUICES FROM PAN to blender. If the cooking hasnÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t yielded much juice, add a splash of water.

 6. Blend. 

7\. Taste. It shouldnÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t taste overly Ã¢â‚¬Å“liveryÃ¢â‚¬Â. If it does, add more seasonings. 

8\. Eat on its own or with crackers, tortilla, celery, carrotÃ¢â‚¬Â¦ 

9\. Prepare for bionic strength!

[http://hartkeisonline.com/whole-and-natural-foods/liver-the-worlds-most-nutrient-dense-food-easy-liver-pate-recipe/](http://http://hartkeisonline.com/whole-and-natural-foods/liver-the-worlds-most-nutrient-dense-food-easy-liver-pate-recipe/)

### Lyndra — 2012-03-16T05:15:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1283>

> **Glamazon;1281 wrote:** This is good. I found it on the net. It reminds me of liver sausage which is not as intensely flavored as just liver. I always think of pate as baby food for adults.

Sounds good - I will have to give it a try.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-16T07:17:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1315>

We had this discussion on the MDA monster thread with everyone sharing their suggestions. It was concluded that lamb's liver and pig's liver are the mildest. MamaGrok eats one ounce raw (chopped up and quickly!) every morning. Shijin adds it to her burgers and neither her nor her kids mind. I buy high quality liverwurst from a local butcher (made of nothing but pig's liver, pig's head and herbs) and it's easy to eat, both cold and cooked (where as when I tried to cook chicken livers I almost puked). Dr K said to me to soak them in milk before hand if you do cook. I eat some every day because my K2 is low.

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-16T07:47:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1320>

I made meatballs with 1Kg beef mince and 500gm sheeps liver, with turmeric, cumin, maca, cinnamon, oragano. After they had been devoured, I told my 13yold there was liver in there and she said "Oh, that's a good way to get it into us. They're yum."

### caroline — 2012-03-16T09:19:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1334>

I make chicken liver pate a lot - I take it to parties etc as well. everyone loves it. It is very simple with onions, garlic, salt and pepper and butter and then put it in a food processor. I can post the recipe if anyone is nterested

### CoolingWeb — 2012-03-16T09:34:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1335>

Cumin. That is what you need.

250g Beef liver cut thin slices (Stroganoff). Add Salt and LOTS of Cumin (3-4 tea spoons or more) and some garlic (powder is fine). Mix well. I add red chili peppers flakes to make it HOT HOT

Put olive oil and butter in a skillet. When butter melts and skilit is hot add the liver.

Toss for few minutes until it gets dark.

(lately, I started adding the curcumin to it. Generous amounts. 2-4 tbs. I add it 1m before Its done.)

You are done.

Eat with steamed vegetables. Broccoli and such ...

Enjoy.

### Grottenolm — 2012-03-16T10:16:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1343>

Having never eaten liver while young, or prevously in my life, I would highly recommend lamb liver to start with. It tastes mild and sweet, and even milder than chicken overs in my opinion. Soak it in lemon juice for a few hours before cooking to draw out the bitter blood, and you will be sweet.

I like to mix it with lamb mince and make spicy meatball curry. Or just panfry thin strips on extremely High heat to seal, leaving the middle still pink. Serve with bacon and don't forget to deglaze the pan with cream or wine for an accompanying sauce  I love leaving the butcher with a cheap bag of organs that will let me eat like a queen.

### mtdejong1@gmail.com — 2012-03-16T13:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1360>

I mix it with ground meat and Italian herbs, then fry. Tastes good with ketchup.

### David — 2012-03-16T15:02:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1411>

Check out the information at the Weston Price site:

[http://www.westonaprice.org/food-features/liver-files](http://http://www.westonaprice.org/food-features/liver-files)

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-16T17:37:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1510>

I hate all the liver I have tried so far, but I knew I needed to eat it. And I can actually afford it grassfed so I had to find a way.

This month I finally figured it out.

I use grassfed beef liver from WF. I soak it over night in milk. Then rinse it. I put it in a food processor and add salt, an onion, and bacon if I have it. Blend it until it is a paste. Then add it to my ground beef. Then I make the burgers however I want to. I love to add curry powder to my burgers, but I change it up often. I have extremely sensitive taste and smell.. so on occasion I get a faint hint of the taste of something "different" than beef, but it's not bad in any way. Most people I think wouldn't even know it was in there.

### Nan — 2012-03-16T22:22:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1685>

One key to great fried liver is to make sure the slices are thin, about 1/4 inch. Most store liver is about 1/2 inch thick, which means the marinade doesn't get through it. I marinate the liver in some white wine (milk, or rice wine or other light vinegar work too) overnight; coat with coconut flour, fry in bacon fat quickly, then top with fried onions and bacon. And for some reason, I don't think the meal is complete without turnip greens or other sharp greens as a side. I feel better when I eat liver.

### vkiernan — 2012-03-16T23:14:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1706>

I love fried beef liver and onions. I fry up my onions in butter then add the liver and fry it in amongst the onions till browned. But, don't overcook it. This grass-fed beef liver has been the best. I even eat it in the am with my eggs. Too bad one doesn't get more with a 1/4 beef. Will have to find some somewhere else. Quick, easy and delish.

### Birdy — 2012-03-16T23:55:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1728>

I like cooking it with bacon and onions.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-17T00:39:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-1748>

I buy lamb liver, puree it up, then mix it in w/grassfed/finished ground beef - and season w/ turmeric, cumin, cinnamon, black pepper, salt and garlic

On monday I made enough to last me 2 weeks...and then the kids wanted burgers for lunch - and then they wanted them again 2 days later!

no complaints that the kids are eating liver - liver is a heck of a lot cheaper than buying green pastures FCLO!

Beef liver, I'm still getting the hang of cooking. Definitely giving grilling it a try when I spend some time traeger-sitting, oops I mean house sitting for my parents.

Chicken liver, whole other story. whenever I roast a chicken or two, I cook a little batch of pate for my mom and I to snack on while we cook and catch up. It's great spread on romaine leaves.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-18T17:00:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-2386>

I love lamb's liver, fried in butter with bacon (and sometimes with onion) - that's a perfect meal for me. Chicken livers are great, too. A great way of having them is as a stuffing for a grilled portabello mushroom, with an additional topping of mashed cauliflower (mixed with a little cream cheese if you're eating dairy, or a little bit of coconut milk if you're not!). Another nice way of cooking them is to place sheets of baking parchment on a tray, make a little pile of finely chopped onions and leeks, top with thinly sliced liver and dripping, season, wrap into parcels and bake.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-18T17:49:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-2394>

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-08T02:11:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-12553>

What I do when we slaughter a hog or lamb is to cut the liver into about 1-2c pieces. (My husband and I will each have about a cup for each meal.) I then freeze the liver pieces in individual bags and put all the individual bags in one big bag. I usually double bag organ meat so they will stay fresh longer in the freezer.

We grow our own hogs and most of our cattle but get our lamb from a local rancher that does not treat her pasture with anything but manure. Yes, liver can be toxic if the rancher sprays his pasture. I would ask the rancher about his practices before buying an animal.

Eat your liver twice a week. You will not overdue the fat solvable vitamins and your urine will have a rich yellow color.

### ginastarke — 2012-04-15T03:48:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-15196>

This is more on the primal side, since it has a little dairy and nightshades, but once I got the lamb liver of the grill, it tasted a bit stronger than expected and I made "gyros" with it: I sliced up the liver with cucumbers, onions and as little as I could manage red pepper and tomatoes , and made tzatziki with Greek yogurt, lemon juice, fresh mint garlic, cucumbers and pepper, which I also used as little as possible, and served in in romaine leaves. It was terrific!

### BeingVenus — 2012-04-16T19:54:29+0300

I used to love liver but for some strange reason after pregnancy I just couldn't stomach it unless its in a pate. Since I'm too lazy to make

PÃ¢tÃ© except for Jewish holidays I just make a frozen liver pills, which is supposedely how Sally Fallon gets her liver in every day. In fact, I just chopped up a 1.5lb lamb liver in to little baggies and put it in the freezer this morning.

"The best way to eat raw liver is to freeze it for 14 days to avoid any parasitic infection (unlikely but possible especially in those with impaired immune systems). When frozen, cut it into teaspoon sized pieces and put them into little coin bags. Keep them in your freezer and use one baggie at at time, chopping into little pills to swallow with a drink. The primary benefit of swallowing liver pills frozen, is you canâ€™t taste it. I take my liver pills with raw milk for the extra nourishment and so I donâ€™t burp up the taste."

### Lyndra — 2012-06-24T01:23:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-do-you-or-dont-you.185/post-33229>

> **Cavemam;33332 wrote:** Sorry to threadjack, buuuut -my backyard remodel is almost complete. Have big ol planting beds now. Lyndra - did you do asparagus here in CO? Any ideas on how to grow it?

Yes, asparagus grows quite well but it needs good drainage, adequate water, and seems to attract weeds like bees to honey. It is planted in mid-spring, usually bare-root plants (so it's too late for this year). You'll be able to harvest a few spears the second year but not too many, otherwise you'll deplete the roots too much. For details on growing, see [http://www.ext.colostate.edu/mg/gardennotes/719.html#asparagus](http://http://www.ext.colostate.edu/mg/gardennotes/719.html#asparagus). I used to volunteer as a master gardener, so ask me about plants .

# Thread: How long before the CT-mayhem ends?

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 208
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamorama — 2012-03-16T06:27:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-1303>

Has anyone here done CT for long enough to predict roughly how long the insane hormonal- and detox upheaval lasts? I feel like nothing is predictable anymore since starting CT. I know its a good thing, but I also would like to know when it stops, and I can only focus on benefits.

Amongs my less positive symthomps is bloating (probably due to excess estrogen being released from fat stores), insomnia (on and off), KP on my arms gone quite bad (completely gone until I started CT), tiredness, fatigue, sinus-issues and cold (similar to seasonal allergies) and energy levels more up and down rather than consistently good. My mood has generally been good after the first 2 weeks of CT, but what bothers me most is the unpredictable sleep at night.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-16T06:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-1306>

I'm not sure, but with my limited experience in helping people in my own clinic with this, I KNOW that it takes longer and is more unpredictable with women, both the Leptin Rx and CT. I don't mean this in a sexist way at all, it just is what it is. You ladies are more nuanced hormonally and if you've been through childbirth that changes your metabolism and immunology, too.

I suspect that the more weight one has to loose, and the more hormonally wacked-out things are, the longer and more bitter the road to OPTIMAL.

But hey, we all want to get there, so keep on keepin' on!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-16T15:59:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-1430>

I wish I knew the answer to this too. I'm not having sleep problems (knock on wood); the sleep has actually gotten better in that it seems to be deeper with no interruptions at night and I wake just before the alarm sounds. Occasionally have had to get up and pee during the night which was unusual before CT therapy began.

But I've had many ups and downs on mood and energy over the course of 2 weeks of CT therapy. Some days my limbs feel like weights are attached and I'm so incredibly fatigued that all I want to do is sit down and not move. Other times I feel like a normal human being with normal energy. I've not once had this burst of energy that some folks talk about, but I'll take 'normal' over weak and lethargic any day.

Had a lot of sinus drainage early on but as I enter week 3 this seems to be lessening. Thankfully!

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-03-16T17:02:29+0200

My cycle length is the same - 31 days- as normal but this months cycle has been the worst ever for me....since Hashi's diangnos, my periods have been extremely short and light (and completley nonexisitent if going through a thryoid flare up) with no cramping...THIS months was painful cramping and heavier bleeding...in addition to larger more tender breasts and general bloat...even a bit more constipation than usual...I have yet to start on my BHRT (still waiting on my Dr Dzugen plan!) but am hoping some progesterone will offset some of these unpleasant side affects...

Hang in there ladies -the end will be worth it I'm looking fowrad to getting this hormone stuff straightened up and to see some serious fat loss

### Birdy — 2012-03-16T18:13:53+0200

I continue to have digestive issues the day after a bath. I sort of have to keep near a bathroom, but I am not uncomfortable at all. I can tolerate a little bit of citrus, but most other vegetables don't interest me that much.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-03-16T19:07:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-1597>

I started my face dunks, cold showers, wearing less clothes in a cool house several weeks ago and have done cold baths with ice packs on tummy and thighs for 2 weeks now. Things seem to just be a roller coaster. Good one day, crappy the next. I'm having horrible headaches and I constantly feel dehydrated despite retaining water. I dropped ~4 lbs right after starting only to retain that much + more water within a few days after that. It seems to be slowly coming out.

Also brain fog has been coming and going as well as energy. Irritability/moodiness rears it's ugly head without warning.

I think the worst though has been a resurfacing of carb craving. I haven't wanted carbs since October, but now I'm really wanting grilled cheese and chocolate chip cookies.

### trudyap — 2012-03-17T01:38:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-1774>

I haven't even started with the CT yet. Don't know why but haven't been able to bring myself to start. Can anyone tell me how they started, progressed and have maintained it. And what to be looking for? I'm eating Paleo and need to lose about 50 pounds, have been battling the bulge all my life and @ 60 I want to get the victory once and for all. Regarding CT, I really liked the sound of the mattress that someone posted about on another thread. It sounded easier than taking cold baths!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-17T02:25:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-1795>

> **trudyap;1776 wrote:** I haven't even started with the CT yet. Don't know why but haven't been able to bring myself to start. Can anyone tell me how they started, progressed and have maintained it. And what to be looking for? I'm eating Paleo and need to lose about 50 pounds, have been battling the bulge all my life and @ 60 I want to get the victory once and for all. Regarding CT, I really liked the sound of the mattress that someone posted about on another thread. It sounded easier than taking cold baths!

I did a few spot-icing sessions using cold packs from the fridge on the outside of a thin tshirt and then directly against the skin after a session or two. I skipped the face dunks and tried cold showers - but even 20 seconds of that was absolute torture. I finally opted to jump into the bath by doing what is being called the "croak soak" - running warm water in a tub initially (maybe an inch or two), getting in, then adding cold water and letting the tub fill. This croak soak method really was a much easier transition than I expected and I have only just stopped using it as I start the 3rd week of CT. I highly recommend it if you're having trouble getting past the mental block of cold water. I didn't add any ice to the water at first and had no thermometer so don't know the temp in those first 2 weeks, but it was certainly cold enough to make my skin cherry red. Once I got a thermometer, it showed my skin temps around 58 degrees. It might not be optimal, but it wasn't bad considering that my body starts heating the cold water. Now I've begun adding ice blocks and will keep going.

Try the croak soak method if you're ready to jump in a bath, otherwise, face dunks. The baby step on a face dunk would be to put ice cubes in a bag and place the bag over your face. This is sometimes done therapeutically to calm anxiety patients. It might get you started so that you could then try face dunks, and gradually, the full bath. Good luck! You can do this.

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T17:38:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-2012>

Man, all this mayhem talk makes me nervous! ;-)

I'm one week in, and so far, the main thing I've noticed is better sleep ... deeper ... and I'm waking up feeling refreshed, which is a small miracle for me! I am a good girl as far as getting 8 hours; last July, I started getting my a\*\* to bed by 11:30 (I'm a terrible night owl) and my body went through all sorts of changes that took months to regulate (including major crashing during the day, brain fog, fatigue, etc). Thankfully, the worst is behind me (I think?), but I still have a hard time getting up in the morning. I wake up feeling very tired, not wanting to get out of bed, even after 8 hours of sleep.

This last week, though, I started sleeping so deeply during the night and awakening around 6 am feeling ... awake! Wow. I've SO been wanting to experience this ... heard about it, like it's the land of Oz or something ... I'm only a week in, so expect to experience some of the detox stuff as time goes on. I have very stubborn belly fat that I'd really like to lose, but I've made peace with it. We'll see if CT zaps it, as promised.  There are definitely some hormonal things really out of whack with me (I have low progesterone, endometriosis, low DHEA, and who-knows-what-else), and Jack seems to think that CT will fix all, so there's my commitment: Fix all the hormone issues. Anything else will be a bonus!

(In about 4 days, I'd graduated to the 45-minute cold baths, then 2 days later, added ice, including ice packs along the torso. This last time, I had bouts of shivering in the water, and for about an hour after. When I went to bed, though, I fell asleep HARD and FAST.)

There definitely is something to women having a different experience with CT and the Leptin Reset. I'm glad to see the conversations about it ... our bodies are just different hormonally and it's nice to be able to explore that together.

### Bee — 2012-03-17T18:53:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-2050>

This is all SO good to read. On the loose stools--wow. Did not make that connection at.all. until now. I was wondering why I can go from constipation (well, no urge to go for 2-3 days) to then having loose stools without much if any change in diet. CT makes sense.

When I was doing the face dunks, I started solid sleep right away. Now that I've started baths (though the water temp & therefore skin temp is not low enough) I'm back to the 3am insomnia. I know I am estrogen dominant and have low progesterone (8 day LP), but eating Paleo has moved ovulation from CD 18-21 to CD15 since January. In 7 years of charting I have never seen it that early. I'm noticing small changes in my LP length, too... considering restarting my NPC again next cycle and maybe picking up some of that metagenics estrofactors Glam mentioned.

What I'm really hoping to see is _any_ hint of a libido. Right now I have none. And the moods! Could be PMS... but I'm glad to know I'm not alone in this, and I'll stick it out.

### gr8tfl1@yahoo.com — 2012-03-18T13:54:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-2341>

> **trudyap;1776 wrote:** I haven't even started with the CT yet. Don't know why but haven't been able to bring myself to start. Can anyone tell me how they started, progressed and have maintained it. And what to be looking for? I'm eating Paleo and need to lose about 50 pounds, have been battling the bulge all my life and @ 60 I want to get the victory once and for all. Regarding CT, I really liked the sound of the mattress that someone posted about on another thread. It sounded easier than taking cold baths!

trudyap I am 50 and been doing CT for 2 weeks. I "hated" the cold when I started. I mean really..."hate."Most of my earliest memories are of being too cold. And, the only thing I ever allowed myself to use the term "hate" for was the cold.

So, here's what I did. I jumped right into the cold baths. I didn't have a thermometer until a few days. It turns out my bath water in my tub is messed up and comes out about 70 F. Pretty warm but it sure felt cold to me (now feels really warm to me). I slowly lowered myself into it when it was about 3 inches deep. Felt fabulous once I got in. And, I got use to it right away. Did that for about 3 days them got a thermometer and ice bags and do 55-50F for 45-60 minutes. It's FABULOUS!!! It feels great. For the first week or more there's lots of shivering after so I wouldn't recommend it at night right away. Too hard to sleep while shivering.

Overall, I recommend getting into tub and then starting the water. I now use my son's tub which starts out at 60F.

You can do it. I'm into week 3 and I no longer even dislike the cold. Actually, I'm feeling a bit warm right now!

Nothing left to hate in the world. How awesome is that!!!!!!

### AdrianaDr. Radler-Pohl — 2012-03-18T17:49:38+0200

### Destiny — 2012-03-18T18:20:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-2400>

> **trudyap;1776 wrote:** I haven't even started with the CT yet. Don't know why but haven't been able to bring myself to start. Regarding CT, I really liked the sound of the mattress that someone posted about on another thread. It sounded easier than taking cold baths!

What worked for me was to start with face dunks and my arms submerged in the bathroom sink. After a few days, I started taking bath with warmer water and gradually was adding colder water. I went from 78 degrees to 54 degrees in a couple of weeks. Some days if I don't feel alright, I go a few degrees up. Just listen to your body and do it !

### Adria — 2012-03-19T00:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-2482>

I'm on week 4 of CT. Taking it slow, the first week was face dunks and cool water after my hot shower. After the 2nd week I lost 5#, seems to be all in the thighs and butt. Even DH commented that my pants look baggy. I've been keto-paleo for 3yrs and VLC for over 20 yrs so I've been at normal weight for long time. Was shocked at the 5#. Now I'm up to cold baths 55 degrees for 10 min. and at night I use cold packs. Face dunks in the morning. Theres something changing but I can't put a finger on it, just feeling so good that I'm hooked. I had hysterectomy so I don't know how this is affecting my remaining ovary or my cycles. No signs of pre-menopause symtoms yet, I'm 49yo.

### cjb — 2012-03-19T01:47:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-2514>

Hi everyone - new to posting but I was following the monster thread on MDA. I tried CT in the tub a few weeks ago and couldn't handle it on my feet so I did spot CT on my belly. I followed suggestions from others and ordered a pair of the scuba socks and tried it this morning. Between that and getting an inflatable pillow so I could lean back in the tub did it for me. I was able to stay in for 45 minutes when my previous experience was nail biting, teeth grinding for 30 minutes. So, I'm going to do the tub as much as possible and supplement with the spot CT in the evenings. If I did the tub at night I think it would keep me awake.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-03-19T04:18:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-2569>

interesting to hear everyone's experiences. I am praying CT brings back TOM for me...I haven't had TOM since I turned 40 last August. If Letpin Reset and CT bring it back, that will be something. My gyn told me it's menopause. I could cry but any way....I about 10 days in to CT, 1 week of actually getting in the tub. Tonight I went 45 minutes relatively OK. That was a biggie for me. I do spot icing in the morning and baths at night. I haven't had any crazy hormone stuff yet. But I do feel like a burning from time to time after CT. But I'm cold for a long time too. I also notice I have to pee a lot even though I wouldn't say I am drinking more. Other days, I am so thirsty and nothing quenches it for me. Luckily I sleep pretty well but I do feel really tired a lot. The bloating does drive me up a tree...for me, I am wondering if it is the coconut oil? Could the bloat be because it's working on the leaky gut? For the people who commented about their muscles feeling tired and heavy, I think I remember reading that this happens when your muscles are converting from being sugar burners to fat burner, so that's a good thing! Keep going everyone...I know it'll pay off. NITE

### Sherry — 2012-03-19T21:19:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-2898>

I'm wondering if anyone is having up and down reactions while in the cold tub. I started CT about a month ago. I graduated to the baths about three weeks ago. My tap water is extremely warm (75) so the first few soaks were about 60 degrees. I have finally gotten to the point in the last 10 days or so where I have enough ice to get it down to between 50 - 55. What I've been experiencing (even with the higher temperatures) is that one day I can get in that water and have no problem making it 45 minutes. It doesn't even feel cold after the first few minutes. The next day I can start shivering the minute I sink myself down and it won't stop. It takes every ounce of willpower I have to stay in for 30 min. And then I can't stop shivering for hours afterwards. I'm trying to do a bath every day but sometimes I have to skip a day. I know that detox symptoms can be widely varied and I'm wondering if the reaction to the baths is the same? Am I not cold adapted yet? Anyone else experiencing this?

Yesterday my bath was easier to get into, and then this morning I had a harder time getting in. My first thought was (this could be totally rediculous) that last night we had chicken drumsticks, and I ate the skin. We're fast forwarding the process, and the toxins are bailing out. (I also read an mainstream medical article about a year ago about how weight loss ALWAYS means toxins being released, so much so that doctors must balance the good of the weight loss with the diseases that could be caused by the circulating toxins. That last part made no sense to me, b/c they showed no evidence that the toxins could cause anything but passing symptoms, not actual disease)
>
>
>
>
> > **Glamorama;1326 wrote: Good to know. So maybe another month of feeling weird. Its like constantly being in-between feeling AWESOME and CRAP. Never a happy middle.
> >

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-20T21:20:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-3446>

> **Emilie;2356 wrote:** What's LPD? I'm trying to make an abbreviation glossary (as all these abbrev's are driving me crazy!)
>
>
>
> Also, my libido is definitely coming back. Too bad, though, as I'm a very busy single mom! Haven't been thinking much about a man. Hmmmmm ;-)

Emilie LPD is luteal phase defect. If you chart your cycle using NFP (natural family planning)-sympto thermal/Billings/Marquette or use another FAM (fertility awareness method) , the third phase of your cycle is post ovulation where the corpus luteum is ruling the road. If the # of days from ovulation to menses is short, it is said you have a Luteal Phase Defect. LPD usually means fertility issues. The number may vary depending on who you read, but 12 days is a common marker.

And for those of us who chart as a means of family planning, you definitely want as long a luteal phase as possible, because that is the major phase for relations while avoiding pregnancy. (The other being Phase I which is from TOTM to the appearance of fertile mucus)

### differentstory — 2012-03-21T17:49:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-3912>

Thank to you guys, I have had some success the past two days! I did the "croak soak" the past two nights with good luck.

I started out 3 weeks ago getting in to a full bathtub of water. Big mistake. I would completely freeze and the water was only 82. Did that twice and was completely miserable both times.

Then switched to icing which was okay, but a bit of a PITA.

Then two nights ago, I filled the bath with an inch of water. The temp was probably 85. Then I got in and turned on only the cold water. I was sitting up with my legs crossed (indian-style. . . or is it native-american-style these days??). I put in only about 6 inches of water. Enough to cover my full legs when straightening them out in front of me. The water was 72 degrees this way. Yay! Not miserable in the least.

Last night, I went further. Knocked the temp down 10 degrees and increased the water level 4-6 inches so now the water is up to the middle of my stomach. Again, not miserable. Although, I should say that I am not able to move because if the water splashes on my upper-body, I am then freezing cold with goosebumps! HA!

I also make sure I have a very compelling book in hand that keeps my attention.

My goal is to keep increasing the water line until I get my stomach covered. Once I can do that successfully and feel comfortable, I'm going to go for total submersion. That's my plan today anyways!

For those of you who are having success, how much of your body do you submerge in the water?

I am struggling getting my shoulders in the water. I get so cold when I do that.

Last thing, I am finding that I sleep much more when I am CT'ing (not good). I am like clockwork. I sleep almost exactly 9 hours a night (although I wish I slept 6-7 hours!). The past two nights, though, I am sleeping 10-11 hours a night.

So happy this thread is here! It makes me feel better!

Final warning: on Friday night, I was icing and got frostbite on my abdomen. I was using ziploc gallon-size freezer bags filled with water that had been frozen. I was having success on my stomach, but the bag slipped down to my abdomen and I didn't notice. It did burn for a few minutes and I thought I adjusted it because the pain stopped. Really, I think I just went numb. 10-15 later, I removed the bag and felt my abdomen/stomach. I had this 2"x4" patch that felt like a piece of paper that someone had poured glue over and it dried. I had no sensation there either. It was the weirdest thing and totally made me FREAK OUT. Good news is that feeling came back within 60 seconds . Today, 5 days later, this area is red, stings a bit, and is itchy. That's it. It did make me give up icing though!

Now, looking back, I realize I was doing icing hoping it would make my transition to the bath easier. I have found, though, that the method I am doing now (with the baths) has been the easiest out of anything (cold showers - horrendous!, icing, face dunks).

I've taking krill oil, cod liver oil, fish oil for years and years. I eat salmon every week and grass-fed beef, those are my main go-to foods, eggs, coconut oil, butter, raw cacao, drink tea. I was told I had some estrogen dominance. I see you write excess estrogen. Is that what you mean?

What is the surface temperature of the ice pack? I'm wondering if it is too cold? I was using a freezer bag full of water (mixed with alcohol) and it was far colder than the 55-degree CT bath. My guess is you are going too cold too fast?

### Claudia — 2012-03-21T18:20:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-3920>

> **differentstory;3937 wrote:** Thank to you guys, I have had some success the past two days! I did the "croak soak" the past two nights with good luck.
>
>
>
> I started out 3 weeks ago getting in to a full bathtub of water. Big mistake. I would completely freeze and the water was only 82. Did that twice and was completely miserable both times.
>
> Then switched to icing which was okay, but a bit of a PITA.
>
> Then two nights ago, I filled the bath with an inch of water. The temp was probably 85. Then I got in and turned on only the cold water. I was sitting up with my legs crossed (indian-style. . . or is it native-american-style these days??). I put in only about 6 inches of water. Enough to cover my full legs when straightening them out in front of me. The water was 72 degrees this way. Yay! Not miserable in the least.
>
>
>
> Last night, I went further. Knocked the temp down 10 degrees and increased the water level 4-6 inches so now the water is up to the middle of my stomach. Again, not miserable. Although, I should say that I am not able to move because if the water splashes on my upper-body, I am then freezing cold with goosebumps! HA!
>
>
>
> I also make sure I have a very compelling book in hand that keeps my attention.
>
>
>
> My goal is to keep increasing the water line until I get my stomach covered. Once I can do that successfully and feel comfortable, I'm going to go for total submersion. That's my plan today anyways!
>
>
>
> For those of you who are having success, how much of your body do you submerge in the water?
>
>
>
> I am struggling getting my shoulders in the water. I get so cold when I do that.
>
>
>
> Last thing, I am finding that I sleep much more when I am CT'ing (not good). I am like clockwork. I sleep almost exactly 9 hours a night (although I wish I slept 6-7 hours!). The past two nights, though, I am sleeping 10-11 hours a night.
>
>
>
> So happy this thread is here! It makes me feel better!
>
>
>
> Final warning: on Friday night, I was icing and got frostbite on my abdomen. I was using ziploc gallon-size freezer bags filled with water that had been frozen. I was having success on my stomach, but the bag slipped down to my abdomen and I didn't notice. It did burn for a few minutes and I thought I adjusted it because the pain stopped. Really, I think I just went numb. 10-15 later, I removed the bag and felt my abdomen/stomach. I had this 2"x4" patch that felt like a piece of paper that someone had poured glue over and it dried. I had no sensation there either. It was the weirdest thing and totally made me FREAK OUT. Good news is that feeling came back within 60 seconds . Today, 5 days later, this area is red, stings a bit, and is itchy. That's it. It did make me give up icing though!
>
>
>
> Now, looking back, I realize I was doing icing hoping it would make my transition to the bath easier. I have found, though, that the method I am doing now (with the baths) has been the easiest out of anything (cold showers - horrendous!, icing, face dunks).

Sounds like cold urticaria (hives), which is what has been happening with me but only with ice and not with cold showers or baths. I am not fully submerged in the cold water, my tub is too shallow, I have to keep moving to make sure I have enough water on me and no my shoulders are not in and my arms are holding the book (just started a new book and it's not compelling enough to hold my attention)...tap water is about 55 degrees now and I would like to get it colder but scared of using ice. I agree the cold baths are easiest for me, too, but not pleasant at all.

I have a new area on my tummy of red, sore hives from a gel pack I tried for maybe 5 minutes, maybe 10, 2 days ago. Back to cold baths.

### Claudia — 2012-03-21T18:23:36+0200

Yes, probably. I'm not sure what the temp is of the gel pack. The last time I got hives it was from ice cubes in a ziplock bag and I had ice cube hives...the outline of the cube on my skin. I guess the freezer bag mixed with water and alcohol won't be a good idea for me. I will stick to baths. I am in NY and I live near the beach and I"m wondering about going swimming. I love to swim and do it all summer and a little in the fall, too. I don't know the temp of the water but I might check it...it's gotta be much colder than 50 though right now. I am looking at some spring bathing, though, for sure. I love to swim and miss it normally, but it will be strange to be all alone in the water.

### differentstory — 2012-03-21T18:38:43+0200

Okay, being new to ALLLLLL of this, this may be a stupid question, but I just have to ask. . .

I consider this CT stuff to be a bit like running a marathon. We are making our bodies work HARD! And when I used to diet and exercise hard, my weight would completely stall because I was pushing myself into starvation mode. Does starvation mode apply when you're doing Paleo/Leptin Reset??? Could it be that we are not eating enough for such a rigorous workout??

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-21T18:47:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-3935>

> **MartiD;3920 wrote:** I am so glad I am not the only one who craves the CB! I had a crazy morning with taking a car into be repaired, getting kids to school and trying to get to work on time but I took an extra fast cold shower and then got in the cold tub for at least 15 minutes. I just have a better day when I do a CB. I had to travel for a couple of weekend earlier this month and really missed them. We leave for Mexico on Sunday for a week and I am sure the tap water at the hotel will very warm and the ocean and pool will be warm. I hope I don't lose all my cold adaptation during the week. I do plan on getting my Vit D levels up while on the beach though!

Are you staying at a hotel? Maybe they'll have an ice machine. It might take some effort, but I bet you could get a CB together that way.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-21T18:56:15+0200

I think the answer to this question is in the CT blog series. I would suggest reading the series again. I just finished reading the series for a 2nd time, and I learned so much more. I'm sure I'll learn even more reading it a third time. I printed them out, and I now read the CT series while soaking in the cold tub.

My take is that since we are working with an entirely different metabolic pathway, all bets are off regarding how we used to think about and experience weightloss and energy balance.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-22T01:48:26+0200

> GLam, once again you fast-tracked a hack. 3 baths and you're adapted! BTW, I don't know if this is an individual difference or what, but I've always noticed little or no bleeding when in water during TOTM. Maybe it's a less-gravity + water pressure thing, IDK, but I had no trouble in the bath, even on the heavy days.

5 baths and 31 spot-CT sessions and I'm hacked... and did I mention the mayhem its been to get there? LOL

I notice I don't bleed for a couple of hours if I spot-CT, I've actually also had full nights of no bleeding on Chart day 2, which is unreal! I just don't like the idea of sitting in a bath rishing grossness. And I hate tampons.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-22T01:57:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-4143>

> **Claudia;3885 wrote:** That sounds pretty good. Craving CT seems beyond my comprehension even though I've been doing it for a few weeks. I was told I had some estrogen dominance. I see you write excess estrogen. Is that what you mean?
>
> Are you doing the Leptin reset at the same time? What do you eat? Just trying to get a handle on your success with CT and how I can be successful, too. Thanks.

Cludia, I am estrogen dominant. For a while ther eit was quite horrific, the physical, but mostly mental changes I went through after Christmas really freaked me out. I went from happy and easy-going to a mental wreck, feeling near suicidal some days, and always down, angry and depressed. I was snappy, teary, suffered insomnia and tender breasts, bloating and my cycles got longer and longer. A blood test revealed high amounts of E2. Doc threw me a few pointers, and I managed to figure out what happend and hwo I got to that place healthwise. Basically I have always been higher in estrogen, its a family-thing. But since I've been burning off so much fat, it got to a point where my sluggish liver simply couldn't keep up with metabolizing all the extra estrogen from my fatty tissues, so all those hormones were floating around in my system instead. The blood test also revealed a fatty liver. I saw my naturopath, who put me on Metagenics Estrofactors for 3 weeks, and in that time I pretty much became myself again. The last 48 hours I've had yet another major shift in my physical wellbeing, in the right direction. I can tell my liver is actually working again, and my hormones are more balanced.

I did the reset from July-late August 2011. I'm now doing post-Rx protocol. Only done CT for a month and a bit... I eat according to fall, so my carbs are down from 6-80 grams daily to 30-50 grams now depending on the day. I get roughly 80-120 grams protein in a day (I am trying to build muscle, hence the high amount). I eat CO off a spoon (ca 2-3 tbs daily) and half a can of coconut milk daily.

I don't do dairy but I do 85 percent chcolate still... will stop this as it gets cooler and darker here. I also eat lots of fish and grass-fed beef. Never eat starchy carbs, I get my carbs from pure coconut water, a very occassional in-season piece of fruit, but mostly I fuel myself with protein and fats.

### angieonthegogo — 2012-03-22T02:32:09+0200

Does anyone think that the compression shirts will help a lot to move everything out faster? I am thinking of getting a tank style and just wearing it all day. Apparently when you have liposuction you have to wear compression garments after. I know it is for the swelling but maybe it helps move everything along? I think Dr. K. briefly mentioned something like that when he said he was wearing quite a tight one. I would give anything to have all this blubber gone!!!

I have some patches of hives too. I couldn't figure out where the H they came from but now that you all are talking about them it makes sense. I woke up the following morning after a long CT bath session and little sleep. They itched and slightly hurt (from itching I'm sure) but are almost gone now. Will see if they come back after tonights session. Glad I'm not going mad from all of this cold exposure!

My face and arms have totally broken out in acne and blemishes too. This is unlike me. I can't wait for this hormone mayhem to be over! Have a feeling it is going to get worse before it gets better but I'm full steam ahead.

ROUGH NIGHT last night! I am in the full throes of hormones right now. I ended up doing CT yesterday in the afternoon and had to take a nap afterwards because I was so tired. Water was 62 degrees and I was able to fill the bathtub all the way up to right under by bra (left it on to keep the girls out of it). Stayed in the bath an hour. So I really pushed myself thanks to others on the forum. Yay, me.

My sleep, though, was bad. . .filled with constant dreaming and I kept waking up. . .I kept having the craziest dreams about learning that other people were finding that red bell peppers make you lose weight.  I was also INCREDIBLY grumpy last night.

### Adriana — 2012-03-22T21:18:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-4609>

I've been doing CT about a week. Within days I popped a nasty zit on my chin, totally uncommon for me except when I supplemented with DHEA. Then today's delightfull surprise...How about vaginal bleeding 8 years post menopause!!! Aaargh, sent me raiding my daughter's bathroom for tampons left when she went off to college. My research says estrogen has been liberated, messing up my hormonal status and sloughing of the vaginal lining.

My quandry - I'm scheduled to have my quarterly BHRT status tested in a month. Will this craziness be flushed out of my system for my doc to get a good reading or am I wasting my time and should postpone testing for another month?

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-22T22:57:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-4644>

Of course, I didn't wear gloves or socks this time, and it was LONG after my last meal, so it's probably all my fault, lol.

OTOH, I broke a long-standing weight barrier, so YIPPEE!!

### Lyndra — 2012-03-23T00:17:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-4675>

I seem to be going downhill now after 14 days of cold baths. Sleep has been bad for months but now is getting worse with hot flashes waking me every 1-2 hours. I went to the gym yesterday and strength was down substantially from last week. I had trouble completing an easy bike ride today.

Oddest thing - physically, I am not hungry between meals but mentally, I just want to eat. I don't have cravings per se, nothing sounds interesting, but my head is telling me I should be eating. Maybe the brain recognizes that the CT is changing WAT into BAT and thinks I need to refuel, but the body is cold adapting and doesn't want food. Strange.

At least I am feeling more comfortable in the tub, only getting cold after about 40 minutes and warming up in about 20 minutes afterwards. Getting through this adaptation is tough.

Lyndra, sorry to hear you feel like you are going downhill, but I'm sure things are getting worked out in there for the best! Toxins must go out, right? Maybe your body is dumping estrogen and that's what's kicking up the hot flashes. I keep reading, increase your B vitamins for CT symptoms.

I am having similar issue with the wanting to eat thing. It's kind of like I feel like something is missing...my brain jumps in and tries to tell me that it is greek yogurt or something...LOL. Mamagrok just mentioned to someone else that if you aren't digesting and absorbing nutrients you can feel hungry. I take DGL but I'm going to try to add Betaine HCL. I also think that CT rewires the brain as well as everything else and sometimes it goes back to old patterns to un-do them. Or something.

Wish I was feeling more comfortable in the tub, that's for sure. 40 minutes is pretty hard for me and it takes me hours to warm up, so I think you're doing pretty great!

### Claudia — 2012-03-23T03:01:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-4775>

> **Glamorama;4169 wrote:** Cludia, I am estrogen dominant. For a while ther eit was quite horrific, the physical, but mostly mental changes I went through after Christmas really freaked me out. I went from happy and easy-going to a mental wreck, feeling near suicidal some days, and always down, angry and depressed. I was snappy, teary, suffered insomnia and tender breasts, bloating and my cycles got longer and longer. A blood test revealed high amounts of E2. Doc threw me a few pointers, and I managed to figure out what happend and hwo I got to that place healthwise. Basically I have always been higher in estrogen, its a family-thing. But since I've been burning off so much fat, it got to a point where my sluggish liver simply couldn't keep up with metabolizing all the extra estrogen from my fatty tissues, so all those hormones were floating around in my system instead. The blood test also revealed a fatty liver. I saw my naturopath, who put me on Metagenics Estrofactors for 3 weeks, and in that time I pretty much became myself again. The last 48 hours I've had yet another major shift in my physical wellbeing, in the right direction. I can tell my liver is actually working again, and my hormones are more balanced.
>
>
>
> I did the reset from July-late August 2011. I'm now doing post-Rx protocol. Only done CT for a month and a bit... I eat according to fall, so my carbs are down from 6-80 grams daily to 30-50 grams now depending on the day. I get roughly 80-120 grams protein in a day (I am trying to build muscle, hence the high amount). I eat CO off a spoon (ca 2-3 tbs daily) and half a can of coconut milk daily.
>
> I don't do dairy but I do 85 percent chcolate still... will stop this as it gets cooler and darker here. I also eat lots of fish and grass-fed beef. Never eat starchy carbs, I get my carbs from pure coconut water, a very occassional in-season piece of fruit, but mostly I fuel myself with protein and fats.

Our diets are very similar but I eat a lot less carbs even in summer...now I know from Dr. K to eat with the season so I'll try a little fruit when it warms up here...we are headed toward summer in NY while you are headed toward winter. I can barely get to 30g carbs if I increase veggies. I started the Leptin reset in November and did it for 5 weeks with lots of the signs of LS but definitely not all of them (zero libido) and Dr. K suggested I was LS and should move on to post-Leptin reset, which I have been doing with some higher BAB days. I don't think I am LS though and I hope the CT helps. And I think my estrogen dominance was only slight about 5 years ago when I last tested and is probably worse now. I am so thankful for your post. I feel like a complete crazy woman and have been for awhile and what you describe is how I have been feeling: angry, depressed, I snap at the kids for the slightest thing, just horrible, I am outta my mind (and from NY . I had no idea this could be ED. Where do I get the Metagenics Estrofactors? Are you still taking it? What is it? It really fixed the problem or are you hoping CT and Leptin will help even more? I love 85% chocolate, but 90% is great, too, and I recently tried 99% Lindt (not bad). I make my own with coconut oil, raw cacao, and stevia (melt it, mix, and freeze into chocolate). I also mostly grass-fed beef and raw salmon, some veggies, raw butter, coconut oil, eggs.

I am mess emotionally lately. Has anyone felt CT making them moodier, more emotional?

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-03-23T14:29:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-4916>

I don't have any major symptoms yet but I am realizing that I have a lot of warm adapting to un-do. I have lived most of my life in snowy cold climate but from the ages of 11-14 we lived in Alabama. Whenever anyone asks me if I ski or if I like the snow, I always tell them that living in Alabama for three years ruined me for the cold. Little did I know how true that is. I also am very frugal (I work for a non-profit and have to be) and keep my heat very low (55-60) but to make up for it, I bundle up and have used an electric blanket at night for years. I noticed that every year, I turn the blanket up higher and higher and always thought they kept “wearing out.” My parents were visiting and I let them use the blanket and my mom told me it was very warm, which I thought was weird because it seemed to barely heat up for me. Well, after reading CT 7, I realized that the electric blanket has NOT been good at all…all these years trying to get warm, I have in effect made myself colder! It seems like lately I keep finding out I’ve been doing everything backward – cutting out fat to lose weight, using heat to stay warm when I needed to eat fat to lose weight and get cold to be warm!

So, I keep going with spot CT in the morning and cold baths at night. Last night I could feel the cold coming off my skin and hitting my clothing and was so chilly even though the air in my room is warm. I just can’t wait until I start radiating heat (this summer I’ll probably eat those words-LOL).

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-23T14:30:23+0200

fwiw... I've slowly been making bath time for the kids cold water.... start 'em early!!!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-23T15:10:26+0200

### KetoWarrior — 2012-03-23T16:17:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-4967>

> **Clabbergirl;4963 wrote:** Forgive me if I've missed this, but feeling hot all the time and hotter to everyone else is a sign of being cold adapted? I've seen that several people are experiencing this now. All I can think of is when I was on BC pills a couple of years ago and perimenopausal - I was hotter than everyone all the time and didn't even know what cold adapted was. But this being hot is one of the signs (provided a person is doing CT of course)?
> I'm guessing yes. If you read _[Adventures in Diet](http://http://www.biblelife.org/stefansson1.htm)_ by Vilhjalmur Stefansson you'll notice he remarks about how the Inuit sleep naked in 60-degree F rooms at night and nobody is cold.
>
>
>
> The temperature at night was round 60\*F. The ventilation was excellent through cold air coming up slowly from below by way of a trap door that was never closed and the heated air going out by a ventilator in the roof.
>
>
>
> Everyone slept completely naked - no pajama or night shirts. We used cotton or woolen blankets which had been obtained from the whalers and from the Hudson's Bay Company.

It's just a guess, but what I think is happening is we are producing more heat in response to alpha-andrenergic stimulation (adrenaline) as our BAT and BRITE are coming on-line. At work the other day after dealing with a tense situation I noticed I got so hot that I wanted to stick my head in a freezer. It wasn't like I was sweating-hot either. It was different. Rather it felt like I was cooking in the hot sun even though I was indoors in 72 degrees F. I'm a male and at 39 slightly too young for andropause.

>
> It's just a guess, but what I think is happening is we are producing more heat in response to alpha-andrenergic stimulation (adrenaline) as our BAT and BRITE are coming on-line. At work the other day after dealing with a tense situation I noticed I got so hot that I wanted to stick my head in a freezer. It wasn't like I was sweating-hot either. It was different. Rather it felt like I was cooking in the hot sun even though I was indoors in 72 degrees F. I'm a male and at 39 slightly too young for andropause.

Thanks for this info and your perspective on it. I'm looking forward to cold adaptation, but not looking forward to the 99+ degrees temps and 100 percent humidity prone to this area starting in about 2 months.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-23T18:05:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5019>

> **Lexi;4845 wrote:** has anyone tried DIM: [http://primev.com/dim.aspx](http://http://primev.com/dim.aspx)

I do take DIM. Testing I did in December showed I have low levels of estrogens (E1, E2, E3) but high levels of 4-hydroxyestrone which means I am converting too much E1. I am also taking methylation products because of poor conversion of 4-hydroxestrone to a less harmful metabolite. I am still having hot flashes but when I checked with the doc, was told it could take 6 months for things to stabilize.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-23T18:13:48+0200

I am still having hot flashes but when I checked with the doc, was told it could take 6 months for things to stabilize.

How much DIM are you taking? I've just purchased some and the instructions indicate 1 to 4 tablets daily with no other guidelines. Was curious what dosage others are using.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-23T19:42:53+0200

But this being hot is one of the signs (provided a person is doing CT of course)?

you're on your way...you're also probably starting to see some LS signs as well....

### Claudia — 2012-03-23T20:18:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5085>

> **Glamorama;4859 wrote:** Ask the doc about Estrofactor Supplement. Rosemary is also good for liver-cleaning.
>
>
>
> My weight-distribution is typical for a person with ED and insulin-resistance (which I had before the reset). I have a belly, big boobs and bigger upper arms. Also decent hips/thighs. I know from my case, my liver wasn't able to clear all the estrogen that was released from fatty tissues because it was fatty. It became that way thanks to a diet high in sugar and carbs. As for my estrogen, I think thats partly genetics, and partly the food I ate growing up. My soy-loving tofu-eating decade of being a vegetarian surely didn't help either.

Sounds like me. I never had a belly before the babies, but I do know, and big boobs and I have fat on my arms and hips and thighs. I am only about 8-10 lbs overweight. Interesting. I was never a vegetarian, but yeah, that wouldn't help at all. We've banned soy for the last 10 years, but did go through some tofu and soy milk experimentation before we knew better.

### Claudia — 2012-03-23T20:21:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5087>

I still have hives on my tummy. I took a cold bath last night for 25 minutes but not fully submerged so my belly was not in the cold water at all and it was only 59 degrees. It was so easy compared to 53 degrees and more submerged. Should I keep going or take it even slower because of the hives not being gone? I tend to push through things too fast sometimes because I can take the pain (after going through childbirth, hives are nothing).

### Lyndra — 2012-03-23T21:42:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5125>

> **Clabbergirl;5060 wrote:** How much DIM are you taking? I've just purchased some and the instructions indicate 1 to 4 tablets daily with no other guidelines. Was curious what dosage others are using.

Doc recommended I take 150 mg twice per day. YMMV of course. I take it (and almost all supplements) in the AM just in case they have any energizing effect that might worsen my sleep. Only exceptions are B complex, progesterone & magnesium at bedtime.

### differentstory — 2012-03-23T22:06:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5133>

Just came on this thread to post my experience today and I see you guys are already talking about it! We are just wanting to turn our air conditioner on, but something is wrong with it, so DH turned on several fans in the house and I have been DYING! This morning was the worst. He was leaving and he said "Oh do you want me to turn the fan off?" And I was like "HECK NO, I am boiling!! Aren't you??" And he looked at me crazy and said "No, I'm fine." Then I checked the thermometer and it was 73!!!! Normally, I would be cool at 73 but today I was sweating! Not looking forward to summer with being cold-adapted.

### Lexi — 2012-03-24T00:29:20+0200

someone somewhere said they started gaining on progesterone.... i thought prog. was supposed to keep estrogen in check and estrogen is the stuff making us fat.... confused.... and i just started on prog so would really like to know. thanks.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-03-24T01:16:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5215>

OMG, today I am absolutely dead tired and starving. I just posted that I haven't been feeling the effects of CT too strong and whamo. All week I have been so tired and going to bed at 9pm. Yesterday I felt GREAT and got into stuff at home so bedtime was 10pm...well, today I am paying for it. Apparently CT and the Leptin Reset say my bedtime is 9pm!

Also I was STARVING today. I hope to goodness it is not the freaking estradiol pills my doc put me on. I had 1/2 pound of stew meat for BAB and I thought that was a large amount of protein. But I was hungry for lunch by noon and had fish and salad. I also was pretty light on the CO for breakfast and lunch. Big mistake. By dinner I was ravenous. I cooked a 1 pound steak and ate the whole thing plus some zucchini cooked in CO and the equivalent of at least two tablespoons of CO with cocoa powder and stevia (trying to give up the stevia but not there yet). And I seriously feel like I could have ate a whole other steak if I had one. CT early and bed for me!

Speak of CT - I think I overdid the spot CT this morning. I put the ice pack directly on my skin - back and front torso. My belly has a huge pink mark on it that has not gone away still more than 12 hours later. It looks like a wine stain birthmark...yikes? What do you think - burn mark or O6 reaction? My back is fine. My belly of course it the "fat" part of me.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-24T11:39:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5355>

Wasn't able to spot CT or have a cold bath yesterday (I really missed it), but did find that I was way too hot in a house I've always thought of as being cold! I also had a deep ache in my stomach, like I'd overdone a workout and the muscles were sore. My biggest problem, though, was visiting my parents-in-law for lunch and having to eat everything. They did make a supreme effort (and they always use real ingredients), but it was simply not politic to refuse the mashed potato (extremely small helping), and stewed plums with baked custard topping (overly sweet, and extremely small helping). I only hope I haven't killed the benefits of CT with that .... Had horrible heartburn all night, and dreadful milk chocolate cravings, too ....

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-24T12:17:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5357>

> **oliviascotland;5388 wrote:** My biggest problem, though, was visiting my parents-in-law for lunch and having to eat everything. They did make a supreme effort (and they always use real ingredients), but it was simply not politic to refuse the mashed potato (extremely small helping), and stewed plums with baked custard topping (overly sweet, and extremely small helping). I only hope I haven't killed the benefits of CT with that .... Had horrible heartburn all night, and dreadful milk chocolate cravings, too ....

I'm treating this as a cautionary tale. We are visiting my parents today, and they'll be serving conventionally-raised, grain-fed chicken, marinaded in a rancid, high-O6 salad dressing. I'm already bringing food for my son, mostly because he doesn't like chicken. The thing is they think what I'm doing with CT is a little out there, so it makes it a bit more difficult to go against the grain. But I will, 'cause part of me is doing this for them. I keep praying all of my family (and friends) will see the amazing changes in my life and health, and be inspired to make similar changes.

### vkiernan — 2012-03-24T14:31:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5368>

> **Evo Mama;5390 wrote:** I'm treating this as a cautionary tale. We are visiting my parents today, and they'll be serving conventionally-raised, grain-fed chicken, marinaded in a rancid, high-O6 salad dressing. I'm already bringing food for my son, mostly because he doesn't like chicken. The thing is they think what I'm doing with CT is a little out there, so it makes it a bit more difficult to go against the grain. But I will, 'cause part of me is doing this for them. I keep praying all of my family (and friends) will see the amazing changes in my life and health, and be inspired to make similar changes.

I hear ya. Very tough when family is involved especially the dh's side. They all eat SAD and think they are doing themselves a hugh favor. They will cook pork loins and dump bottles of processed crap all over it. Ewwww! I have had to tell them I cannot eat certain things now due to my health. I have Candida so for now it is a good excuse. Of course, I am hoping it will go away soon. But, it is still hard. Now I bring food I can eat even if it is only venison sausage. That way I get some protein and they usually have some veggie I can eat. It works. Make a plan and stick to it for you! It is "your" health. Just keep smiling and reminding yourself that the food they are eating got you to this point in the first place. I even bring my own salad dressings to restaurants now cuz I don't want to eat that disgusting high-06 dressing you are talking about. Good luck to you.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-24T16:43:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5417>

Hasten to add that my 84 y-o father-in-law refuses to cook with oil, just butter or lard, and apart from the potato and pudding (stewed plums - done in water and sugar, and home-made baked custard with egg yolks, cream and sugar), everything was great - no grains, baked tomatoes with braised lettuce topped with parmesan as a starter, roast beef, leeks and courgettes/zucchini and potatoes (mashed with double/heavy cream and butter) for a main course, and plums topped with baked custard for pudding. Everything local, everything organic. Just the carbs and the sugar that worry me, not the content of any dodgy oils! The only reason I ate every scrap I was given was due to the supreme effort he'd made as well as a history of family problems ...

Back on track today (although up another 2lbs, to be expected and probably water weight), and enjoyed my cold shower this morning, and am off for a nice cold soak in a minute, then walking the dogs in shorts and a tee-shirt as we have unseasonally hot (by Scottish standards  ) weather!

My infra-red thermometer arrived this morning, so it will be interesting to see skin temperature when I get out of the bath. My 11-year-old daughter, who's just back from boarding school for the holidays, thinks I've gone mad! However, my 15-year-old has started CT at school, and is really looking forward to optimising this when she gets home and can eat properly.

### differentstory — 2012-03-24T19:02:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5472>

I'm wondering if we are all turning into Michael Phelps (with the 12,000 calorie diet) and we don't know it????

My experience:

Similar to some of you, I was COMPLETELY exhausted yesterday!

I've really been pushing it with CT this week.

I did 4 straight days of 60-minute sessions in 60 degree CT baths. Everyday I was taking it further - colder water, more depth and more minutes.

Felt good and warm the first 3 days in the bath.

Last night, not so much. I was very tired before doing CT, but I pushed myself to do my session anyways and I was pretty cold in the water. I wonder if my body was almost too tired to regulate my temperature? I was pretty much counting down the last 20 minutes. For the first time ever, though, my upper arms turned pink as the water was filling the tub.

After getting out, I was soooo cold too and ended up falling asleep cold. I also slept 11 hours last night!!!!

Today (the next morning), my body is SOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO tired (not sleepy, more exhausted) and very sore like I have exercised A LOT. When eating breakfast this morning, I was very hungry (definitely new for me) and I was able to eat the entire meal without feeling full (all 48g of protein).

I think we have to remember that these CT baths are probably similar (to our bodies) as to running a few miles. Which, for some of you, you might be okay with this but for me I don't think I could even run .25 of a mile currently!! So my advice is to be easy on your body and make sure you are eating enough.

Think I will skip the CT today.  (Darn!)

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-24T19:15:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5480>

Having skipped the CT yesterday, it was much harder today ... Managed 25 minutes in 52F water though, and was a deep reddish pink all over afterwards, but skin temp. was only down to 57F on my new infra-red thermometer. I suppose I'm making progress? Still shivering intensely an hour after getting out of the bath ...

Y'know, sometimes I think we get sucked into the mindset of thinking "If it's good, more is better!" If you go back to the CT protocol blog, Dr. Kruse says we should plan on doing the baths "2-5 times a week depending upon your starting weight, BF %, and what your goals are." I don't think we should necessarily be doing the baths daily.

Your fatigue is probably your body asking for a little rest : )

### tweety — 2012-03-24T19:55:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-before-the-ct-mayhem-ends.193/post-5500>

Good point. I have been taking it slow, no shivering but I still feel like I am making progress. I had a few days of intense muscle tiredness, and I backed off a little and I am back tp feeling better.

As a side note, I am so so so thankful for CT. I had been on the reset since last summer and my husband was a very cranky, unwilling and stubborn supporter and sometimes participant. I told him about CT a few weeks ago, which I thought he would balk at, and he embraced it entirely! This morning, on his own accord, he started counting all his protein grams trying to get his BAB! Thats enough success for me, I am happy! Thanks Dr. K!

# Thread: Labs for Leptin Insensitivity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-16T14:02:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/post-1377>

Hi, can someone please list the labs Dr Kruse recommends to test for leptin insensitivity/sensitivity. Please include the name and the ideal range. and the non ideal. I know its in the blogs, but I am sure some one wrote them down. Thanks!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-16T16:21:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/post-1446>

I think you're looking for this: [Optimizing labs](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/)

How did I find it? [Here](http://https://docs.google.com/document/pub?id=1lhFLT8y0Lhn4KVX9B7YNJNRbduLr7Z_6bme_8E-XYOo). Bookmark that last link especially.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-16T19:18:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/post-1606>

Leptin resistance means you'll have high leptin in the face of low VIP, low MSH, and low AVP levels, not to mention a host of other mismatched hormone signals: low GnRH, low growth hormone, etc. These are pretty expensive tests, unfortunately.

I think this is part of why Dr. Kruse suggests the mirror test: If you look in the mirror and your pudgy...well, you know.

I'd love to order all these test on my obese patients, but most people don't want to spend that kind of money, yet labs really do help if someone is really sick or horribly stalled.

Here are some cheap tests that give you a clue: low HDL, high TGs, high A1c, high PM cortisol, low vitamin D, low DHEA, low magnesium, high HS-CRP (in my limited experience anyway). Insurance will cover these pretty well most of the time and they're not too pricey. Hope that helps.

### vlynnb — 2012-03-18T23:23:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/post-2473>

Dan, I know everyone's insurance differs but mine is a high deductible. catastrophic policy so I am not sure it would cover these tests. Does there usually have to be a certain diagnosis to justify these tests or will a routine yearly physical usually justify them?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-19T02:31:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/post-2525>

you can get a Leptin level or a T3 and rev T3 and then use the conversion calculator at the STTM.com to see where your ratio is. We like it above 20. Other signs......high LDL levels because of a low T3 conversion to pregnenolone. high ferritin, haptoglobin, HS CRP, low Vit D and low DHEA levels.......all things that walk hand and hand with LR.

### L8F — 2012-03-19T02:32:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/post-2526>

The most bang for your buck comes with HS CRP, DHEAS and Vit D. You can learn a lot from those numbers. Also for women, a day 21 Progesterone and Estradiol are helpful.
