### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-03-19T21:39:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/post-2910>

> **Jack Kruse;2535 wrote:** Lucy knows the deal now.......she heard it live from our mouths.

Great! I just had them done recently myself - so now how do we interpret the results? High/Low/ok indicates what?

And btw, it's SO reassuring to know you're still looking in!

### L8F — 2012-03-19T23:37:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/post-2957>

Post the results here with ranges, and people will comment...

Basicly you want high Vit D, meaning at least 50, Dr K likes 70s+. Others out there vary in what they prefer. Depends what is wrong with you etc.

DHEA(s) should be high. Example, mine was VERY low in the 70s. This is NOT optimal. You want MUCH higher. I forgot to mention the preferred lab is DHEAS which is a better more stable number than just plain DHEA which is also measurable.

There is also a whole science of relating Cortisol (Saliva via ZRT labs or thru Canary Club) and its relation to DHEA. Stop the Thyroid Madness also has more on COrtisol results.

HS or Cardio CRP is a marker for general body inflammation. Higher than 3 puts you in the higher risk cardiac category, but also is not good from a general health standpoint. That being said, acute ailments or issues can spot raise the CRPs. Unless you get the sensitive or Cardio CRP it will not be as helpful. It should be as close to zero as possible. Mine went down with strict AI Paleo protocol, and I am looking forward to retesting now that I have been ketogenic AI paleo pretty strictly for a few weeks, plus on/off CT.

As for E and P, it is important stuff. Progesterone is a range, and it should be nicely up in last half of cycle. 10 is low, for example. The E to P ratio in second half can also tell you a bunch about the mystical "Estrogen Dominance" which just means you have too much Estrogen circulating around. This is usually, though not always coupled with a too low amount of progesterone. =general imbalance. Too much E mucks around with all the other hormones and their functions=badness.

Vkiernan here knows a lot about the supps etc to work on this, and I think she has a good doc advising her. DIM is one, as is Milk Thistle which supposrts the liver working overtime on all this E processing. Cold also is supposed to help.

So there are my thoughts. HS CRP to get overall inflm, D for immune status and propping up all the rest, DHEA for hormonal status, and female hormones to get more down the line in the hormone cascade.

### L8F — 2012-03-20T00:37:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/post-2977>

I think the key is, unless you have a good Paleo type or BHRT doc to interpret things for you, to stick to basics as you implement all the Paleo diet, as well as any AI diets and LR stuff. Then you can retest reasonably cheaply the main markers to see the direction you are headed. If after some time things don't feel right, or you are at a body fat % plateau etc, then dig deeper.

The thing I have learned is that there is SO much going on in us, and you can delve as deeply as you want. How deep do you want to go,and how much of a difference will it make?? Not sure, but if you have AI issues, it doesn't get much better than strict Paleo AI diet, and maybe ketogenic.

Thank you for that! I was just looking to get my labs done but didn't want to spend $2,000 (yikes!). I did get the spreadsheet from Shijin13 - that was super helpful! ### L8F — 2012-03-21T02:47:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-leptin-insensitivity.200/post-3693>

> **differentstory;3514 wrote:** Thank you for that! I was just looking to get my labs done but didn't want to spend $2,000 (yikes!). I did get the spreadsheet from Shijin13 - that was super helpful! >

> Also, just wanted to add, I am completely clueless as to all of this. So sorry if I'm asking stupid questions!

Well, what are your symptoms? Do you think that you could have thyroid issues? If so, get TSH, Free T3, Free T4, and Reverse T3. Also Cholesterol is also a good one to get. I would just go regular fasting cholesterol panel. Or, you could just go the basics, find out what those say, and go from there. I think a tight diet will help a ton of stuff, and it is what you would probably need to do anyway if there were derangements in stuff.

So, do the basics, see what is up there. Also symptoms help a lot in knowing what to test....Save your $ is that is not in surplus, and get super good diet going and test the basics...

Thanks so much for the response. My husband originally weighted in at 400 2 years ago. He's lost 100 pounds all my himself, but weight loss always (always always) stalls at 300. I really really want to help him.

Me on the other hand. . .\*sigh\*. I am a mess. Very poor diet for the past 5 years. I started a slow carb diet about 16 days ago and was hungry and generally miserable but started losing (lost about 4 pounds). Then I switched to paleo/leptin reset 12 days ago. I feel awesome. No cravings. Each day gets better and better but I am stuck with the weight loss.  I gained all 4 pounds back and haven't lost anything.

Prior to all of this, I was speculating there was something wrong with my horomones/thyroid.

Any help you can provide would be awesome. At this point I am lost. I am determined not to give up, but am frustrated today (scale was up again!! 5 straight days in a row.)

### chrystin.comeau@live.com — 2012-03-22T00:07:35+0200

>

Honestly, I thought "Whatever. This guy doesn't know me. I don't have menstrual/fertility/ovarian issues! My hormones are fine!" Fine if you don't see the need for estrogen or progesterone, LOL.

### L8F — 2012-03-22T01:26:40+0200

It is NOT a rapid weight loss strategy, but a get your health back and watch your weight normalize kind of thing. IT WORKS!!!! Especially if your hormones are all messed up (they are, almost everyone on here has messed up hormones and thinks they don't at first) Stay with it. It is simple to do, but not easy. I cannot remember who are the weight gainers initially, but there are many of them out there. Keep going, spend some time in the success stories area for inspiration.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-23T00:32:43+0200

# Thread: Post workout carb eating?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-16T17:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-1479>

It seems a common recommendation/ practice to eat more carbs following weight training to replenish depleted muscle glycogen stores. What happens when we do not do that and eat low carb instead??? how does the glycogen get restored? does it come form protein via gluconeogenesis? from fat? Is it a BAD idea not to eat a more carby meal following LHT (Lift heavy things) exercise. This is for those of us trying to lose body fat. Thanks!

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-16T17:05:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-1484>

When I LHT or HIIT I do it right before my next meal. I eat keto. In the new monster thread Quelsen gives an excellent explanation why you don't need this if you are keto adapted

No need to eat lots of carbs when you are a fat burner.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-17T03:24:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-1828>

OK, but how how does the glycogen get replaced when eating low carb? I thought it was important to replenish glycogen stores by eating carbs after a workout...

### Glamorama — 2012-03-17T04:54:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-1860>

> **PaleoDentist;1831 wrote:** OK, but how how does the glycogen get replaced when eating low carb? I thought it was important to replenish glycogen stores by eating carbs after a workout...

Because I'm in early fall here in Oz, I still have 30-60 gram carbs daily. 14 grams of those come from a can of pure coconut water with pulp straight after my LHT-sessions. From my understanding, you can build muscle with just fat, protein and CT... My muscle is coming out nicely with strength-increase every week, and I'm low-carb, but not keto yet, as I'm waiting till winter.

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-17T13:44:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-1918>

If you are eating a keto - paleo diet but still maintain a carb intake (from vegetables) and you keep it in the under 50 gram a day range, you will never deplete your glycogen stores. The avg human can store 400 grams of gylcogen in their muscles before it gets stored as fat.

The trick is by eating keto you turn yourself into a fat burner instead of a sugar burner. Your body will use fat as fuel source saving muscle and liver glycogen for when it is needed in emergency situations or extended bouts of stress.

The only time you completely deplete them is through intense exercise over a long duration( marathons) or through a diet that is completely void of carbs (Eskimos eating blubber and meat)

Meal timing is important for muscles. So after a workout consume a protein and carb meal. Think 30 grams protein and 15 -20 grams of carbs from veggie sources. Keep your fat low in this meal so you do not overly slow absorption (the protein will slow it down enough) Spread the rest of your carb intake over your other meals.

The reason to put the two together is glycogen helps shuttle amino acids into muscle for recovery and repair.

BAB- protein/fat little or no carbs

PWO meal- protein/carb little or no fat

Dinner (if neccessary) protein/fat little or no carb

adjust timing depending on when you wotkout.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-17T14:33:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-1935>

> **PaleoDentist;1831 wrote:** OK, but how how does the glycogen get replaced when eating low carb? I thought it was important to replenish glycogen stores by eating carbs after a workout...

your liver does it......gluconeogenesis. IF you decided to be warm adapted you will need the carbs.......if you decide to be cold adapted you do not need them much at all. It really comes down to how you want to live.

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-17T15:45:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-1962>

> **Jack Kruse;1940 wrote:** your liver does it......gluconeogenesis. IF you decided to be warm adapted you will need the carbs.......if you decide to be cold adapted you do not need them much at all. It really comes down to how you want to live.

Dr. Kruse, just so I understand. It was my understanding that gluconeogenesis does not occur until you have depleted all of your muscle and liver glycogen. Through extended intense exercise, bouts of starvation or a diet void of a carbohydrate source. (Inuits)

If I understand you correctly, you are saying that once you become cold adapted the pathway bypasses what you have stored in muscle and liver and that gluconeogenesis (the making of glucose from non-carbohydrates) occurs in the liver. So it fills the storage of liver and muscle and uses the leftovers to fuel the body along with keytone bodies from a ketopaleo diet.

I will have to reread the CT series to make sure I understand this action.

Thanks for this thread. This is really helpful. And my take is that to the degree that you are cold adapted is the degree to which you can do without carbs post exercise. IMHO which is very, very H, it would be possible to have a false sense of how LS you've become other than relative to your LR experience. (unless you test!!) Ditto for how cold adapted one is. So which is the worse error - eating too many carbs post WO because you are more cold adapted than you realized? (Banana in Alaska winter problem) OR eating too few carbs thinking you are cold adapted when you aren't really there yet?

### SimonM — 2012-03-17T22:54:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2126>

Continuing misunderstandings here

There are no Inuit sprinters/middle distance runners for a very good reason. And don't tell me they CAN sprint to get away from polar bears.

Hunter-gatherers of any stripe, even those who are persistence hunters, on average do not run all that fast. Many times much slower than even an average calibre modern marathon runner, but they can keep going ALL day. (Modern runners find it very difficult to keep up with traditional persistence hunters because modern runners are used to running in straight lines on even terrain and are not used to ducking under trees and jumping snakes etc - it is not a speed thing).

The keto/ low-no carb plans is great for endurance and abysmal for speed or speed/endurance. During any activity with a wodge of anaerobic quality to it you will fail big-time without carbs. The reason is that even if you are a fat-burning machine (and many successful ultra endurance athletes like the long course and Ironman triathlon specialists have achieved this - it is a routine effect of typical base building), during a highly anaerobic event, the body cannot produce glucose from fat fast enough. That is why studies have shown big gains in endurance with loss of speed in athletes switching to fat-dominant diets.

Yes, you can see huge gains in V02 max from becoming a fat-burning machine, but it case this gets tossed back at me, v02max is a very poor predictor of competitive performance. Other facts such as economy are more important. In other words, having a high V02max does not necessarily mean you can out-perform anybody with a lower Vo2max. As Prof Tim Noakes has pointed out - if that were true then all competitions would be decided in the lab.

Recently some elites have been playing around with a "train low, race high" thing, where they train on a high fat diet (and feel absolutely crap) and then race on high carbs, and it seems promising. Arguably the world's #1 endurance guy ultraman world champion Jonas Colting eats low-carbish Paleo in training and scarfs high carb, even junk high-carb, while competing.

Carbs for higher intensity exercise are absolutely essential: Armstrong (cold adapted?) 70% carbs; Phelps (cold adapted?) massive amounts of carbs; Sherpas (cold adapted) eg Apa Sherpa - record holder with 22 Everest summits - high carbs, and high carbs + high fat once working past base camp; Kenyans (world's dominant runners above sprint distances) - high carb.

Dr Kruse makes the valid point that the longevity of high-carbers like the Kenyans is probably not good, and I have lost track of the number of former Olympic-level athletes who are suffering in later years with very serious health conditions...so it may be, as Dr K says - that there is a trade-off here - short-term glory (on carbs) or long-term health.

So, @cgk, when you ask

"So which is the worse error - eating too many carbs post WO because you are more cold adapted than you realized? (Banana in Alaska winter problem) OR eating too few carbs thinking you are cold adapted when you aren't really there yet?"

the answer probably is...it depends whether quick recovery and ability to do the training necessary to develop anaerobic capacity and power is your aim, of whether you are more focused on health and longevity and the ability to sustain a lower-level performance over 4 hours +.

Great thread! Thanks guys.

### Barry — 2012-03-18T01:56:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2182>

Simon & co

some very good points there, this is certainly a big topic though and its one I have a fair bit of experience with. I'll outline a few things here, theory, hypothesis and practical experience. Need Dr K's input and then we'll see where we go from there

I'll start from the top and work backwards:

For about 3 years I have been keto adapting. Its been a gradual process of increasing fat intake, cycling carb intake, and gradually reducing carb intake. I've aided the adaptation through my training strategies. Basically, I;ve done a lot (and I mean a lot) of fasted state training. When I say "training"... I mean ultra endurance stuff.. Ironman type training... 4-5hr bike rides, 3-4hr runs (and longer but I'll get to that) and long swims. I built this up over the years from doing say a 1hr run fasted to a 5hr bike ride fasted, with minimal eating during too. I now do all my morning training (5-6days a week) fasted, i.e. no breakfast. It could be a 2-3K swim in the pool or a 5hr bike ride. Always fasted, coffee being my breakfast of choice ;-). All this was done based on the research I've studied on glycogen depleted training, PPAR, CPT-1, IMTG, Mitchodrial Biogensis, IL-6 and basically everything related to upregulating fat adaptation.

I now run ultramarathons and have competed in 100mile mountain races. I've raced in 8 ultramarathons over the past 2 years, distances between 30 and 100miles, all trail mountain type. I raced each of them fasted. That is, I get up, while everyone else is shovelling porridge, banana's, bagels etc into them to "fuel"... I am sipping on a fresh coffee, and maybe one or two ergogenic aids. I've won 2 races, finished 2nd twice, 3rd in another and been in the top 10 in the remainder. I mention this not to "bragg" but this to put things in context in terms of the level I am competing at.

Theory and Hypothesis

This is where I am hoping I can link in with Dr K. So the theory is all based on fat adaptation and fuel efficiency. So upregulate PPAR, switch on beta oxidation, improve CPT-1 function and various other enzymes and transporters. For more info on this, I've written an article which explains most things: [http://www.optimumnutrition4sport.com/?page\_id=547](http://http://www.optimumnutrition4sport.com/?page_id=547) ... looking back on it, I have missed out on a couple of things which I;ve read through Dr. K's work. The effect IL-6 has (and I mean good affect in terms of lipolysis), UCP3 and possibly 1 or 2 other mechanisms.

The Link/Hypothesis:

My speculation (and it is just a speculation which is why I am sharing this here) is the adaptations gained from CT in relation to ketogensis can be also gained through ultra fasted state endurance training.

\- it improves insulin sensitivity and thus leptin sensitivity

\- it increases fat oxidation both adipose and IMTG

\- it increases mitochondrial biogensis

\- FAT/CD36 is upregulated

\- NPY is swtiched off during exercise

\- UCP3 is upregulated

\- other pathways that i have forgotten !

There are studies on athletes which support the above, mostly. The studies show that the adaptations take place but that performance doesn't improve in most cases. The reason for this is quite simple. As Dr.K mentioned, it takes time to fully adapt 24-36months. Most of the studies are carried out between 4-8weeks. On top of that they are neither CT adapted, keto or in my case "fasted state" adapted.

I'm probably leaving a few things out as my head has literally been melting since I started reading Dr K's blogs !! but I hope he can comment on the above !

Another Link:

Just to put things in a bit more perspective. I have run over hills and mountains for 6-7hrs, at a decent pace, with some big max heart rate climbs and efforts, on empty - no breakfast, and with only 2 snacks during - I use coconut/nut butter/oat homemade bars. So we're talking maybe 40-50g of carbs for the entire run. I know there are going to be some serious concerns about cortisol and telomeres etc but I don't want to conversation to center around that for now. I do what I do because I love it. Its an amazing feeling and experience. And with that I think comes some big time epigenetics. There has got to be some gene expressions as a result of this possibly relating to SIRT-1 and PDC-1 amongst others. Again, hopefully Dr K can comment on this !

Grey Area

As mentioned I think by Simon, the big grey area for me is top end intensity training, so flat out intervals whether that be 600-1200m reps on the track or 2-3min efforts on the bike or even just threshold work. If we are in the anaerobic state, we need glucose. Now the thing is, there are other systems - Cori Cycle and Glucose Alanine Cycle. Possibly also so glycolytic amino acids can work too.

Apart from my own athletic adventures, I work with elite athletes too - Tour de France Cyclists, Olympic Triathletes and World Class Distance Runners. From my experience in this field, I can tell you that I know of none that are following a ketogenic diet. Some of my atheletes are close, but nowhere near

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-18T22:48:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2463>

Hi guys, I want to jump in this thread as I am looking for performance gains, though not quite in the same vein.

I am 40 yo female powerlifter, I have been using CT going on 3 weeks now. Cold baths, 55F for 35-60min, almost daily in the evening after I train and have dinner, and 5 min cold showers 55Falso, in the morning.

I have seen some promising results, but it is too soon to be sure. Subjectively, I feel less sore, and have greater energy. I feel recovered from training sooner, like I could handle an extra day's training, but I won't do that just yet. I have hit some PRs on body weight movements, like pull ups, and I have hit some recent PRs in squat and DL, but I switched programming (and coaches) so I can't truly say my recent improvements are direct result of CT. It sure feels like it though!

I have a meet in 4 weeks. I will not do anything different from what I am doing now, eating VLC, about 120g of protein, baths in evening, random spot icing and the AM showers.

I'm thinking of trying CT before training, or even crazier, during. As in, when I am done with squatting, icing my glutes hammies while I bench. Then when I move onto deadlifts or powercleans, icing, chest, delts. Or icing before my lifts. Yeah, nutty. Which is why I won't do it until after the meet.

What forms of CT are you using regularly, and when?

### Barry — 2012-03-19T01:02:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2500>

> What forms of CT are you using regularly, and when?

Hi Teenie Leek (sounds a bit funny saying this, anyway)

CT I've done has not been specific in terms of Dr K's methods, more just training outdoors for hours in minus temperatures.

In terms of timing, most say post CT is best time to train.

I think we do all have to bear one thing in mind. Over recent years, ice baths etc have been shown to blunt adaptation to the muscle stress. So while it does numb the pain when used post heavy training, it can actually inhibit the beneficial inflammatory response

So my advice to you would be to CT pre workouts, not during training or not post training. However, during competition, this is where CT post is fine and even during, albeit this is something I have never really seen or have experience with

hope that helps

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-19T01:21:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2505>

Simon, I did look through the Vesper site, and have a few ideas: sleeping on a conductive mattress pad would be a start for the grounding. As far as compression and cooling, I was also thinking about training while wearing a cooling vest. The compression part is a bit more difficult. Powerlifters can lift massive amounts with compression clothing, squat suits and bench shirts. I compete "raw" which is just belt and knee wraps, when one competes with compression suits/shirts, that is "geared" and a different category. I cannot wear compression gear in competition. Just cotton tshirt, lycra singlet. I don't want to train in anything that I won't be able to compete in.

Now you have me thinking though, I could train my volume days in semi-compression (under armour) and do my intensity days (which mimic the meet, 3 attempts at the 3 lifts, low volume but very heavy) in my regular gear. hmmmmph. lots to consider

I work 2 jobs, and I have tried to do 6 days a week, experimented with Bulgarian/Romanian methods, and was too much with my work schedule. After the meet, I may add in a 4th day, on the weekend of "accessory" work: farmer's walks, tire flips, weighted pull ups and dips. I'm still in the idea stages.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-19T18:45:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2798>

Thanks both Simon and Barry for your comments. I got into all of this because I was a 47 year old crossfitter suffering from crappy sleep, lousy recovery, and ultimately was trading DOMS for same day pain - even though I was eating 90% + paleo/primal. Performance is on my radar screen, but functionality/longevity has to come first - that was a promise I made when I became a parent right? IOTW, I don't really care if I have to scale a WOD or do a different WOD altogether. I just want to be doing the RIGHT WOD and eating the RIGHT food to go with it.

I need a graphic that somehow plots anaerobic/aerobic exercise against duration against temperature/cold adaptation and is then somehow superimposes what type of pathway the body is in and then how you would best fuel before, during, after the exercise.

Some sort of x, y, z axis, color and pattern coded gem that would make it easier to figure this out.

Just another N=1 who wants to eat and move to be well for a good long time.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-19T18:50:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2803>

> **SimonM;2612 wrote:** One quick point - what you are calling "compression" and what Vesper calls "compression" are apples and oranges. From what I understand from the website, the Vesper wraps are more like tourniquets - much greater force being applied to actively (almost) block blood flow and INCREASE build up of lactate.

Try putting on a squat suit. It is not under armour like material. You struggle and sweat getting that thing on. It constricts like mad. It IS like a tourniquet.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-19T19:01:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2810>

> **cgk;2807 wrote:** I just want to be doing the RIGHT WOD and eating the RIGHT food to go with it.
>
>
>
> Just another N=1 who wants to eat and move to be well for a good long time.

Does your CF box offer strength only classes, or the option to do strength only portion of a class, and opt out of the metcon? Though I realize that many CF boxes just offer metcons, there are a few like mine, that are strength biased and have lots of strength only workouts and/or strength followed by short conditioning. We also have lifting only classes where they can follow a custom program we've written, or one they have come up with. Another idea is scale workouts yourself, telling the coach what you plan on doing, rather than doing "their" version of scaled. Generally I'd have people scale reps and rounds and/or time limit on an amrap. If people have health issues, I generally scale all 3, or just give another workout altogether.

Your coaches should be amenable to your requests. If they aren't, I'd suggest finding a new box, or following another box's programming from home. HTH

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-19T19:22:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2824>

Teenie Leek

Definitely! Seems that the pure strength days/ metcon days in our box- are less obvious than they used to be. It is definitely harder to program/plan yourself, when WOD's are posted the night before. Totally concur that you can program yourself within a box, but there are limits to how much you can do that and still feel like a member of the box. During the Leptin Rx, I just went in and did one of KStarr's MWOD's. Now it is time to figure out what next... I would just as soon do Oly lifts 2 days a week and 2 body weight metcons and hope that is enough and not too much. No clue yet on timing either... since I did an 0515 class before breakfast until I started the leptin reset. I guess working out fasted before lunch and then eating x or y for lunch based on whether or not it was a strength or a metcon would be one way to go about it.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-19T19:45:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2840>

> **cgk;2833 wrote:** Teenie Leek
>
> Definitely! Seems that the pure strength days/ metcon days in our box- are less obvious than they used to be. It is definitely harder to program/plan yourself, when WOD's are posted the night before. Totally concur that you can program yourself within a box, but there are limits to how much you can do that and still feel like a member of the box.

hmmm, I disagree, I have people who "do their own thing" and people following what's on the board, and everyone (to my knowledge) feels right at home.

I have members who I tell ahead of time what the workouts are, because I don't want their individual programming to get messed up. Many times I will tell them to come in anyway, if the wod is front squats followed by a 15 mim metcon, and they are supposed to be back squatting and pressing, they can still come in, back squat with the class (as they front squat) and then press as the others are doing the conditioning. They often cheer on the others in between sets, and play a supportive role. It is actually very nice to see.

IS there any way you can find out the workouts ahead of time? And I believe Jack has said optimal time for working out is 1-5pm, although I realize this not possible for most people. Where are you located? I'm in Bergenfield, NJ

### Barry — 2012-03-19T21:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2899>

> **cgk;2807 wrote:** Thanks both Simon and Barry for your comments. I got into all of this because I was a 47 year old crossfitter suffering from crappy sleep, lousy recovery, and ultimately was trading DOMS for same day pain - even though I was eating 90% + paleo/primal. Performance is on my radar screen, but functionality/longevity has to come first - that was a promise I made when I became a parent right? IOTW, I don't really care if I have to scale a WOD or do a different WOD altogether. I just want to be doing the RIGHT WOD and eating the RIGHT food to go with it.
>
>
>
> I need a graphic that somehow plots anaerobic/aerobic exercise against duration against temperature/cold adaptation and is then somehow superimposes what type of pathway the body is in and then how you would best fuel before, during, after the exercise.
>
> Some sort of x, y, z axis, color and pattern coded gem that would make it easier to figure this out.
>
>
>
> Just another N=1 who wants to eat and move to be well for a good long time.

CGK,

my only advice would be this - don't follow any x,y,z axis chart with fancy colours and patterns !! Use the key principles related to health and fitness - keto/paleo template, low stress, good sleep, natural movement/strength based exercise etc... then go on feel and listen to your own body ! it knows best... no PT or WOD chart is going to know how you feel and think...

Teenie Leek's advice is good... partition your strength/HIIT sessions based on feel.

But like I said, above all, be good to your body and it will return the favour !

Barry

### SimonM — 2012-03-19T21:49:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2917>

Yeah what he said

No, but really,as my coaching mentor Ian King has said, sports scientists are actually sports historians... they are so far behind that you won't find a graphic that is up to date, let alone can stay up to date. Besides which, thanks to Jack, we are doing stuff (CR) that no one else has looked at it yet, and as you can see from the discussions here, we are still trying to work out what the effects are on energy systems and how to fuel and so on.

The other thing is that the "right" training for you depends totally on your long-term goals, which you've clearly stated, and on breaking them down into specific attributes, or qualities, that you want to develop. So you are prioritising longevity - what does that look like for you, and what do you need to achieve it? Similarly, "functionality" - an exercise isn't functional because some functional fitness guru says it is - it is functional if it fits your unique needs/goals. So functional for what, excactly?

The only thing I would add to Barry's comment is that in the "listen to your body" category, pay equal or more attention to your performance figures - that is, record and chart your performances. I have often had brilliant sessions when, going in, I have "felt" like crap. I set my 400m PR on a day when I had been physically sick a couple of hours before. If you are obsessive enough to also keep some brief record of your food choices and timing, plus weather (temp and pressure), then you may start spotting some patterns.

### Barry — 2012-03-19T22:20:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-2933>

> **SimonM;2624 wrote:** Maths is not my strong point, but according to the calculators I've found online you are using a kcal per minute of running at 9 or 10:00 per mile pace and a guesstimate at my weight (I guess). Obviously, I am running sub-5:00 per mile pace - kcal cost is way higher - and I am 149lb.
>
>
>
> I'm prepared to put in the year or two Jack says are necessary to adapt to CR and keto, but as I've said in previous posts, my hesitancy is due to the fact that there are no examples of speed athletes having any success. Jack continues to mention elite athletes using CR, but I guess because of confidentiality cannot mention names. Meanwhile, all the high performers he has mentioned as being prime examples of what can be achieved - Armstrong, Phelps and the Sherpas - all eat high-carb.
>
>
>
> If you'll excuse me banging on about this - it's not a question of whether or not we can do short intervals, or high intensity workouts on keto or low carb (with or without CT), as clearly we can...it's a question of the quality/speed of those intervals. I'd like to see some records/ results/examples that show that world-class performances are possible.
>
>
>
> Cheers Barry.

Simon

I'm with you here in terms of elite athletes and performance measures. I do think that you are possibly creating a psychological barrier in thinking that it is not possible or hasn't been done before. Remember Roger Bannister ?? Like everything, you must believe it is possible in order for it to happen.

But without getting into a philosophical debate, I think speed energy production is possible through low carb. As I said, you don't need much to run 100m or even 1 mile efforts. And of course you haver your PCr system as well as the others I've mentioned, in addition to the ones we may not know about yet ! And yes, fat burning needs oxygen... but PCr, Cori and Glucose/Alanine doesn't !

Re CGK advice - very good points, especially related to tracking your own performance, good/bad days etc. Oh, we're not all "sports historians" though ;-)

### SimonM — 2012-03-21T02:11:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-workout-carb-eating.205/post-3660>

> **Barry;2943 wrote:** Simon
>
>
>
> I'm with you here in terms of elite athletes and performance measures. I do think that you are possibly creating a psychological barrier in thinking that it is not possible or hasn't been done before. Remember Roger Bannister ?? Like everything, you must believe it is possible in order for it to happen.
>
>

> But without getting into a philosophical debate, I think speed energy production is possible through low carb. As I said, you don't need much to run 100m or even 1 mile efforts. And of course you haver your PCr system as well as the others I've mentioned, in addition to the ones we may not know about yet ! And yes, fat burning needs oxygen... but PCr, Cori and Glucose/Alanine doesn't !
>

> Re CGK advice - very good points, especially related to tracking your own performance, good/bad days etc. Oh, we're not all "sports historians" though ;-)

Interesting point, Barry. Bannister was not only medically trained but was researching 02 uptake by breaking down runners on a treadmill - and this in the 1950s! He had the advantage over everyone else chasing the sub-4 mile in that he knew from his experiments that 3 x 400 in 60sec + 1 x 400 in 59 WAS physiologically possible. So I'm looking for pre-attempt data, too

And I have a wonderful anaerobic energy source in lactate (which I train to use) thanks very much, without all your other esoteric and/or unknown pathways!!!! hahahahahahaha

I was looking on some of the low-carb forums to see what others' experience was. One guy, a cop doing SWAT team training, was adamant that going Very Low Carb had no negative effects whatsoever on his "high intensity" training. The basic plank of his HIT was running 1.5 miles in just under 12 minutes! Yes quite. I train with 60-year-old+ women who can do repeat miles at that pace. Another guy agreed that VLC was the way to go for HIT: he runs marathons. So just for the record, what I'm looking at is the optimal fuel for repeats at a minimum of 90 per cent VO2 max.

Interestingly, one guy said he'd had a intensity performance fall-off when he went low-carb, and had "cured" it by adding in more fat - to the extent of "snacking" on tablespoonfuls of oil. (MCTs anyone?) You mentioned you were eating loads of fat. Maybe that is a missing factor for me - get fat intake to >40 per cent? Any thoughts?

# Thread: Need help getting below 50g carbs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GigiPatata — 2012-03-16T18:18:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1551>

Hi - I am a 45year old female. 5'10" 215Lb. BMI=29

I started paleo eating in December 2011 and then leptin reset mid-January. No weight loss to date. I have re-started leptin reset a few times: in the first 5-6 weeks I would have a huge carb craving/binge about every 10-12 days. Then I would get back on track. In the past 2-3 weeks I have not had any major binge (perhaps a couple of very minor ones) - this is a great victory!

I have definitely noticed some great improvements: almost no carb cravings; no longer need to snack (yay!) softer skin; much more alert and feeling awake; better energy; libido slightly improved (room for improvement). I am still not sleeping well most nights, but have had a few nights of good deep sleep.

I eat 50g protein breakfast at 6am, lunch at noon - usually a salad with chicken and then dinner at about 6-7pm. (Love the no-snacking!) I have recently started CT with cold baths - up to about 15-20 minutes.

So the good news is that the carb cravings and binges are almost gone. The tough part is that I still have a very hard time getting my carbs to under 50g per day (let alone ketosis).

I need to lose weight - I have lost zero pounds. I have thought that the getting my carbs down would come eventually - and in some respects I have had a victory in not having any binges lately. But any time I reduce my carbs and attempt ketosis I get very hungry, hypoglycemic, and can't sleep. (Two nights ago I had to have a carb snack in the middle of the night just because I was desperate to get some sleep after 25 g carbs that day.)

Any advice in how to get my carbs down? I eat about 1-2TB coconut oil with every meal. 50g protein breakfast is 2-3 eggs, bacon, and ground meat. No dairy.

Also I 've been considering starting some weight training given that I'm showing signs of leptin sensitivity - but would that derail my attempts to go low-carb?

Advice from you wonderful knowledgable people (most of whom I've been following on MDA) would be much appreciated!!

### Birdy — 2012-03-16T18:27:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1558>

What are you eating? Personally when I want to lower the carbs, I stick to greens and vegetables as seasoning. I use garlic instead of onion, and cook meat with celery, fennel, mushrooms and parsley instead of beans or carrots. I don't use tomatoes much, only the fresh kind, but if you cook with tomatoes, the carbs start to really add up. For fruit, I stick to lemons and berries.

If you have to carb-snack at night, you are still wacky hormonally. Check the monster thread for what to do about sleep. I would recommend following Dr. K's light recommendations carefully until your sleep is fixed. I found that eating more fat in the evening helped.

Don't fret about your weight!!!!!! Just remind yourself how wonderful it is not to be bingeing.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-16T18:34:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1564>

On the MDA Monster thread - there were several ppl who were having binging problems - they eventually found success by increasing their BAB, some above 75g of protein + FAT.

increase the BAB, focus on the meal timing and keep your carbs to in season veggies only.

FWIW if you're still having the carb cravings - back off on the LHT. when the cravings go away - then push the LHT/HIIT. you need to convert from being a sugar burner to a fat burner....you're still a sugar burner...which is why you crave the carbs.... when you can look that really freaking good cupcake, potato, basket of strawberries in the eye - and go meh? then you're moving toward being a fat burner...

example: last night DH kept trying to get me to eat a rice crispy treat (I'm still working on him) - I kept telling him I didn't want it...it had NO appeal to me... HE was like WTF? you never turn down sweets... I told him - hell strawberries are too sweet for me these days....

### GigiPatata — 2012-03-16T19:05:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1593>

Thanks for the replies! I think I have to examine my current carb consumptions - on most days it's just vegetables but definitely I need to start paying attention to onions and tomato sauce! The night carb-snack was a one-time thing, when I had tried a day of ketosis - otherwise I never eat at night (even before LR).

I think I will just focus on getting my carbs gradually lower - and will try to increase BAB and fat.

Right now I eat carbs mostly to avoid feelings of hunger and hypoglycemia (and insomnia as I had the other night). How do I avoid the light-headedness that comes from reducing carbs? Is it just a matter of gradully reducing carbs? Or eating even more fat? I guess I've been trying to curb my current habit of eating 4-5 TB coconut oil a day - maybe I need to go nuts with it!!

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-16T19:10:16+0200

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-16T19:15:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1603>

When you are trying to get into ketosis, is just about cutting carbs? Because some days I am near zero carbs, and I am still not in ketosis. Does the ratio of protein and fat come into play here? How high does my fat have to be? Could I be eating too much protein? I \*think\* I am eating high fat (fatty cuts of meat, sauteing in butter or CO, fatty fish, whole eggs, coconut milk in my coffee etc)

Is there a dietary factor I am missing? Is my 2 cups of coffee interfering? Is it the few carbs in the coconut milk? Argh.

### JC2K — 2012-03-16T19:22:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1607>

< 100 grams of carbs, the lower the better. High fat, protein moderate to low. Higher protein can potentially affect ketosis through gluconeogenesis.

Look for hidden carbs in what you eat. Ketosis takes a few days to get into as your body works through it's glycogen stores.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-16T19:31:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1610>

Thanks JC2K.

Hmmmmm. Since I train very hard weighlfting and powerlifting, I've been keeping my protein up, maybe that is the problem. I generally eat 120g protein, I weigh 152#.

I know about carbs in eggs, onions coconut milk. Those are really the only carbs I am consuming. Rarely I will have some spinach or a lil bit of tomato sauce. Not often, at least I don't think so.

I have a meet in 4 weeks. After the meet I will re-arrange my protein/fat and see if I can get into ketosis. If the lower protein affects my training, I'd rather have that happen after the meet. Not a good idea to change things now. At that time I will experiment more with CT WRT my training.

### JC2K — 2012-03-16T19:34:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1613>

If you're got an event coming up, definitely don't change things at the last minute. Great way to fail. Protein converts at a 58 percent efficiency to glucose. So 120g = around 69 to 70 grams of glucose to add into your numbers.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-16T19:45:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1616>

> **JC2K;1615 wrote:** If you're got an event coming up, definitely don't change things at the last minute. Great way to fail. Protein converts at a 58 percent efficiency to glucose. So 120g = around 69 to 70 grams of glucose to add into your numbers.

HOLY CRAP. That is a lot of carbs! I had no idea. So I would have to have less than 85g of protein (which would provide me with 49g of CHO?) that seems pretty low, but then again, everything I used to know has been turned on its head. Maybe I can train hard on that amount. Wacky.

Yeah, like I said, I'm going to tweak things after the event. I have some ideas that I'd like to try out, crazy ideas have been coming to me while I'm in the bath, for now I am happy with my increased recovery the CT has provided.

### GigiPatata — 2012-03-16T19:46:02+0200

I hear you about not being tempted. Although there are still some things that tempt me, last week DH was eating some store-bought cookies and "felt bad" eating them in front of me. I told him I wasn't in the least tempted - that they looked like sugary sawdust to me - he couldn't believe it - normally I would have eaten at least a few with him. Had they been beautiful homemade cookies I may have been tempted - I still have a ways to go, I guess.

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-16T19:47:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1619>

Ketosis does not happen overnight, you have to go days sometimes a week before it kicks in and then you have to stay there. Have a day that goes above 50 grams of carbs and you just set yourself back a few days.

Please do not take this the wrong way but at your height and weight 50 grams is nothing for you in the morning. Your total protein intake should be around 100 -150 grams a day.

I suggest you up your protein in the morning along with fat. Remember if you cut the carbs out you need to replace those calories or you will just put yourself too deep into a deficit. Also eat a bigger late meal. That should hold off any midnight cravings.

Think about this- using the high end of my suggestion 150 grams of protein a day that is still only 600 calories. Add to that 75 grams of fat and that is another 675 for a total of 1275. If you are eating less then that you are really not getting enough food.

I know Dr. says calories don't matter but if you have not made the switch from LR to LS and you still haven't adapted to CT then they do!

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-16T20:10:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1632>

> **JC2K;1615 wrote:** If you're got an event coming up, definitely don't change things at the last minute. Great way to fail. Protein converts at a 58 percent efficiency to glucose. So 120g = around 69 to 70 grams of glucose to add into your numbers.

I am going to disagree with you on this number from the stand point that you will only get that type of conversion if you are eating a diet completely void of any carb source. If you are still getting in 25 grams of carbs a day you can be in ketosis without causing gluconeogensis.

Also you need to distinguish between what you are taking in as food carbs and what is actually being turned into glucose in the body. Blood glucose numbers do not equate to gram consumption.

What are you basing the protein suggestion on? I've been trying to figure out for awhile now how much protein I should have. I've read that for ketosis it's actually important to keep protein down, but you're obviously saying the opposite. I'm 5'5 and about 118 lbs, so not a lot of weight to lose, really I just want to get toned. I'm more doing the Rx and CT to straighten out hormonal issues, not so much deal with weight.

I generally stick to the 50 g protein BAB and have a total of 90-100 g of protein for the day. My fat intake is usually about 110-120 g/day. Am I way off??

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-16T20:23:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1635>

> **BJK77;1636 wrote:** What are you basing the protein suggestion on? I've been trying to figure out for awhile now how much protein I should have. I've read that for ketosis it's actually important to keep protein down, but you're obviously saying the opposite. I'm 5'5 and about 118 lbs, so not a lot of weight to lose, really I just want to get toned. I'm more doing the Rx and CT to straighten out hormonal issues, not so much deal with weight.
>
>
>
> I generally stick to the 50 g protein BAB and have a total of 90-100 g of protein for the day. My fat intake is usually about 110-120 g/day. Am I way off??

I base my assumptions on that it is a known fact the body needs protein (amino acids) to stay in an anabolic state. It has been established that .8 to 1.0 grams of protein is needed per 1 pound of lean mass.

Since most people do not know their body fat percentage, a general rule is .8 per pound of weight. so at 118 pounds x .8 = 94 grams of protein a day so I would say you are in the zone.

### GigiPatata — 2012-03-16T21:09:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1653>

> **Continuum Fitness;1621 wrote:** Ketosis does not happen overnight, you have to go days sometimes a week before it kicks in and then you have to stay there. Have a day that goes above 50 grams of carbs and you just set yourself back a few days.
>
>
>
> Please do not take this the wrong way but at your height and weight 50 grams is nothing for you in the morning. Your total protein intake should be around 100 -150 grams a day.
>
>
>
> I suggest you up your protein in the morning along with fat. Remember if you cut the carbs out you need to replace those calories or you will just put yourself too deep into a deficit. Also eat a bigger late meal. That should hold off any midnight cravings.
>
>
>
> Think about this- using the high end of my suggestion 150 grams of protein a day that is still only 600 calories. Add to that 75 grams of fat and that is another 675 for a total of 1275. If you are eating less then that you are really not getting enough food.
>
>
>
> I know Dr. says calories don't matter but if you have not made the switch from LR to LS and you still haven't adapted to CT then they do!

Just looking at your math again - for me my 50g protein breakfast itself = 650 calories (2 eggs, 6 thin strips bacon, 1/4 lb pork) plus 2TB CO = close to 900 calories total.

But my other meals have been much lower in protein and thus lower cal, so your point is still taken.

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-16T21:18:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1654>

> **GigiPatata;1655 wrote:** Just looking at your math again - for me my 50g protein breakfast itself = 650 calories (2 eggs, 6 thin strips bacon, 1/4 lb pork) plus 2TB CO = close to 900 calories total.
>
>
>
> But my other meals have been much lower in protein and thus lower cal, so your point is still taken.

I am only counting total protein for the day 150 grams of protein x 4 cals/gram = 600 calories

two eggs = 10 grams of protein.(40 cals) total 140 calories for two eggs

6 bacon strips = 17 grams of protein.(68 cals) total 250 calories for 6 strips

total protein for your breakfast is 27 grams or 108 calories

CO has no protein and totals 260 calories of SF.

so your breakfast is 650 calories with 108 being protein

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-16T21:35:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-1658>

Easiest way to cut carbs is to eat more healthy fat, IMO.

Slow progress. Hmmm....I think you need labs. You should have made a bit more progress by now. Think about sleep, stress, and digestion, too. Get CBC, CMP, TSH, cortisol, DHEA, hs-CRP, a1c, magnesium, and Vitamin D for starters. My gut feeling is that something is holding you back...hormones?

### Glen PDQ — 2012-11-24T14:32:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-65504>

For the persons who have been eating paleo/epipaleo without weight loss, it is possible to lose inches without losing weight. It has something to do with keeping or gaining muscle mass while losing body fat. I broke through one of my plateaus by increasing the BAB amount of fat and protein. I figure if there is not enough meat protein in the meal then my body might start breaking down its own muscle.

Could lack of weight loss be a fluid retention problem? I was reading up on the 8 different forms of vitamin E and found that a metabolite of 2 forms of vitamin E (gamma tocopherol and gamma tocotrienol) is LLU-alpha. LLU-alpha helps get rid of excess fluid and can lower blood pressure.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-24T14:57:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-help-getting-below-50g-carbs.212/post-65508>

There is also another point to ponder Glen. I've been low/no carbing for 2 years. Went paleo last year and have not lost an ounce. It turns out I have too much reverse T3. Which means I have been hypothyroid for decades but the docs all missed it because my TSH and T3 look "normal." My advice to anyone who isn't losing and is following either of those eating plans is to get a full thyroid panel just to be sure. If you aren't losing and aren't eating SAD there is something else going on. Keep looking.

### ASHO — 2012-03-22T03:20:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/picture-of-keynote-speaker-dr-kruse-at-paleofx.214/post-4201>

More information about Paleo RX 2012:

[http://perfecthealthdiet.com/?p=5724](http://http://perfecthealthdiet.com/?p=5724)

"Paleo FX was a terrific meeting. It’s not easy to stage such a large event. Kudos to Michelle and Keith Norris, Kevin Cottrell, and the 100 volunteers who worked so hard to make it a success. Jack Kruse in his keynote speech talked of “paying it forward,” and the volunteers and organizers certainly did that.

Jack is a better speaker than writer, and I enjoyed the talk. He recounted a story of a woman he met as a surgical resident. She had an inoperable cancer; Jack closed the incision in the operating room, and she lived for another six months. She willed her wine collection to Jack, and each day before she died she took a single bottle from each case to the beach and wrote a letter to Jack explaining its provenance and what it meant to her. The package of letters came along with the wine after her death, and in a note she mentioned the story of the Old Man and the Starfish, and told Jack, “You are my starfish.” She advised him to withdraw from the rat race and enjoy life, as she had in her final six months. Jack didn’t pay much heed to that advice until 2006. Then, after reflection and perhaps some cold thermogenesis, he realized that he ought to “pay it forward” by working “transformational change.” At this point he pulled out what he said was a stick of dynamite and a lighter:

He said that the dynamite could work transformational change on the University of Texas, and it was our obligation to go out in the world and work transformational change by converting others to Paleo.

Luckily the fire marshal was not present, or PaleoFX might have come to a premature end."

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-22T14:03:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/picture-of-keynote-speaker-dr-kruse-at-paleofx.214/post-4307>

Thanks for sharing! I cannot wait to see and hear more from PaleoFX. Was it announced where it will be held next year?

She had an inoperable cancer; Jack closed the incision in the operating room, and she lived for another six months. She willed her wine collection to Jack, and each day before she died she took a single bottle from each case to the beach and wrote a letter to Jack explaining its provenance and what it meant to her. The package of letters came along with the wine after her death, and in a note she mentioned the story of the Old Man and the Starfish, and told Jack, “You are my starfish.” She advised him to withdraw from the rat race and enjoy life, as she had in her final six months. Jack didn’t pay much heed to that advice until 2006. Then, after reflection and perhaps some cold thermogenesis, he realized that he ought to “pay it forward” by working “transformational change.” At this point he pulled out what he said was a stick of dynamite and a lighter:

**Here is a video highlight of PaleoFX12 from Abel Bascom (one of Dr. K's PaleoFX12 Top Ten Moments):

**

"If you couldn’t make the trip to Austin for PaleoFX, here’s a little taste of all the fun we had.

Hanging out with Mark Sisson, Dean Dwyer (see the end of the video, you silly Canadian), Dr. Jack Kruse, Dave Asprey, Jimmy Moore, Kevin Cottrell, Shirley, Keith Norris, and all the other masters of the paleo domain was an absolute blast. To all of you who made it possible, thank you and congratulations on pulling off such a killer event.

Another note: thanks for all of the compliments on our silly CavemanThink shirts – we are super encouraged. Alyson and I will be printing them soon. If you want to enter to win a free shirt, hop on over to our new baby, CavemanThink.com and sign up.

Alright, here’s the unofficial PaleoFX highlight reel…

[http://www.fatburningman.com/paleofx-was-awesome-heres-proof-video/](http://http://www.fatburningman.com/paleofx-was-awesome-heres-proof-video/)

# Thread: Mike Matarazzo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 96
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Riversedge — 2012-03-17T06:54:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-1872>

Ok, started this Leptin Rx on march 2nd. I was 312 lbs weighed in today march 16th at 296 lbs. This is going so great its a little scary. I'm not craving anything although until today I was making and eating everyday a paleo chili i made up, but did not know tomato's were a no no even though the list was in the matrix I have.

So today was a great day I did some CT for the last 3 days and its going well just showers but the water here is almost 40 degrees so it does the job I'm up to about 5 minutes taking a deep breathe works for the shock although the shock is fading.

I still have not started working out (which makes my weight loss even greater) because of a dislocated shoulder and clavicle which is the reason or excuse for the weight gain of 60 lbs in 4 months. Surgery in a month unreal how the ins. company delays.

I will stay on this path for the rest of my life.

Mike

### Hotmail — 2012-03-17T11:19:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-1898>

Well done Mike, great start and great results.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-18T05:39:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-2305>

Thanks Hotmail...

Another good day today. 3 eggs and chicken sausage patties for breakfast. Had a late lunch I wasnt hungry until 4 so my late lunch was also dinner. I had Sirloin steak about 14 oz and a bunch of asparagus W/coconut oil and garlic sauce.

My diet Rockstar addiction still lives on i will slowly wean myself off it (mostly for the caffeine) soon. I had my first craving and it was huge, for the grapes in the fridge so i had a few bites of chicken and 2 huges glasses of water. No CT today i will get back to it in the morning.

Well until next time folks i wish you all the best stay strong and chilled.

### adam_ — 2012-03-18T22:12:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-2456>

Mike,

Great to see that there are other guys making progress with these approaches - well done.

What's the issue with tomatoes, I've never heard of that. Where can I get hold of a copy of this "matrix"?

Thanks,

### Riversedge — 2012-03-19T05:20:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-2607>

Adam,

Thanks for the compliment. The Matrix is on Robbwolf.com unfortunately tomato's are a nightshade and Dr. Kruse does not use them and I want his results so I will follow it to a T. Adam also a great book to get is Paleo Solution by Robb Wolf i have read it cover to cover at least three times.

I did paleo for six months last year leading up to my Triathlon. Unfortunately I fell at work and dislocated my shoulder and Clavicle a month before. I was devastated and went into my six month funk. I think Dr Kruse has th perfect approach for me. I first heard him on the Paleo Summit and it seemed as if he was speaking right to me. Now here I am trying to get through all the blogs forums, studying and understanding the quilt.

I had another great day on the RX. Steak for breakfast, the last of my paleo chili (which is off the menu because of the tomato's) for lunch and leftover steak and sausage patties for dinner. I did pick up 3 pounds of haddock with every intention of having it for dinner but time was not on my side. CT was 6 minutes in 45 deg shower hoping to get in the ocean soon its a half mile down the street but I just don't think I'm ready for that yet.

until next time Stay frosty friends.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-19T20:50:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-2876>

Well, I took my before Pictures I forgot to take at the start. I have already lost 17 lbs and good thing I did'nt take them 3 weeks ago because I am totally disgusted with myself. I cant believe how bad I look. Not another piece of bread, pizza, grain or carb of any kind until I look like I used too. CT for sure tonight

### Riversedge — 2012-03-21T03:43:19+0200

Today was a great day for my reset. I don't weigh myself everyday, just every Friday. today's menu was 5 egg breakfast, 1lb of steak tips for lunch and the lb of haddock is cooking on the grill. I did CT this morning and I will do it before bed, I'm still on the showers the water out of the tap was 48 so my skin is numb coming out.

The other adventure I had today was my MRI on the Shoulder with dye shot into the joint. very painful but hopefully will give the Surgeons a better look at the damage so I can move on with surgery or get back into working out.

I hope everyone had a great day today.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-28T04:30:18+0300

Well, today went ok i stayed strict even tried to kick my diet energy drink habit I did cut it out for most of the day but what a caffeine headache could not pick my head off the pillow in the late afternoon so I broke down and had a Rockstar damn it. I will wean off instead of cold turkey. I dont like coffee so this is what I have used for my little boost over so many years. The headache prevented me from going to the butcher today for some fresh meat. ### Jim — 2012-03-28T05:46:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-7576>

Mike,

You are doing great. I used to drink lots of Diet Coke for the caffeine too. I weaned my self down to two drinks per day over a few weeks. Then after a while on the diet I totally lost the craving for it. One day I just stopped. Stay on the diet and stay away from all artificial sweeteners. One day you'll no longer feel the need and just stop too.

Good luck.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-29T22:23:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-8452>

Thanks Jim I appreciate the support. I had a busy day yesterday stayed on the good path. Rotisserie chicken on salad with skin of course. Dinner I had a piece of sirloin and prime rib i made for the first time. I had an awesome CT session yesterday.

Today is a different story... I had a great breakfast 4 eggs and 5 slices of thick cut bacon. Lunch I had pineapple ugh. I wasn't going to eat but not sure what came over me the same old tired mind games with myself. I did squash that "well its over now" thinking and let it go.

I'm going to have seafood tonight just hope I can cook it better this time (I hate seafood). I will get a CT session in today at some point the tap water here is back under 50. I ordered all the supplements last night and I will start them when delivered. I have been taking lots of D, but seeing I don't like seafood I need to get the fish oils in.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-29T23:25:17+0300

### Riversedge — 2012-03-31T05:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-9093>

Happy Friday all. Today was a good day lunch with my daughter. The University food is a disappointment I had to resort to pork loin and greens. I will write a letter telling them my concerns. I think the meal plan was $2700 this year and in no way was it worth it. Pizza, tacos, subs a great salad bar but NO bacon or eggs really. I hope dinner is set up better who knows. Anyway I had chicken wings for dinner I was going to have a Nice big Porter house but the gas on the grill ran out.

I had The best CT session yet this morning! Granted I'm still just doing the showers but our water is at 47 so it does the trick. I stayed in for at least 20 solid minutes no flinching or ill effects. It didn't end there I came up with an Idea to open up the window (second floor as not to scar the neighbors) and not dry off while shaving and brushing my teeth. It was as if I was still in the shower at 34 degree wind hitting me. That is the first time I actually shivered since starting CT.

Stay Frosty Friends

### Riversedge — 2012-04-02T04:01:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-10107>

I hope you all had a great weekend....I know I did. I had great CT sessions this weekend for the first time I stayed cold for hours after. I dont know if that is a good thing but I was just billowing heat all weekend. The water dropped back to about 48 this weekend and one session it was 47 a few degrees makes a huge difference it seems. The eating was great. Saturday I had eggs and Bacon for breakfast, flank steak for lunch and steak tip on greens for dinner with some chicken. Today was similar breakfast Eggs and bacon, no lunch and Paleo Chili for dinner.

My one month Leptin RX weigh in is Tuesday! I know the number doesn't matter, as I am seeing progress in the mirror big time. I still '"Do Not have any Cravings" this is the FIRST time in my life I can say that, it just makes me smile. ~I'm Krusing~

Stay Frosty Friends

### Riversedge — 2012-04-10T02:04:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-13180>

Wow, its been six days since my last forum post. The flu like symptoms vanished the next day, toxic dump? I had a great weekend as far as staying 100%. I had ham yesterday. I had a slight craving my Dad's apple pie but I didn't touch it. Never mind the manicotti and homemade sauce with his made from scratch meatball's. The Diet has been solid and 100% but I lost 2 pounds this week I am always hoping for more as do most of us but I was good with that.

The CT is going Great the water runs between 47 and 51 here out of the tap. I can stand there all day it seems. I am going to take it up a notch with the CT.

I finally have my surgery date April 19th. I will start today with a more intense CT and see if it makes the surgery go a little easier.

The next thing I need to attack is my sleep, its all over the place. I have the room pitch black and still lay there waiting to fall asleep. I started reading the Monk that sold his Ferrari last night and fell into a deep sleep after about 40 pages so I will do the same tonight only much earlier.

Stay Frosty friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-289 ~4/6/12

### Riversedge — 2012-04-14T03:31:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-14976>

Thanks Kiwi and Lyndra, I had another great week. I cant Believe I have not touched a Carb in almost 6 weeks! No cravings like I said thats a first for me. I wish I was working out but that will have to what for another 6 months or until I get the OK after rehab.

The sleep is all over the place but the past few nights I had to knock myself out with a few Benedryl's I'm hoping that will get me back on track. I think I'm a little anxious about the surgery also so that's not helping.

The CT is going great I now enjoy it and I'm not bothered by the freezing water. I actually look forward too it. I do think the shower is better I don't really feel the bath. I have heard people say if your still in the bath your body creates a barrier of the heated water who knows. All I know is the constant flow of 48 degree water is awesome and freezing. I do let the tub fill during so I'm up to half the calf my feet being cold seems to make the body colder.

My Diet has been spot on (except the diet rockstar) I still seem to need the caffeine. I had my weekly weigh in today and I'm down 4#'s this week I love it.

Stay Frosty my friends

CW-285 ~4/13/12

The CT is amazing 25 minutes in the water at 49 degree's. Spot CTing the shoulder to get it ready for surgery. It will be 88 here tomorrow I have my pre-Op and hoping to walk down to the beach and try the 46 degree water.

The Boston Marathon is tomorrow and the are asking people to sit it out. I thought they were the healthy ones? Maybe they should start a winter one. I'm sure when I get home, I'll here how many people dropped or Unfortunately died. They will need lots of ambulance's on heart break hill.

Thank you all. Hitting the rack now. I have everything covered for the recovery. I will keep in touch here when I can. I do have high expectations for a quick and smooth recovery.

The other day I smacked my arm on the door knob when the door bounced back. I've done CT multiple times a day since getting the two huge bruises, one on the bicep and one on the forearm. ### Riversedge — 2012-04-21T04:56:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-17315>

Hey everyone, thanks for the nice thoughts very much appreciated.

Well just a quick note here. Surgery went well pain yesterday post was a little harsh. The Pain block that i didn't really want but after talking to many folks I had. The problem was They said I have strange anatomy? They could not pinpoint the nerves so they just injected where they thought it would be. Needless to say they found the wrong nerves even as of today part of my neck, trap and chest are still numb. So no block on the shoulder area, I now know = pain.

The surgery went as expected, except the damage was less then thought from my contrasted MRI three weeks ago. The fraying bicep was minor, the tear in the supraspinatus and labrum were aaaaahhhhh gone! So CTing for the past 6 weeks and i cranked up the CT for the weeks leading up to surgery. I will ask more info on the damage seen in the MRI and what was seen when he went in. I didnt have a chance to chat with him after I was real out of it.

I left with Oxycodone made me sick so I was giving Vicodin later at night. I had extreme pain after i got home and for about 10 hours on. I took the Vicodin at 10 pm and 2 am with motrin in the in between hours. Today I felt incredibly good upon waking. I got up at 5:30am had lots of chicken for breakfast and only have had Motrin today and my last one was 8 hours ago. I had a great CT session today. I had the shower water directly on the shoulder for at least 10 of the 25 min at 48\*. I was actually colder than usually and light headed in this session not sure why maybe all the garbage pumped in me.

The list of meds yesterday was unreal General anesthesia, Fentnyl, versette and morphin yikes. One of the Incisions was left open I guess for drainage. The bone chips and spurs were taken care of and I think was the main reason for the pain. I will know more on April 30th. I cant help but think that the CT had an major part in the healing. I cant explain the tears not being there i hope my surgeon can. I will tell him about what I have been doing. I told the nurse all about it yesterday before surgery, but I think she was just listen and pacifying me. You can lead a horse.

Well, I'm off to sleep four recliners no waiting. I can't believe How much I miss my bed.

Stay Frosty Friends

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-21T06:07:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-17328>

CT heals muscle......and its reprodible in humans. I use it all the time in spine surgery

### Riversedge — 2012-04-22T04:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-17627>

Well, My shoulder feels pretty good. The rest of me is a mess. I'm pretty sure all the meds on the day of the surgery are messing with my system. I didnt sleep more than 2 hours last night. I'm agitated, back is sore, my neck is sore not sure what they did to me. My stomach is the worst, haven't had much to eat i'm getting some food in. The constipation has finally ended.

My CT session was great today 30 minutes and I was freezing. The ice has never left my shoulder since the surgery for more than a half hour. I did the Pendulum exercises today and that went well. I did a little stretching. Hoping for a better day on Sunday.

Stay Frosty Friends

### Riversedge — 2012-04-23T02:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-17932>

Thanks Lyndra, Its been a long weekend but productive.

Let me start by saying I'm CTing the rest of my life. I can not believe the healing process CT brings about. The movement I have in my Shoulder and arm is amazing. I haven't taken any narcotics since early yesterday morning. I took 1 Motrin 800 today. I have also been spot CTing all day, the shoulder wrap with straps is awesome. I'm thinking of starting a company with a hot tub that has a cooling system, I'll call it a Jackruzzi.

They told me at least 6 months of PT. I'm so much more optimistic about a fast recovery. Thanks to Jack Kruse, the Malibu Triathlon in September just may see me compete.

### Riversedge — 2012-04-25T04:16:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-18592>

Thanks Kathy, I've had some good and bad days. The past few have been tough. I have started doing a lot more movement which of course brings with it pain. I have taken some Vicodin the past few days. The CT today was good but didnt feel as great as it has maybe the healing process. I haven't been eating a lot a few days just one meal but paleo. I will be making my Paleo Chili tomorrow, I need some nutrients and I load the chili with kale and spinach.

I lost a 1Lb this week well last week I didnt expect to lose much but I'll take it. I have had problems sleeping also. I have always slept on the surgery side so it will a while I guess.

Stay Frosty Friends

CW-284 ~4/20/12

### Riversedge — 2012-04-25T07:03:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-18626>

Thanks Nonchalant I didn't see your comment before. Its good to be back and catching up.

### Riversedge — 2012-04-27T05:22:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-19253>

Well, its been a rough week but I'm making progress. I have had a great week of CT and LRX doing at least 2 sessions of CT a day the Shoulder getting the most attention. I have gone back and forth with the pain Meds, when I do have pain if possible I hit the cold water.

I went on my Surgeons website (which is very cool) and found out exactly what he did. Three Procedures, most being bone removal and burring down the AC and Clavicle removal of the (bursa sac?) and tendon removal and reattachment. I will ask more questions Monday as to why the Supraspanatis was not fixed, I'm thinking the tear was not more than 50% and if so what kind of improvement i had since the contrasted MRI. I do have some great range of motion already hand can already go above my shoulder and I can touch my lower back (sounds easy) with my hand. I do get waves of pain and I'm pretty sure its the bone healing I have broken over 20 bones in my life and the pain is similar. This surgery is harder than the knee in some aspects.

My LRX has been great but with full disclosure I did have a steak and cheese sub the night after the surgery. The only other problem this week is not eating after waking. Sometimes I would not eat until almost noon but that has changed back to BAB. I blame the drugs mostly, as it has messed up my stomach and elimination.

WHEW, that's all for now. My weigh in day is tomorrow, I'm hoping to break the 30lb's lost mark.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-284 ~4/20/12

GW-201

### Riversedge — 2012-04-28T04:53:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-19515>

Well, today was an ok day. I did not sleep more than 2 hours last night today was needless to say a lay around lazy day. The shoulder acted up a lot today.

The good news today is I'm officially down 30lbs in eight weeks with just a few cravings (and not acting on them) the whole time. I had an awesome CT session. Even though I had a bad day, CT made it better. I will go to sleep early tonight and rest up.

LRX went great later in the week. Earlier in the week was a nightmare late bab and didnt eat enough. I need to get out of this house!

Have a great weekend all.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-282 ~4/27/12

GW-201
