### Riversedge — 2012-05-03T02:48:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-20864>

Hey All, Thanks for the great encouragement and thoughts.

Everything is going well in every aspect of my recovery. I have had a great week with Leptin reset and CT. I even have my son doing CT now which is great. I am also trying to get him into the Paleo lifestyle, he is 19 and said he will try his best. I bought him a TRX last year and he is in great shape but wants to see his abs pop. I am just interested in him being healthy, but he is 19 and as you know we were all invincible then or so we thought.

I will be hitting the ocean on Friday (the water is at 52) after all its at the end of the street and the shower water is starting to warm (bummer) it was 56 today. I still spot CT all day with the ice on my shoulder it helps with the pain.

I received my Blue blocker glasses and started wearing them at night. Also I have the F.lux app on my laptop now, Thanks to Kevin Cottrells guest blog on CT11. What an amazing story and results, he is truly inspiring.

I hope this note finds everyone well and Optimal.

I very much appreciate all of you and the support here.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-282 ~4/27/12

GW-201

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-03T02:54:58+0300

We appreciate you too, Mike. Thanks for the update. You're doing so well. My surgery is scheduled for the 29th, or earlier if a spot opens up.

I've ordered blue blocker glasses too. I have the F.lux. It's so fun to see my screen switch to pink.

Onward to optimal health!

### kcorcoran@comcast.net — 2012-05-03T03:07:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-20867>

### Riversedge — 2012-05-05T04:42:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-21500>

Thank you.

It's been a good week some ups and downs with the shoulder. The cold seems to be the only thing that really helps the pain. The Vicodin does not seem to do as well. I did take a few more but have noticed the difference when I do the CT. Yesterday and today were terrible, because I had my first PT session yesterday and she killed me. She did get my arm all the way straight over my head which is good in a recovery way but painful.

CT was awesome today. I was in the shower today it was 55 and I had three ice blocks (frozen water jugs) in the tub as it filled. After a 15 minute shower I slipped into the bath for 45 minutes. I had 2 of the blocks on my chest and stomach (w/compression shirt) that's the first time I've done that and now I'm hooked. I was going in the ocean today but the weather was terrible. I will be out of town this weekend so I will try for the ocean monday.

The Leptin reset is going very well. More fish needs to come into my diet. I'll have to start hitting the fish restaurants here they are very good and god knows I still cant make it taste good. I did get more greens in this week. I'm down another 3lbs this week and noticing more changes in my body. I am getting more muscular which still is a trip for me because I haven't done any moving at all just in the CT and out. I don't fully understand it but I love it. I think my hormones (for once in my life) are starting to align.

UPDATE: Wooo Hoo I will be out of town but i was just informed I will have a huge JACKRUZZI in the room with ice down the hall. That = as many great CT sessions as i can get in. I (while she is at the Rick Springfield concert) will be totally chilling out...Literally!

Stay Frosty Friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-279 ~5/4/12

GW-201

### Riversedge — 2012-05-08T04:13:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-22075>

I hope everyone had a great weekend I know I did. The CT sessions in the jacuzzi tub were great. The water was at an even 50 and I was in for two 30 minute sessions on Saturday. Sunday we slept in so I was not able to do another. I did a shower CT at home on Sunday the water was 55 and 40 minutes. Today was 35 min at 55 degrees.

The Leptin reset was good but again full disclosure I cheated Saturday night.Johhny Rockets Double Cheddar Cheeseburger and a chocolate shake. I know I waont do this again the bun and choc shake killed my stomach all night. Scary how my body has changed those used to be a staple binge food for me. I was back to it Sunday and today.

Rain for the next three days so I will shoot for the ocean Thursday its 51.2 today.

I will be rejoining the gym tomorrow legs legs legs, until my shoulder is ready and the Dr. gives me the OK.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-279 ~5/4/12

GW-201

### Lyndra — 2012-05-08T06:04:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-22123>

I just experienced a similar lesson myself after ingesting some wheat. Won't do it again. That's what life's lessons teach us. It is remarkable how what used to be tolerated seemingly without problem now appears to be quite toxic. Keep at it with the CT.

### Riversedge — 2012-05-09T01:25:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-22410>

Lyndra, That was a tough lesson for me also.

I am happy not to be one of the folks here with the leaky gut, Diabetes or any other chronic problems. I have looked at my past BG levels and I am shocked at some of the numbers I see. I didnt realize or simple didnt know the implications of such high numbers. I did like the decline in BG numbers to the 80's, 90's and once 75. So not only has Jacks RX bringing me closer to an LS life. Obviously I was pre Diabetic from the numbers I see in front of me (thats scares the \*\*\*\* out of me). I witnessed one of my favorite people when i was young (my great uncle Mike) lose toes then feet then legs and finally pass away from this destructive disease. Looks like I found Jack just in time. I think what saved me over the years was a job that had extreme physical demands.

To anyone who is Interested, I have done Paleo before (last spring and summer) and it worked well. The major observation and difference between Paleo and the Leptin RX With CT (and that is crucial) are the cravings. On the Paleo alone I did lose weight but I always had a plateau I could not break, the 255 mark. The cravings were extreme and I think the CT has crushed them.

People can attack Dr. Kruse all they want. I know what my results are. Will they work for them? I don't know. You will never know if you just attack him without actual data of your own. I have told Jack many times that I was drawn here by the summit and I just related to his story. I think his paragraph about people who have never been Obese, thinking they know the journey or the psychological stressors we have to navigate are simply delusional.

I'm done ranting sorry. I re-joined the gym today and as you may or may not know I can only do legs. I can say I was not ready for the 8 month lay off weakness.

I do have to tell you I have never blogged before (and it probably shows). I only started blogging because I felt it would help me with this journey, and it truly. One byproduct i hope for is it helps just one person here. Whether it be to simple start the pathway or some sort of Inspiration to keep plugging away. I am thankful for the people here that have inspired me many times.

So I have a favor to ask. I have always loved the notion of "Pay it forward" and do so in anyway I can. I remember reading here in the trenches of this web site. Someones story of how they are following the pathway but eating cans of tuna everyday and struggling to make ends meet. I would like to offer a delivery of Us Wellness meats to be delivered to them. I am not sure how to go about it or even if they would except it. It would be my small way of paying forward and I owe that not only to them but to myself.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 279 ~ 5/4/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T18:47:15+0300

### Riversedge — 2012-05-13T09:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-23624>

Thanks Lyndra. Jack I keep an open mind with everyone but quickly become a skeptic when I hear the same old things ive heard for years.

I didnt lose any weight this week not sure why but thats ok its been falling every week for two months.

CT is going fantastically ice bath with blocks of ice today .

It has been a tough weekend Boston University lost three incredible kids yesterday. My daughter is very upset and I told her we are fragile and to live everyday like its your last. I think I'll take my own advice. As you can see I'm still up at 3am I just hate my mind racing I know she is in her dorm finishing her paper but I wish she was here in her room.

Stay Frosty Friends

### nonchalant — 2012-05-14T02:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-23778>

Mike, I know exactly how you feel. My son is in Afghanistan. You have to let them live their lives.

### Riversedge — 2012-05-15T22:26:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-24338>

Hey Nonchalant, I do let them live their own lives even though I tweak there thinking now and again. Your son is in our thoughts we thank him for his service to our Nation.

I had a little set back with the shoulder at PT. We are not sure what happened but I think she just bent it to far back to early and pop. I don't know what it was hoping just inflammation the next few visit will tell the story. I came homein serious pain and went to my trusted friend CT. I also followed that with the Vicodin I haven't taken for a while. Seems better today.

Last week I did not lose any weight I didn't gain any either. I will be going to the gym every other day now but still only legs and Ab's. I could do the other bicep, tricep and shoulder but I'm not sure about throwing off my symmetry and balance.

Any Thoughts on this anyone?

My eating remains the same Leptin reset I did have berries W/whey smoothie one morning but that was it for carb's besides Veggies. My FBG still remains optimal. I still never get a Ketone reading any higher than trace or Negative. I will be making a yearly physical appointment for the beginning of June with a request of all the pertinent test's. I know she will be happy with the weight loss but she doesn't know about the shoulder yet. I will let her in on the Protocol I have been following

Well, here's to another great week. I WILL make it in the ocean by the end of the week. I can still smell low tide I will wait for high tide for sure. I am allergic to seaweed and the last time it hit me in the face I looked like Rocky Balboa after the first fight.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 279 ~ 5/11/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Lyndra — 2012-05-15T22:36:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-24344>

So sorry to hear about the setback. CT will be your ally with that.

I think you'll be fine just doing lower-body workouts for a while. I broke my wrist last December and did mostly lower body but also did what I could with upper body. I found a study that said that your injured arm will gain in strength even when it idly watches while your other does a workout. So much strength gain is neurological so that makes sense to me. Plus it's good mental stimulation trying to figure out how to adapt your routine.

I hope you get better soon. Keep hanging in there.

### Riversedge — 2012-05-29T17:55:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-28178>

Hey all, I took some time off from posting but I'm back. My shoulder is slightly improved except for the muscle that runs to my mid-back from my rear delt. I will know more monday at my follow up with the surgeon.

I have been eating well trying to tweak a few things. I am still (3 weeks in a row) at 279lbs. The only thing I changed is doing heavy leg workouts twice a week so maybe inflammation, muscle gain or just a systemic adjustment.

CT is going very well I have been in the ocean almost everyday this past week and weekend. The ocean temp runs between 52-56. I have an infrared temp gun of course with the sun the closer to shore the warmer, so I tread out at least 100 yards first. I have been staying in for an hour or so one day 90 minutes. I also have gone in for 45 and came out read for a bit then back in for another session.

I hope to connect with the groups going for Ocean swims in Rhode Island, NY and California It would be great to meet some fellow Cter's.

I hope you all had a great long weekend and are looking forward to a great summer.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 279 ~ 5/25/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Lyndra — 2012-05-29T18:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-28182>

Good to hear from you again! I wish we had an ocean nearby - salt water just has its own feel. Let us know how the follow-up goes.

### Riversedge — 2012-06-03T17:44:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-29214>

Thanks Lyndra, I see the surgeon Monday morning but it has not improved much unfortunately. I have been doing my CT in the ocean and I love it. So much better then standing in a shower or laying in the tub. I did get seaweed in my eyes and of course that night my eyes swelled shut. Up at 3am for benadryl that was Thursday finally today it's almost gone. The eating is going the same I am trying to incorporate more fish but. I just can't stand it, I will keep trying.

The Weight has finally started moving again. Down 2lbs this week so the plateau has been broken! Movement will be ramped up this week training needs to start for the Triathlon in september. Whether I can compete or not is still in the air. The swim training will have to wait until the shoulder is fully healed. The one positive is I am a very strong swimmer and will need very little training. I will do breathing exercises all summer in the ocean which will help a lot.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 277 ~ 6/1/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-04T02:59:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-29284>

For water temps I just bring a meat thermometer.... Simple! Best of luck on your recovery

### Riversedge — 2012-06-06T03:02:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-29650>

The Surgeon visit went well. I was concerned with the pop but he wasn't and said it was an adhesion/scar tissue. I got him to give me another Ice wrap which I love, now I have two. I am progressing well, he told me I need patience with this surgery. The typical recovery is 6 months its been 6 weeks. I guess I'm just anxious to get back into life. I still spot CT my shoulder all day long, no drug has helped with the pain like CT.

I had a great workout today legs and Ab's of course. I "tried" the use the left side upper body and the right will neurologically keep up, but It throws off my back and neck way to much so I will stick with legs. I will try to get on the bike this week the problem of course is the shoulder I will use my full suspension on the smooth bike path and ride with one hand if need be.

The CT has been awesome! I enjoy the ocean good thing the weather will be better going forward. I don't care if its raining its the nasty surf and undertow i worry about not having use of both arms. I am heading to Cape Cod for a few days the ocean will be nice and cold. I will also eat only fish while I'm away.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 277 ~ 6/1/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Riversedge — 2012-06-17T03:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-31679>

Hey All, It has been a very rough few weeks for me. I lost one of the greatest people I knew. My auntie Honey was like a second mother to me when I was young and she will be missed very much.

The Clavicle is acting up along with the shoulder. The Clavicle needs to be addressed by the Surgeon we shall see where that leads.

The Nutrition has been great, I did have black berries and cherries Tuesday but wow did it mess up my stomach. Ease back into fruit if you are going to, I think I will leave them out. I have lost a pound a week the past two weeks not much but heading in the right direction. I have been doing leg workouts for a few weeks and have gained muscle so we will go with that as the reason....

The CT.....Well, simply amazing. Nothing beats the ocean for it! I was in Connecticut (rocky point beach) earlier this week and the people thought I was insane. I stayed in for over an hour at 61\*. The pool is open and I have spent a few days in there at 67\* but today it was up to 70 so down the street I went. The ocean water was 60\* I was in for 50 minutes and yes the only one in the water. The insane looks continue. I stayed clear of the seaweed (allergic) as much as I could but the sea was angry my friends we shall see tonight.

Hope everyone is doing well and enjoying the Summer. I hope I can run into some folks out in Southern Cal I will be there for 3 weeks on Vacation.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 275 ~ 6/15/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Lyndra — 2012-06-18T00:43:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-31832>

Glad to see you checking in. I'm very sorry to hear about losing your aunt. Please accept my sympathies. That makes everything so hard.

Cool that you are getting into the ocean for your CT. Keep up all the good work.

### Riversedge — 2012-07-02T23:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-35128>

Thank you Lydra for the kind words.

I'm doing great the shoulder is finally making progress again. The clavicle has good days and bad we shall see. The CT has been great. I hit the ocean as much as possible which has been everyday unless we have thunder and lightning. The water runs around 60 give or take a few degree's I stay in for an hour if possible never less than a half hour. I also have the pool sometimes I journal the pool but often I'm in it for a few hours a day. The water temp is all over the place but right around 70.

I did see someone mention somewhere on here they are no longer sensitive to the sun with CT. I also am not burning and have nice even tan no peeling.

Paleo has been going as planned. I did try (last Saturday night) the cheat meal route. I had Chinese food and oh my god never again. I think it was the MSG and Carb combo but I thought my heart was going to beat out of my chest I was up all night. I cant believe I used to eat that a few times a month. I did drink at my nieces wedding but just vodka on the rocks no mixers and didnt take much two drinks and I was done.

The workouts are going great still just legs and abs with easy cardio but I have to ramp that up soon. The triathlon is still questionable although even if my shoulder is not 100% I'm a strong swimmer and have no fear I will just train my lungs in the water until then.

I lost two more pounds since last logging on. I seem to be down to losing a pound a week consistently and I'm fine with that. I am sure when I can start swinging the Kettlebell and get back to heavy lifting it will step up.

I finally found my journal I was 320 on Feb. 15th two weeks before starting this pathway. I was following Paleo but not the leptin RX.

I hope you are all enjoying your summer.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 273 ~ 6/29/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-02T23:47:27+0300

### Riversedge — 2012-07-09T22:10:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-36381>

Jack, I hope my experience can help. The results are amazing. I had my visit with the surgeon Friday, ten weeks out from surgery and my results are ahead of schedule. I have learned so much just having this Injury and following all the protocols. I actually think t5he recovery would have been even faster (in my layman's opinion) had the Sternoclavicular Joint been addressed earlier in my recovery which has been an excruciatingly long 10 months. I am sure it is messing with the mechanics of the shoulder and its stability. I will finally be getting an Ultrasound guided injection.

The eating has been great I finally tried IFing it went well. I had imagined I would pass out without food for 24 hours but with not eating carbs and still 0 cravings it was easy.

I am down 40 pounds since starting the Leptin reset 48 total.

CT has been great as always, the Ocean is amazing and will be integral part of the rest of my life. The side benefit is the golden tan I have and how my skin adapted to the sun on its own, without the chemically laden sunscreens.

I will be heading out to California for my yearly 3 week summer vacation. You better believe most of it will be in the Pacific Ocean from Malibu all the way down to the border at Imperial beach.

I hope you are all having an Excellent summer I know I am (minus the shoulder Injury). Off to the beach

### Riversedge — 2012-09-07T10:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-49940>

Hello all, well it's been a hell of a month literally. I have discovered through N=1 that CT for a fact in my experience curbs carb cravings. I made the mistake of stopping CT and caffeine at the same time to see where it would lead. The good news is I only gained 3 pounds which is monumental considering what I ate.

The caffeine addiction I had (sugar free Rockstar I don't like coffee) was brutal. The withdrawal was unbearable at times as I was up to 4 16oz cans a day. The headaches were extreme and took 3 weeks to improve, the fatigue is still an issue. I'm just hoping I didn't permenantly damage my adrenals.

That's it for now as you can sure see my sleep cycles are haywire also. On the upside My shoulder is almost fully healed 2months ahead of schedule thanks to CT.

Stay frosty friends

Riversedge

Ps: I'm off to California for another two weeks vacation on September 11th to watch the tri (shoulder could do it) but my body is not trained for it so I will be cheering them on from the sidelines.

Well, it's back to the primal ways. I went way off track had a nasty run in with the seaweed again and followed a devastating sad diet for three weeks. The damage 12 pounds and bloating, gas returned and I now know I can't drink alcohol any more.

The good news I have been cleared to work and I'm very excited. I need to do four weeks of work hardening. I am way ahead of them I started lifting weights again and boy does it feel good to be sore again, it's a great sore. The shoulder feels great some minor pain in certain positions but nothing I even notice. The shoulder held up well with military presses, lat pull downs side laterals but bench press still a little rough. I did some Kettlebell swings and they are awesome. I've never done them before but it will be a staple in my workouts.

I will be making great progress with this lifestyle now that I can put it all together. CT will be a daily ritual I cannot wait to see the results of ct and working out. I will stay 100% as I have in the past today is day two with the return of the pathway in my life.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-17T23:34:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2136>

I have some big reservations about this. Fat is prohibited in the protocol because it derails the pathway to the hypothalamus. I'm one of the moderators on the humongous international hcg forum (25,000+ members) and in the 3 1/2 years I've been on there, the ones who tend not to stabilize are the ones who do only a 23 day round or who had fat in their diet.

Now will CT fix that pathway that the fat messes up? I don't know. But you can be our guinea pig  I suspect you would have had this result without the hcg, using only ice though. Because WITH hcg you should have lost 20 pounds at least while doing the CT because Doc says it would step up the losses. So if you'd done the hcg protocol with CT you might have lost 30 pounds in those 4 weeks. Even if you were to the last few pounds. Contrary to what we read in Pounds and Inches you CAN lose more than is healthy because I've seen it several times on the forum.

So Trish I'm not sure it would be worth doing hcg with the CT. I really think the CT alone would do it if you only want reshaping and to lose a few pounds.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-18T02:14:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2189>

I might do a round of hcg with CT to see what it does.  I tend to be a slow loser, about 20 pounds in 40 days. It would be interesting to see if the CT increases the losses. I really don't think you need hcg in your case though since you didn't need to lose much. I think CT did the trick for you and would have done so without the hcg. I suspect the hcg has had nothing to do with how you're reshaping and losing inches. Lucky girl!  The reason I say this is because I've talked to others who have eaten a paleo diet with hcg and didn't lose much. But they didn't know about CT.

I actually love the protocol myself. I've lost 75 pounds and kept it off. I don't mind the restrictive diet. I think it helps a lot of people break out of the grip of certain foods. One little bit of fat added and I either gained or stalled in a round. So do most others which is why Simeon told us how important it was to avoid it. Yet as soon as the diet portion was over, fats are encouraged. So I know fat has an impact. I've seen the evidence on thousands of people too much to disbelieve it. The reason people can regain is grains. Americans love their grains. It's very hard to get people to realize how damaging grains are to our health. And of course wheat is in EVERYTHING.  Time and again we see people complain because they gain 4 pounds on pizza. They want to eat "normally." Well duh. They need to learn that their bodies can't run on junk.

We sure face a battle with educating people!

### caroline — 2012-03-18T03:26:15+0200

Hi Colleen! I have been following you for so long on the HCG forums. You are one busy lady!! > **caroline;2221 wrote:** That is so me - skinny fat and that elusive last 10 lbs!! I so want the scale to pleeeease go down!

@Caroline and PattyCakes, I am in the same boat as you. I have lost these 10 lbs on hcg last year and I gained it back in the last 7 months, it's hormonal gain with me because I went to paleo/keto on phase 3 when I finished my round. I eat a very clean diet and I gain 2-3 lbs with each TOM and it doesn't come back off again.

I am considering another hcg round with CT. I started CT a week or so ago and got cold urticaria when I added ice to my cold bath. Then, I took about 5 days off to heal from the hives and jumped back in again with face dunks, cold showers, and cold baths. I am up to 20 minutes cold bath at 53 degrees. So far I'm ok but I am staying away from ice for now, too scared of the hives. Tonight I didn't feel up to CT..too tired. I have been super fatigued with CT, which is weird. Even shopping today was tiring, and that is very unlike me.

Anyway given my strange hormonal gains, I am unsure whether to do hcg again, but I am so uncomfortable in this body. My body composition is actually worse now with this new gain than pre-hcg. I can't fit in my clothes, the 10 lbs makes a huge difference on me. I am just puffy.

ChimpChick, can you share with me also where you get your hcg? Perhaps I need a different kind. I did hcg sublingual Rx. Can you share more about how you are combining Leptin with hcg? I would be afraid to deviate from the Dr. S. protocol, so no fats for me, but I wouldn't eat fruits and grissini this time and I would definitely have meal 1 for breakfast and then an early dinner.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-18T03:42:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2236>

Oh my goodness! What a fabulous trip. I can't wait to hear all about it, Caroline! Why are you going to Tibet? Any special reason?

On the hhcg, some of the companies say they just changed what the label says but haven't changed the formula. I don't know if they are telling the truth or not though.

RBee, I really think a lot of the time the trouble people would have with stabilizing was that we all had been taught that eating every 2-3 hours was the thing we should do. Now we know better and I'm seeing that some who have had all kinds of trouble ease into much more stability with going 5 hours or more between meals. But yes if your hhcg wasn't doing what it should, you would have been losing on a starvation diet and it would have messed up your metabolism.

So, it's slow going for me...I am sure it will take a long time to cold adapt.

I am getting my thyroid checked soon, doc. appt is March 29. I have suspected a thyroid issue and wonder if the hcg made it worse somehow. On the other hand, CT and hcg might heal me. So many have said that hcg heals everything. I don't really know what to do.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-18T06:02:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2308>

Hcg actually helps thyroid problems while you're on it, Claudia, so that wouldn't be the cause. But you very likely still have an issue. Just the cold water should help. 53 degree water is cold.

Suzie, some doctors think hcg is damaged by b12 so I don't mix with it. I did for one round but that was it. I mix with bacteriostatic water. You'll have to order that online. All the brands are good. I've used hucog, many like corion. But they are all good.

Hi Colleen. I went to China last year and loved it and came home with some Chinese friends - They call me their Aussie Mum. I find them to be so kind and respectful - especially to their elders. My husband passed away a year and a half ago so I am trying to keep busy.

This trip will be thru very primitive areas in Tibet with very basic lodging and food - so no chance of pigging out on donuts or anything!! I hope I am not too much of a princess for this trip - I have never been camping or anything remotely like that and never wanted to either - so this will be interesting. Apparently there is a 4 or 5 day period where we probably can't get a shower and will be sleeping in dorm type accommodations. My biggest concern is my hair!! a girl has gotta look good right?? We are going to the Mount Everest base camp. I can't wait to see the Sherpas in action!

About the hhcg - I don't think I believe them when they say they haven't change the formula - in fact - I am just remembering - they said they have now added an appetite suppressant. Before they used to show you the label so you could see the strength. They don't do that now. You don't have any trouble getting RX Hcg in the states?

### caroline — 2012-03-18T12:03:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2329>

I think so many of us had trouble stabilizing after hcg. Dr. K talks about that. Doesn't he say the pathway isn't complete? It isn't our fault - he says we really need CT. I personally have to eat very few calories to loose. I never did well on Atkins either where so many people have - probably mostly men. At least Dr. K says hcg does work whereas so many think it is quackery. I never told anyone I was on it - they would have thought I lost my mind!

I really get being so uncomfortable in your body - that was always me. not even so much how I looked but I felt terrible.

I loved the leptin RX - for the first time in my life I wasn't hungry all the time and no cravings at all. Right from the very start with 50 to 60 grams of protein for breakfast I wouldn't be hungry until dinner. It was like a miracle. I didn't loose weight on the reset tho - I put on a couple of pounds and when I started it I was already up a couple of pounds so I really want to get down about 6 pounds. I feel so much better at that weight and not all puffy. good luck to all of us on this crazy journey. I wish Dr. Kruse would weight in on how his wife did on this protocol - obviously it is such a different journey for women

### JoanneA — 2012-03-19T01:25:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2508>

There can be reasons for not stabilizing, cheating off protocol, adding things to the protocol (MOP) its actually important to either pick one or the other to do HCG or LRX, not a good idea to mix them, although i will be adding CT to my hcg round only, it has been noted that someone who does not use their tbsp of milk, as well as at least one melba have issues srabilizing with nuts and dairy, i recomend Robin Woodall for the HCGers as she has written a second hypothisis on the hcg protocol, its very important not to mess with tge protocol of even upping the calories as 500/less work in conjunction with your hormone system, there can be problems with your thyroid later, yes HCG can fix thyroid problems but not if one has not been faithful to the protocol, if you want to do paleo with HCG your risking your hormones, as HCG is not a diet, people are fooled by the fact that they are still losing weigh, but truth be known are left wondering why they are not stabilizing or having now other issues, its a sad fact that people only have weightloss information about HCG, but nothing else, in the end i believe these ignorant people may be proponents to speak against hcg......... (weightloss Apocalypse, emotional eating rehab through the HCG Protocol)

### colleencoble — 2012-03-19T04:04:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2562>

The hcg protocol time is a perfect time to do what is called a food challenge so I don't believe it's harmful to eliminate the milk and breadsticks. Quite the contrary. Quite often the base of our weight problem is a food intolerance that is causing inflammation. When you take out the gluten and the milk for 6 weeks, you can clearly see if they are a problem for you or not. Time and time again I've seen people take those things out and their weight loss immediately is better. That's not because of the calories. It's because the source of inflammation is gone. That's why most of us have trouble losing weight--we keep eating the inflammatory foods.

thanks ChimpChick,

right now i don't think my body can handle another round. i did so much detoxing, my hormones got so out of whack, etc. that am just concentrating on nutrition and getting my biochemistry right. i was so perplexed at how bad i felt after going from 163 to 129 that i knew i had to do some digging. long story short: found out i have been a life-long pyroluric (blood disorder where zinc and B6 are severely depleted) and have hashimotos/hypothyroid, with the hypo probably since my early 20s.

i have been icing my thyroid, but haven't jumped in the tub yet... hope to this week... maybe another HCG round is in my future. i think the people who do best on it have their hormones and minerals in good balance. i hope to be there one day!

hi Claudia,

well neither endos i saw would do the the full thyroid panel or ANY of the hormone tests. the first endo did not even want to treat the hashi's! EPIC FAILURE. i am working with Alexander Haskell who wrote the book Hope for Hashimotos long distance. I got my labs thru him. i found out @ the pyro from reading Nora Gedgaudas' book.... and did my own testing. I am now working with a functional medicine center near me and just sent in a round of stool, urine and blood samples. Also jsut found out i have H. pylori, so that explains why the leaky gut was not getting much better with just paleo and leptin reset.

my LIW was 129, on the reset i got to 138 and stayed there for a while. then i got the 2 diagnoses early this year and i was an emotional mess. i cried for 2 days straight about the hashimotos. now i am 143... when i was really stressed i delved into some GF bread and some other carbs, and it definitely "stuck". I know I should really start CT, but honestly have been chicken. i will do it this week!

i did not put the ice directly on my thyroid, i wrapped the ziplock bag in a thin kitchen towel and tied it behind my neck... good thing i live alone!

if your doc won;t do a full thyroid and hormone panel, i suggest you sign up with Canary Club and do it thru them. Go to Stop the Thyroid Madness website and find out what a full thyroid panel is and what other tests they recommend, like ferritin, etc.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-19T22:43:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2940>

Some over on the hcg forum have been trying the leptin reset idea with their hcg. They've been eating one meal for breakfast then another for dinner instead of no breakfast then lunch and dinner. And they've been doing no fruit and replacing it with protein. Their losses don't tend to be better but I believe they will stabilize better. It's not been long enough to see for sure but it makes sense that the timing would help with the leptin signaling. I think that's why I've been stable. I never ate every 2-3 hours like many of them do. Many break up their food and eat every few hours and I think that's a mistake.

Advantages. Well, hcg gets off the weight FAST. I haven't seen CTing by itself drop a lot of weight quickly, though the weight tends to start dropping slowly if you need to lose. Average weight loss for a woman in 6 weeks with hcg is about 25-28 pounds. I'm a slow loser so I lose 20 pounds a round. If I knew then what I know now, I would have cut out the fruit and upped my losses to at least the average of 25 pounds. But that's a regular hcg round. I'm not sure how fast the losses would be with adding keto to the mix. Traditionally fats derail the pathway to the hypothalamus. Doc thinks the CT will fix that but I don't know whether the losses will be better, the same or worse. And what works for one person doesn't always work for someone else. We've had people on the forum add coconut oil and still lose but they tend to be the younger ones with better metabolisms. And then they often didn't stabilize. Hopefully CT will change that but I don't have enough data to say it will yet.

If your main concern isn't dropping weight fast then adding hcg to the mix isn't going to be helpful anyway. It's not going to aid in any transition. You can order it from overseas pharmacy places. The main one right now is ReliableRX.

### JoanneA — 2012-03-20T01:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2995>

Stabilizing is dependant on strict protocol, although Dr.S info is outdated Robin Woodall has more to tell about Leptin and the protocol, the fact that people add in things and still lose or lose more isnt a win situation, because hcg is simply not a diet (i could scream this from the roof top) hcg works in conjunction with the strict protocol as is 500 being max eating along with hunger (leptin) even under 500 cals for most. Following strict protocol is not relative at all, its the same for each patient unless theres allergies to some of the foods. You simply dont treat hcg as a diet and do what works for you unless your at the end of losing all your weight and your leptin is signaling hunger more often, which happens, if you want a good diet get off hcg and switch, if you want hcg then there are rules that apply. I did a 36 day round losing 28 pounds and 23 inches, i went into maintenance (the fats phase) and ate all kinds of fatty foods and stabilized well no gain! I did not cheat and followed through, im on my second round day 5 feel great and im down 7 lbs already, hcg is temporary treatment, it helps far beyond our shallow ideas of what appearance is about and should be combined with emo eating rehab as well as how to live later, correcting hormones with Hcg works, and loss of fat is a great benifit!!!!

### Martin — 2012-03-17T18:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-2045>

Since 24% of the population has a genetic susceptibility to react to biotoxins - Lyme, mold, certain algae - I suspect there are quite a number of folks here who can share what to do and how they're doing.

From one of the leaders in this field, Dr Ritchie Shoemaker:

"It all starts when a person is exposed to a biotoxin. In most people, the biotoxin is 'tagged' and identified by the body's immune system and is broken down and removed from the blood by the liver. However, some individuals do not have the immune response genes (HLA-DR genes) that are required to eventually form an antibody to a given foreign antigen. In these cases the biotoxins are not 'tagged' and remain in the body indefinitely, free to circulate and wreak havoc.

Once present in the body, the biotoxins begin to set off a complex cascade of biochemical events. The biotoxin binds to surface receptors (Toll receptors and many more) in nearly every kind of cell in the body. This recognition and binding of the biotoxin causes a continual upregulation of multiple inflammatory pathways, including production of cytokines, split product of complement, and TGF Beta-1. Biotoxins also directly affect nerve cell function, which is one of the reasons that the symptoms and visual contrast sensitivity (VCS) test are so useful in diagnosis.

[http://www.survivingmold.com/](http://http://www.survivingmold.com/)

Hi, I have done two and my mold doc was very interested in the results as they are a reliable indicator of your condition. I'll see if I can find you a doc in your area that does biotoxin treating.

On my last mold doc visit, I forgot my reading glasses - the same ones I use look at computer - and couldn't take his VCS test on his office computer. But, I was able to tell him that I'd done my online version from Shoemaker's site a week prior and he was pleased that I had done so. I showed improvement!!!

ACTH, HS CRP, and TGF-beta tests are really good at helping to pin this illness down. HS CRP is much more general an indicator, and I'm not sure if it's _directly_ related to mold/lyme, but it's an excellent indicator of overall health.

My TGF-beta as of three months ago was **10,820

C4a is another biggie.

And aMSH as well.

> The Marshall Protocol is a medical treatment being used by physicians worldwide to treat a variety of chronic inflammatory and autoimmune diseases including (but not limited to) sarcoidosis, Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, fibromyalgia, Crohn’s Disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. While other treatments for chronic disease use palliative medications in an effort to cover up symptoms, the Marshall Protocol is a curative treatment, which strives to address the root cause of the disease process.
>
>
>
> Information about the treatment can be found at the study site, marshallprotocol.com and also at autoimmunityresearch.org. The site is run by the staff of the Autoimmunity Research Foundation, a California-based non-profit agency. Over 200 health professionals are members of the site, and discussions are moderated by volunteer moderators. There is no charge to use the website or the treatment and all patients are welcome to participate.
>
>
>
> **The FDA has already granted orphan product designations for two of Autoimmunity Research Foundation’s six applications – Sarcoidosis, and Post Treatment Lyme Disease Syndrome (PTLDS, commonly known as chronic Lyme). ### Martin — 2012-08-10T20:53:09+0300

I listened to Dr Mercola's great interview with Dr Richie Shoemaker on the subject of Biotoxin Illness and heard this about sterol use:

**DM: Do you think that part of the reason that they have this adverse reaction to that (biotoxin) exposure is the impairment of the adrenal system?

RS: No. We see dysregulation of adrenals being under control of MSH. And as inflammation is corrected, this adrenal ACTH cortisol ratio will start to improve. We can see low ACTH-low cortisol, high ACTH-high cortisol, and in any kind of parameter in between, but itâ€™s subject â€“ like all the known cortin hormones â€“ to MSH regulation. If you think you have adrenal fatigue, if you think you have adrenal problem, check MSH before you use any kind of sterol. If you suppress ACTH, you actually can take away the last anti-inflammatory pathway these patients have.

I am thinking that if MSH is low, steroids should be avoided?

I have not found the mechanism behind why, **"If you suppress ACTH, you actually can take away the last anti-inflammatory pathway these patients have."

What am I missing? Shoemaker does not go into details in his book, "Mold Warriors" either. I am considering asking my doc for HC or Isocort and need to educate myself more.

I'm also considering mega-dosing omega 3 in the form of krill oil to help my low 37 (31-86 pg/ml) VEGF**. My 03/06 ratio is 4:1 so I'm not sure if this is not warranted, but it seems like a safe shot at getting some relief.

RS: "As we go forward in this kind of pyramid of treatment, we fix hormonal problems and autoimmune problems very early on. Then we want to increase oxygen-delivering capillary beds. Hereâ€™s where VEGF comes in. Vascular endothelial growth factor has abnormal distribution in these patients. Some itâ€™s quite high; we worry about cancer there. Most people, itâ€™s quite low. Itâ€™s actually protecting against cancer in some peopleâ€™s opinions. But low VEGF is not letting adequate oxygen be delivered in capillary beds.

We need to drive up VEGF to help people with brain fog, shortness of breath, and any cognitive issues as well. We can do that using either the omega-3s in very high doses, or a drug called Pioglitazone or Actos. We canâ€™t use quite as much now that when we used to because of the question about bladder problems and all, but Actos is a good one to drive up VEGF."**

### Martin — 2012-09-10T23:55:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-50807>

Hi elibby1878, thanks for posting here. I found this at [http://www.chronicneurotoxins.com/](http://http://www.chronicneurotoxins.com/)

"Upon completing the VCS test, viewers receive a message indicating that biotoxins are (positive) or are not (negative) likely to be involved in their illness. The criteria for getting a "positive" VCS result is set high to avoid false positive results. This occasionally results in a false negative result; some cases of chronic-biotoxin induced illness may pass the VCS test a some times. VCS can be measured during treatment to monitor recovery."

I am under the care of a mold doc so I have not bothered to learn to interpret the numbers in the score, just whether it was positive or negative. Like you, I'd be willing to guess with a higher overall score you are becoming more loaded up and it's showing in the numbers. Do you have a doc in mind? What part of the country are you in? I can possibly hook you up with a good doc.

Check out Mycometrics, Dr Shoemaker's choice for mold testing. Pricey, but it is the best. Your health is worth it!

Have you had gene haplotype tested? A first choice for anyone wondering if they have a mold/Lyme/biotoxin illness going on. Remember, 25% of the population is susceptible, so stop wondering and get the HLA DRB DRBQ test from Labcorp # 012542. I got mine for $260 at a local lab that does self-pay for a reduced rate. Busted my case wide open! I never looked back from there as I had a place to start moving on getting well.

### Martin — 2012-09-14T14:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-51621>

HLA DRB DRBQ test from Labcorp # 012542 is the one you want. There are many HLA tests and don't make the mistake that I made and order just the HLA DR test! It is NOT the one you need! I wasted $260 of self-payin' cash on that error!

You can get decent info about other aspects of your health picture by those other tests your doc ordered. Search around this site and pick up info on how to interpret the results. Many may be normal but you still feel lousy. Common with biotoxin illness folks. What is goofing up with us is shown by very specific tests that Dr Shoemaker has researched.

The HLA DRB DRBQ test also shows if you are genetically susceptible to Lyme. I'm not sure what Lyme test your doc ordered, but often they are inconclusive, and worse - misleading.

Keep on it, and be your own advocate. Keep us posted.

### Martin — 2013-05-17T15:27:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-86305>

My mold doc, Dr Dave Ou, posted this on his blog:

**The genetics of Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome from biotoxins**

In the vast majority of my patients, I check a genetic test called an HLA DR/DQ from Labcorp (test #012542) I learned about this test from Dr. Ritchie Shoemaker. This gene is located on chromosome number 6 and it encodes a protein called an MHC Class II protein. Each person has two copies: one from the father and one from the mother. This test tells me if a person is susceptible to biotoxin illnesses and I’ve found that 98% of my patients are.

To give some background on this gene, when the immune system first encounters a foreign object such as a bacteria, the first immune cells to arrive on the scene are called APCs (Antigen Presenting Cells). The APC “eats” the foreign object then attaches it to a MHC Class II protein. It’s like taking a piece of the bacteria and and putting it in a picture frame. The APC then moves the foreign object and the MHC protein (the picture frame) to the surface of the cell so that the rest of the immune system (specifically T- cells) can study and learn how to defend against the foreign object. The APC essentially tells the rest of the immune system, “Look what I found! I put it in a picture frame!”

As an analogy, imagine that a crime was committed. The first police officer on the scene was able to take a picture of the criminal before he ran away. The policeman then shows a picture of the suspect to the rest of the police force. The MHC II protein does the same thing as the first police officer: to show a picture of the suspect to the rest of the immune system.

What if the police officer’s camera didn’t work well or he had trouble developing the picture? He wouldn’t be able to show the picture to the rest of the police department so they wouldn’t know who to look for. The criminal is able to run around the city to commit more crimes. This is what happens in Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome. The MHC II protein is unable to show a picture of the foreign invader to the rest of the immune system.

Ritchie Shoemaker, MD, has identified 10 types of MHC II proteins that are unable to show small biotoxins to the rest of the immune system. Biotoxins are poisons released by a variety of organisms. Examples include snake venom, bee venom, and jellyfish stingers. Bacteria and mold also make biotoxins. Some of the smallest biotoxins are those made from indoor mold and Lyme Disease. If one of these 10 types of MHC II proteins run into the small biotoxins from indoor mold or Lyme, they can’t “see” the biotoxins so the biotoxins are able to run around the body causing damage. There are about 50 other types of MHC II proteins that are able to recognize the small biotoxins from mold or Lyme so people with these don’t get biotoxin illneses very easily.

A list of the MHC II proteins / HLA DR/DQ that can’t recognize mold or Lyme biotoxins can be found at [https://www.survivingmold.com/diagnosis/lab-tests](https://www.survivingmold.com/diagnosis/lab-tests). A more complete list can be found in his book Surviving Mold

4-3-53: susceptible to both biotoxins from mold and Lyme

11/12-3-52B: susceptible to both biotoxins from mold and Lyme

14-5-52B: susceptible to both biotoxins from mold and Lyme

7-2-53: susceptible to biotoxins from mold

13-6-52A, B, or C: susceptible to biotoxins from mold

17-2-52A: susceptible to biotoxins from mold

18-4-52A: susceptible to biotoxins from mold

15-6-51: susceptible to biotoxins from Lyme

16-5-51: susceptible to biotoxins from Lyme

1-5: susceptible to low MSH which makes one susceptible to biotoxins from mold and Lyme

About 25% of the world’s population have one of the 10 susceptible types of HLA DR/DQ gene. As of Nov 27, 2012 in my practice, I have tested 227 patients. Out of those 227 patients, 222 have one of the 10 types. That’s an astounding 98% of all of my patients who are vulnerable to the biotoxins from indoor mold and Lyme! Another way of putting it is nearly all of my patients come from only 25% of the population. I have spoken to my colleagues across the country and they’ve had very similar findings to mine. I sometimes joke that the HLA DR/DQ gene predicts if someone will get frustrated with conventional medicine and seek out alternative care.

I’ve written about some of the symptoms and diseases that are associated with these genes in my previous post. Unfortunately, there are only several hundred doctors in the world who are aware of this gene. Therefore, the vast majority of doctors know nothing about this gene so that countless patients are not diagnosed properly. I suspect there has been an increase in the number of doctors who are aware of this gene as a result of a talk given at this year’s ILADS conference and hopefully this important knowledge will continue to grow.

With effective treatment, the urine mycotoxins will go from normal to abnormal as the mycotoxins start to exit the body.

### Martin — 2013-12-07T18:00:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-102726>

Here's an interesting article I ran across while searching for the repair of hypothalamus receptors:

[http://invigorating-acupuncture.blogspot.com/2012/04/review-of-works-of-dr-ritchie-shoemaker.html](http://invigorating-acupuncture.blogspot.com/2012/04/review-of-works-of-dr-ritchie-shoemaker.html)

Here are some excerpts with my thoughts and notes **in bold**:

"If cytokines enter the capillaries, they will invite White Blood Cells into the area. As the capillaries are small blood vessels, they can only support so much traffic and the area will soon be clogged **(and here come those horrible leg cramps when you stretch in the middle of the night).** Cytokines regulate Hypoxia Inducible Factors (HIF) \[20\] which will result in constricted blood flow and a lack of oxygen to the cells, otherwise known as capillary hypoperfusion.\* HIF stimulates **TGFβ-1 (the test for this is what my mold doc uses to monitor progress while he treats biotin illness because over 90% of his patients test high)**, which furthers the inflammation cycle and alters the cell structure, and, Vascular Endothelial Growth Factor (VEGF)."

"By signaling the HIF, cytokines are preventing oxygen from getting to the cells. If the cells do not have enough oxygen then only 5% of their available energy, in the form of glycogen, can be used.\* The rest of the glycogen gets burned up in the form of lactic acid, hence the muscle cramps\[23\]."

"The body can overcome these limitations by burning fat for energy, however once the fat source runs out, and the people with the 6 haplotypes do have some genetic predispositions to be limber (many of these individuals according to Dr. Shoemaker have hyper mobile joints\[24\]) and athletic, then the body will find another fuel source to burn. Since these individuals may not have a lot of stored fat to begin with, or simply run out of fat to burn, the body will start to burn the protein of the lean muscle mass. Leptin is released after the fat stores are depleted **(I believe this should read, "_while_ the fat storesâ€¦)** and the body is signaled to start holding on to fatty acids. Once the body starts to cannibalize itself and eat the lean muscle, it figures, **for the time being**, it will pay the price because the problem will resolve and then the normal stores of energy will be replenished.

This restoration never occurs in a CIRS individual, because the original problem of the foreign antigens was not resolved which means all the other processes are still in play. It takes a minimum of two days of rest for the body to rebuild its glycogen stores\[25\] and most people with CFS, as soon as they get energy, burn it and do not pace themselves **(_hello?!_ Martin, are you effing _listening?!_).** At the same time the body is gaining more fatty acids which become fat weight, and the Leptin release signal never shuts off\[26\]."

"Moving on, this release of Leptin will act on the hypothalamus\[27\]. According to Shoemaker, the increase of cytokines will eventually damage the Leptin receptors on the hypothalamus, which will inhibit the binding ability of Leptin. Dr. Shoemaker amended this statement in IAQ radio episode 136 to state that the receptors for Leptin are not the only receptors which are damaged by this process.\* Since this binding does not take place, the hypothalamus does not produce enough MSH to regulate the inflammatory agents present in the body. Even if Shoemaker is incorrect on this statement in regards to the cytokine effect on Leptin receptors, which I suspect he is not, the demand from Leptin for more MSH would eventually lead to reduced MSH if the antigens were not removed, simply because the MSH would be tapped out." **(time to CT!)**

"From my own clinical experience and what I have seen and heard working with Dr. Rea, I believe that Dr. Shoemaker has potentially come across an important piece of the puzzle. While I am not sold on all that he says about Leptin, particularly trying to link it to obesity in CFS or EI/CS patients (as most of the ones I have seen have been, if anything, underweight) **(Author is not aware of Leptin Resistance in thin people - think Body Composition!)** I do believe that he makes a good case for himself. The lack of oxygen to the cells would cause the symptoms described and supports Dr. Reaâ€™s use of oxygen therapy, and potentially supports the use of Tai Chi, and Qigong breathing exercises among other disciplines. Leptin, while it may not result in weight gain in the patients described, may be the key to a number of other symptoms and the reason why some of Dr. Reaâ€™s overweight patients, and others, have a hard time loosing weight. Obesity is such a multifaceted issue that assuming there is a singular answer is, at this point, suspect at best **(Now we're talking)**. It is also possible that his more recent discovery of more receptors being damaged could be the cause of some of the symptoms."

**So, can the receptors be repaired? Does the Leptin Reset accomplish this damage that are due to cytokines?**

From the good Doc,:
"@Dan **At this point my speculation is that we can retrain the hypothalamus**. I feel I have done this to myself and many of my current patients but no one else is doing this. I have contacted Amgen about this angle and their synthetic leptin trial data but my dealings with them lead me to believe they have other plans for their synthetic leptin. I have spoken to several people in SD who were in their trial and they all had their synthetic leptin discontinued and they opted for belt lipectomies and liposuction to reduce their adipocyte numbers to control their weight. Most plastic surgeons wont do the surgery for this indication because they dont believe it is a weight loss strategy, but most plastic surgeons also think the number of fat cells we have is fixed at birth which is also old school CW. This is a research topic that probably will never get studied until synthetic leptin is FDA approved which I dont see happening anytime soon."

"When someone is not responsive to the receptor signaling of leptin in the hypothalamus, they become unable to burn their excess energy off as pure heat so they remain overweight. The reason is simple. They do not have any excess energy to burn as you will find out. They find it very difficult to lose the weight, even when they restrict calories to starvation levels. This will change when they become leptin sensitive once again. Some obese people appear to do tremendous damage to their leptin receptors and need synthetic leptin to continue to lose or maintain their weight loss. We realized this in the results of Amgen leptin trials data. Moreover, some of these people have found that removing adipocytes surgically also helps them maintain their weight better as well. Long term studies need to be done on these people to see if surgical removal of fat is really necessary for long term weight control, or can long term neuroplastic training be done while the hypothalamus undergoes neuroplastic repair over time. In my opinion, I think if we use timing of meals and couple it to the day light cycles as they adjust daily, we can facilitate that neuroplasticity. This is really the science behind the Leptin Rx. I have read extensively about the work of neurosurgeon and neuroscientists Penfield, Paul Bach y Rita, and Merzenich and their experiments have proved that the brain is very plastic, and can be retaught how to work optimally again if we give it a â€œnew wayâ€ to perceive a stimulus once damage to another part of the brain has occurred.

"We can achieve leptin sensitivity after the hypothalamus is damaged, if we teach the brain how to use our older evolutionary non-leptin neural circuits. Yoking eating to light and day, and making timing to sleep and wakefulness and to the visceral sensations of distention of the gut via the vagus nerve are precisely how the Leptin Rx is designed to work. Taking full advantage of how certain macro-nutirents are tied to light cycle further helps reset hypothalamus in two to three months. The more inflammation that is present, the longer the reset may take. Instead of relying on optimal functioning of the hypothalamic leptin receptor, we can re-teach the hypothalamus to pay attention to the light levels, awakening time, time we sleep and face dark, when our gut is distended and filled with food and when it is not. We can also load the diet with protein and fat at certain times, to take full advantage of the existing working neural circuits in the brainstem and hypothalamus that accounts for carbohydrates, to our advantage in resetting how the brain can more fully perceive our energy status when the leptin receptor is not functioning."

**Hell, yeah.**

Big

I got sulfate test strips from Quantofix in USA, but they are manufactured in Germany by Macherey-Nagel. Urinate on a strip, wait two minutes, compare with chart on container. Consistency helps establish accuracy - do it first thing in morning. Sulfur foods include eggs, broccoli, cabbage, pork, various amounts. After a few months of avoiding sulfurous foods (all while continuing to follow light, emf avoidance, and mindfulness,etc.,) the level went down to around 400 from a consistent high of 1,600. So I went back to eating sulphur foods and now I occasionally test and the levels have remained around 400. Yay! I loves me some pork and broccoli.

### prime1 — 2016-10-26T01:06:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-203316>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=2045)
>
> Since 24% of the population has a genetic susceptibility to react to biotoxins - Lyme, mold, certain algae - I suspect there are quite a number of folks here who can share what to do and how they're doing.
>
>
>
> From one of the leaders in this field, Dr Ritchie Shoemaker:
>
>
>
> "It all starts when a person is exposed to a biotoxin. In most people, the biotoxin is 'tagged' and identified by the body's immune system and is broken down and removed from the blood by the liver. However, some individuals do not have the immune response genes (HLA-DR genes) that are required to eventually form an antibody to a given foreign antigen. In these cases the biotoxins are not 'tagged' and remain in the body indefinitely, free to circulate and wreak havoc.
>
>
>
> Once present in the body, the biotoxins begin to set off a complex cascade of biochemical events. The biotoxin binds to surface receptors (Toll receptors and many more) in nearly every kind of cell in the body. This recognition and binding of the biotoxin causes a continual upregulation of multiple inflammatory pathways, including production of cytokines, split product of complement, and TGF Beta-1. Biotoxins also directly affect nerve cell function, which is one of the reasons that the symptoms and visual contrast sensitivity (VCS) test are so useful in diagnosis.
>
>
>
> [http://www.survivingmold.com/](http://http://www.survivingmold.com/)

Sunlight may have an ability to kill mold - however mold isn't the problem. What it produces is the problem - called Mycotoxins. Mycotoxins are resistant to UV light. Can't radicate the mycotoxins with sunlight. I"m going to dig deeper into this

.

# Thread: Hello, from high desert SW New Mexico USA, HCG, Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-03-17T22:00:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-2113>

Hi, I've been doing a variation of low carb off and on over the last several years. have done two rounds of HCG over the last year. discovered this website in Jan 2012. It was just in time to end my last HCG session. I went on the ketogenic paleo per Dr. K and am pleased to report that I've kept the weight off and even lost a little more.

Here is how I ended my latest HCG session. About a week before the end I increased my protein by 4 oz daily. Then because I have had some digestive difficulties I slowly increased the fat portion 10 gms or so every several days depending on how digestion was going. I'm currently up to over 100 gms per day. I kept the carb portions the same and eventually decreased it a little. My weight has stayed stable and I even lost 3 more lbs.

I have had problems in the past with eating too little, slowing the metabolism and moderate weight gain. So I have been keeping track of my macronutrients and have set a minimum calories that I meet. Not everyone, of course, has to do this. this is just my own physical issues I must deal with.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-03-21T00:07:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-3565>

Wanted to continue my post. The HCG diet is a very difficult diet to do and complete. The ads would have you believe that you only have to take the drops and 'the lbs melt off.' Sixty years ago only rich people did this diet. They traveled to Italy, got injected, ate the meals served to them, did site seeing around town or napped in their rooms. Someone prepared and served their meals, cleaned their rooms and at home the servants took care of the home and family. Contrast that with today, cooking maybe two different meals, cleaning up after ones self along with the drastic drop in energy as the lbs 'melt off.' I can't even imagine trying to work at a job doing this. Mind you the diet wants you to do this for as long as 6 weeks. So if you are going to do it be prepared and make sure you do not gain the weight back after. Go right to the paleo ketogenic per Dr.K. I don't think the homeopathic drops give the same results. I used them first and the real thing the second time. That time i saw the weight come off the targeted areas. It was very obvious.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-04-03T06:35:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-10685>

Here are some thoughts on altitude adjustment. Dr K made a comment on the sherpas and their CT. I realized they also had to adjust to the altitude. Then I realized I was about 20 minute drive from a 10,000 foot mountain easily accessible by tram. I live at 5200 feet here in NM. I took a first trip up paying the full price of $17, yikes. Then I noticed a seasons pass which brought the price down to $2 a trip if I went up twice a week. Much better. I've lived in NM for 15 years and never really adjusted to the altitude. I's still hard to do even a fast walk for any length of time. So I've never been able to enjoy the physical fitness aspects of this area and in fact have developed a negative attitude where NM is involved. That first trip was exhilarating. The trees, the air, watching people skiing and snowboarding was wonderful. Lots of snow there and I was able to do a CT in a snowbank! I was weak, dizzy and got a headache but it was glorious up there. Even though I was eating what I thought was enough food twice a day I got hungry and had a burger, chips and salsa down below.

The second trip up I got so hungry I could have eaten the bark off the trees so I ate chips and cheese dip up on top. I sat and read my book for a half hour after that. I got up went out side the restaurant walked about 20 steps and almost passed out. Luckily I knew to lower my head and didn't embarrass myself.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-04-03T06:50:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-10688>

My third trip up the mountain I decided to take my pulse. I strolled slowly about, enjoyed the scenery and after half an hour my pulse was 125. A half hour after that still walking about I took my pulse again and it was 100. After one hour I rested up there,took my pulse again and it was 78. interesting workout just by strolling about.

I read about the benefits of altitude. One is it increases your metabolism, it increases red blood cells, it improves the oxygenation of ones tissues. At spas in Europe and on expensive cruises, $10,000 per, they have these little machines that mimic the oxygen level at 10,000 feet. You can get a 60 to 90 minute treatment. So that is why I have my sessions at this same time span. The major difference is that when one is actually up on the 10,000 foot level the oxygen pressure is felt on the whole body. The skin does some breathing as well. The machine treatments don't have that. They even recommend this treatment for elite athletes.

It's amusing to me to see the bike riders racing about like mad around the tram parking lot and back out on the road. They don't know they could include a 90 minute treatment at 10,000 feet. Now I know some of them do go up the back road all the way to the top, but they don't stay long enough to take advantage of the treatment.

Thanks for the interest. I'm being sidetracked with a contract job for a few weeks. They shut down the tram for 2 weeks for maintenance and I've been traveling heavily since then so I have only been up the mtn once since april 20 12. I noticed I kept my conditioning and used my breath technique to keep the o2 levels up. I got an oximeter. You can by them at Walgreens of all places. With all the traveling I haven't been able to do the CT or mtn visits. So I have been thinking about what is optimal for me. The idea of 6 pack abs leaves me cold (pun intended). Since I turned 70 my interests have changed. I'm still contemplating and will complete my thoughts another time. it's true though there is a spa in my back yard and I intend to make use of it.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-04T12:22:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-21281>

TerryF welcome and you'll ne happy to know those expensive spa machines are no longer that expensive. I am doing my next biohack using these HBOT machines......and the results so far have been very very good.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-09T04:33:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-22457>

Aah, but Jack you don't have the 110 mile view, the air, the mountain wind rustling through the pines with a machine! lol On a serious note I think there is something to be said for the overall effect of the difference in pressure on the entire body. But I agree the results seem to be very good. I almost immediately noticed an increase in my energy level when I came down to 5200 feet from the 10.378. I've been up a total of 9 times since march 14,2012. All I've done is stroll around, sit and breath, talk with the tourists, and find a secluded spot to practice my singing. yet when I'm back at my home level I'm doing several activities per day and any rest periods I do take actually feel restful.

Visit 9 was May 5 Sat am. After a BAB heavy on the fat I went up via the tram. This time I wore my hiking boots and took my hiking stick. I spent my first hour as usual moving. Thirty minutes sitting and chatting. This time I tried taking both north and south trails at the top part way to find out if they were passable yet. My goal at the end of the summer is to hike the trail from 6500 feet to the top. I hear it is a 4 hour hike. Right now I am very happy that I don't get nauseated and overly dizzy anymore. In addition, right now I want to stay at 10,378 feet as much as possible. When I felt like I might be getting dizzy I used my breath technique two or three times. I felt no hunger and drank my 3 cups of water. It is very dry up there. somewhere around 16% humidity I would guess. At least that is what my gauge said outside my apt. Til next time.

### nonchalant — 2012-05-09T05:52:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-22481>

A couple years ago we flew from almost sea level in Texas to Denver, and then drove up Pike's Peak that same day. LOL! I walked a little bit at the summit, then found I had to stay very still to keep from passing out! I recovered, but had to move slowly! That was before low carb/paleo/leptin reset/CT. I wonder how I would react now, since my asthma is gone, and my pulse rate is lower.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-09T06:38:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-22491>

Hi Nonchalant, i think there is a certain amount of adaptation that must be undergone by everyone. Some don't take long to adapt and some take quite a while. my son was almost hospitalized when he was a teen and visited relatives at altitude. I think 8,000 feet. he was trying to bike ride I think. A friend of mine in her 60s at the time was happily climbing around Durango Co within a day or two at 7500 feet. I, who was 10 years her junior and had been living there for a year already was still struggling. There really isn't any research that I can find that measures anything for those of us who are untrained and older. I did find the Army manual for altitude and CT. Can we develop more RBCs? Can our blood O2 levels adjust properly and stay there so we don't pass out. What is a good blood O2 level for us anyway. I don't know how old you are but I'm talking about over 60 years. I think the low carb paleo, leptin sens. is good especially if you have made sure you are getting the proper amount of protein and fat to keep your health up. They say to come to the altitude and give yourself a few days to adjust before doing anything strenuous even if in good health. I have to make sure I get enough macro-nutrients because it's very easy for me to go into starvation mode and that sure doesn't help my health and stamina any. I think a good protein and fat meal and a stiff dose of bullet proof coffee would be a good idea if you do that again!

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-12T07:05:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-23350>

Yow, Pikes Peak is over 14,000 feet. No wonder it was hard. so far studies say 10,000 is good enough.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-24T08:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-26986>

last trip up the mtn was 5-5-12, 17 days later 5-23-12 today I made another (10th) trip up. Job interfered. About 3/4 of the way up I began to be concerned that I would be cold because I hadn't taken a jacket. the wind was strong, temp was down 30 points and i began to feel cold. Shortly after this I could feel the spread of warmth throughout my body. When I got off the tram at the top I was quite comfortable. I was delighted. I seem to remember recently in this blog someone mention that ones body became warm in anticipation of the treatment. The HR still goes up to 118 on exertion, but the dizziness is controlled with the breath and is less ever all. O2 goes from 87 to 96 and is affected by the breath technique.

i took off my shoes and socks and got some free Vit D from the knees down. I took off my sunglasses for about 20 minutes. My D level was 99 last I checked so I am pretty well protected I feel.

i've been able to think about my buy-in to this including and especially the CT. I'm not particularly interested in a 6 pack, nor in climbing MT Everest on my 74th BD (in 4 years) so I can be the oldest lady to do so. My children all have their own set ways and probably think I'm extreme. I'm done with playing Charley Brown to Lucy's grabbing the football from him. If people ask me how I do it. I have been answering them. I've had people start babbling senselessly. My sweet Catholic nun neighbor even jumped down my throat shouting, "What are you trying to do, kill me?" to my innocent dietary suggestion that might help the pain she always complains about. Well, at least she doesn't c/o pain to me any more but it hurts me to see her hobble so. I've done years of health education and dietary counseling and am done with being attacked for it.

Here is what I've come up with so far. I want to be able to get up from the floor without using my hands to help me. I want to wash my car windows and get into my car seat, putting my purse on the seat beside me without gasping in pain from my shoulders. I want to have energy to do at least 6 hours of various kinds of movement during the day and then go out for a couple of hours in the evening. When I retired 6 years ago people would tell me that I would get bored. I said no i wouldn't because i had a long list of things I still wanted to do. Well, that list still waits for me as I've floundered around with health issues. A lot of it has to do with art, poetry, music etc that i started out with as a young person. An optimal life for me is to have that vibrancy and joi de vivre that I had as a young person. All the better to write a song lyric or a story. It's not the hard muscles I'm after. I'm after the strong supple sound of my singing voice and not the wavering quaver of a decrepit elder. Can I have the stamina to jam with a group on my keyboard once again or play an energetic song on my flute? So far so good. I can do the face dunks now.

Miss Terry you are an inspiration. At first I thought you were a man and then realized you were not. That inspires me more. You have given me a lot to think about today. You have just made my day. Thanks for sharing.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-26T00:25:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-27475>
